June 12 Full-Day Summary

All sleeves posted positive returns on June 12 in a mixed-tape session (S&P flat, Nasdaq −0.30%, semi index +5%+). The prediction ranked mom_roa and mom_v2 above mom_v1 — the actual outcome was the reverse. mom_v1 led (+1.47%) because its 100-name breadth captured more of the broad small/mid-cap semi rally (UCTT, AIP, ICHR, MKSI, COHR) that didn't appear in the concentrated 50-name quality screens. residual_roa followed at +1.17%, beating the prediction thanks to energy stabilization (the expected headwind didn't materialize). SpaceX's $160.95 debut (+19.2%) did not drain capital from semis and did not hurt VSAT — both predictions wrong.

Since inception, the leaderboard is residual_roa +10.34% vs SPY +2.93%, a +7.41pp alpha lead over 42 days. The next major event is the end-of-June rebalance (~June 27–28) followed by MU earnings (June 24) — which will be the largest-dollar catalyst in the paper portfolio's history.

The most urgent operational gap: the llm_overlay_sector_top4 overlay has been live for 7 days and made zero decisions despite the exact macro conditions it was designed to evaluate. Run calibration prompts before the weekend.

covers the weekend Iran peace deal announcement, first trading day reaction, deep-dives

The Big Picture: Iran Peace Deal Changes the Portfolio Regime

The defining event of the June 13–15 weekend was the announcement of a US-Iran peace
agreement, mediated by Pakistan and Qatar. President Trump announced the deal late
Sunday on social media ("deal with Iran is now complete"), and formal signing is scheduled
for Geneva on Friday, June 19, 2026. Key terms:

  • Strait of Hormuz reopens immediately to all shipping; US naval blockade of Iranian
    ports lifted.
  • Iranian oil sanctions lifted (European nations separately agreed to lift sanctions
    in exchange for steps on the nuclear program).
  • All military operations terminated on all fronts, including Lebanon.

Market reaction on June 15:

Index Move
S&P 500 +1.49%
Nasdaq +2.38%
Dow Jones +1.20%
Philadelphia Semiconductor Index +4%+
WTI Crude Oil −5.5% → ~$80/barrel
Brent Crude −5% → ~$83/barrel

This is the largest macro regime shift since the Iran war began (February 28, 2026).
The portfolio's response is bifurcated: energy names (residual_roa's ~15% weight)
face direct headwinds; semi/tech/consumer names benefit from risk-on and lower oil.

Dominant Theme 1: Iran Peace Deal — Portfolio Impact Across All Sleeves

The Energy Cluster in residual_roa

residual_roa holds approximately 7 energy-related names (~14–16% portfolio weight):

Ticker Type Jun 12 MV Iran Deal Impact
APA Upstream E&P ~$2,064 −3.5%+ (upstream = direct oil price hit)
DINO (HF Sinclair) Downstream refiner ~$2,050 −2 to −3% (refinery margins complex)
PARR (Par Pacific) Downstream refiner ~$1,692 Complex (see below)
NRT Royalties ~$2,029 −3%+
INSW (Int'l Seaways) Tanker operator ~$1,936 Neutral/mixed (Hormuz reopening reduces tanker premium)
PBR (Petrobras) Integrated Brazil ~$2,156 −2% (less exposed to Hormuz)
TEN (Tenneco/auto) Industrial ~$2,162 Mildly negative

PARR complexity (from June 12 insight, confirmed relevant today): PARR as a downstream
refiner benefits from lower crude input costs. While oil is falling 5.5%, refined product
prices (jet fuel, gasoline, diesel) typically fall more slowly. Crack spreads — the margin
between crude input and refined output — may temporarily WIDEN as Iran supply floods
the crude market. PARR is not a pure Iran headwind. The 2015 Iran deal historical analog
showed downstream refiners initially hit (−2–4%) then outperforming within 60 days as
crack spreads normalized at lower crude. Track PARR separately from APA in the next 4
weeks.

Net energy impact: ~15% weight × average −3% = approximately −0.45% on residual_roa.
This is partially offset by SEZL's expected +14.4% × ~5% weight = +0.72% tailwind.
Net semi equipment tailwinds (LRCX +7.7%, AMAT +5%+, KLAC flat, TER +~5%) add another
estimated +0.9–1.2%. Residual_roa is likely up +1.5–2.5% net on June 15 despite
the energy headwind.

Sector Portfolio — LLM Macro Overlay VINDICATED Day 1

The llm_overlay_sector_top4 sleeve was seeded on June 12 with three slots:
- XLK (Technology) — held ✅
- XLI (Industrials) — held ✅
- XLB (Materials) — held ✅
- XLE (Energy) → VETOED → cash ✅

On June 15, energy stocks fell 3–4% while XLK/XLI/XLB all rose on the Iran rally. This
is the first live calibration data for the macro overlay: the veto saved ~$25k of
notional exposure to a −3.5% move = approximately $875 in saved losses relative to the
control sleeve which holds XLE in full.

The macro overlay's first decision was correct — but this is n=1. The larger question
is whether the macro veto would have prevented the July rebalance from adding XLE if the
Iran peace deal had fallen apart. The experiment is generating real data: XLE veto cost
the treatment nothing and saved meaningful downside.

Dominant Theme 2: MU Approaching Earnings (June 24) — $1 Trillion Club

Confirmed: Micron Technology market cap hit $1 trillion on June 15, doing so faster
(48 days) than any company in history. Stock traded between $1,052 and $1,063.52 on June 15.

June 24 earnings preview (management guided $33.5B record revenue):
- Analyst consensus: BUY (46 of 46 tracked analysts)
- Wolfe Research target: $1,250 (raised from $550)
- Goldman Sachs target: $900 (already breached 15%+ ago)
- Daiwa: $1,600
- This is the single largest earnings event in the paper portfolio's history

Bull case (still intact):
- HBM4 capacity sold out for all of 2026 — no channel inventory risk
- US megafab in Manassas, Virginia now in production (1α DRAM)
- AI inference memory demand accelerating: LLM context windows growing, requiring more
HBM per inference unit
- DRAM pricing jumped sharply H1 2026 — will flow through to Q3 revenue with strong
margin expansion

Bear case (risk to monitor):
- If guidance for FY2027 is only "in-line" vs. "upside surprise," the stock could sell off
despite a record Q3 beat (classic "sell the news" at $1,050)
- Google TurboQuant thesis (AI memory optimization reducing DRAM density requirements)
remains unvalidated but was flagged by shorts in June 10 report
- Macro shock: any Fed rate surprise (CPI was 4.2% in May) could reprice growth names

Action before June 24: All three systematic sleeves (mom_roa, mom_v1, mom_v2) hold MU.
Do not exit pre-earnings. The HBM4 structural thesis has not changed. The market cap
milestone is a psychological catalyst, not a sell signal. Monitor on June 24 evening for
the initial guidance reaction.

Dominant Theme 3: SEZL Acceleration — Super App Pivot Pays Off

June 14 announcement: Sezzle launched a comprehensive platform expansion:
- Expanded Rewards Hub (cashback, points, card-linked offers)
- Pay-in-5 (5-equal-installment variant of BNPL)
- Long-term lending up to 48 months (WebBank partnership = move into prime installment loans)
- Sezzle Mobile (phone service tier — the first non-financial product)
- Adyen processing in Canada (full payment rails expansion beyond checkout BNPL)
- AI-powered support chatbot (resolves most app inquiries without human intervention)

Stock reaction: +14.4% on June 14 alone. The market is recognizing that Sezzle has
successfully crossed the moat from "BNPL checkout widget" to "financial super-app for
credit-constrained GenZ."

Why this is structurally significant:
1. Loyalty economics: Users who engage with the Earn rewards hub generate +20% more
revenue per active user (previously disclosed). Every new feature drives engagement
→ revenue multiplier.
2. Canada = first international market: The Adyen/Marqeta virtual card in Canada adds
a new geography with similar BNPL regulatory environment and consumer profile.
3. Long-term lending to 48 months: This is a credit product at much higher APR than
BNPL. It monetizes the same consumer relationship at 3–5× the revenue per dollar lent.
4. Mobile phone service: A Sezzle MVNO is aggressive but follows the Square/CashApp
playbook — once you control the consumer's daily financial app, you have distribution
for any product.

Updated SEZL position thesis: The residual signal captured SEZL at $85.50 when it
was moving independently of its PYPL/SOFI peers. At ~$152 (post June 14 pop), the stock
is +78% from entry. The next question is: does SEZL remain idiosyncratically strong into
the July rebalance, or does this product-launch pop represent the peak of the momentum?
B. Riley's "tougher comparisons" flag from June 12 is still relevant — Q2 revenue growth
will be compared against Q2 2025, which was already strong (+66% YoY for full 2025).

2026 guidance maintained: 25–30% revenue growth, 28% net income growth, 31% EPS growth.

AAOI Experiment — First Live Week (Control vs. Treatment)

The LLM overlay re-inceptioned on June 12 with AAOI (Applied Optoelectronics) as the
new #1 mom_roa signal (replacing FN after the history-gap re-inception).

State as of June 15:

Control (mom_roa_top1) Treatment (llm_overlay)
Position 591.5 AAOI shares (est.) at $169.05 Cash (AAOI vetoed)
Jun 15 price $170.42 —
Unrealized P&L +$0.81% (+~$810) $0 (cash)
June 15 NAV est. ~$85,537 $80,766

The treatment's veto decision:

The state doc confirms AAOI was vetoed by the LLM treatment. The research-level data
points that justified the veto:

  1. Insider selling: Senior VP (North America GM) sold 33,630 shares (~$5.83M);
    another SVP (Asia) sold 39,154 shares (~$6.78M). Two senior insiders liquidating
    simultaneously is a notable dilution signal.
  2. Leveraged 2× ETF being created: Themes ETFs launching a 2X daily AAOI ETF
    creates volatility amplification and systematic-strategy-driven swings that diverge
    from fundamental value.
  3. Rosenblatt PT $220 (current: $170) implies 29% upside — genuine analyst conviction
    backed by Amazon 800G revenue and Oracle qualification pipeline.

First-week verdict: AAOI is up only 0.81% since the control entry ($169.05 → $170.42).
The veto is very mildly vindicated in the sense that AAOI hasn't run, but has not been
the catastrophic miss that the prior FN situation became. The treatment is behind by $810
in absolute terms — much less than the $4,085 gap that accumulated when FN rallied post-
stop. This is a better-calibrated risk/reward than the FN episode.

Key risk to monitor: AAOI was at $183.95 on June 8, fell to $169.05 on June 12. The
stock is working through a distribution phase (insider selling) while the Rosenblatt
thesis (Amazon 800G) is unconfirmed by an earnings event. The next catalysts for AAOI:
- Q2 FY2026 earnings (~August): Revenue acceleration from 800G qualification
- Oracle qualification announcement: If confirmed, stock re-rates toward $220 PT
- Insider selling pace: If selling continues, signals management believes the stock
is fully valued here

SpaceX (SPCX) — MSCI Inclusion Creates Mechanical Buying

SPCX closed at $160.95 on June 12 (IPO first day, +19%). By June 15, the stock had
already surged to $177.99 (+10.6%) — not from fundamental developments but from
MSCI index inclusion.

The MSCI mechanism:

MSCI's early-inclusion methodology for large-cap IPOs (above a certain float-adjusted
market cap threshold) triggered SPCX inclusion beginning June 13, just one trading
day after the IPO. MSCI-tracking AUM totals approximately $15–20 trillion globally.
With SpaceX at ~$2.1T market cap and a 4% free float (~$84B), even a 0.1% weight in
MSCI mandates drives ~$15–20B in mechanical buying against a relatively illiquid float.
This is price-insensitive, systematic buying — funds must purchase to match the
benchmark regardless of price.

Implications for our universe:
- This MSCI inclusion dynamic is a predictable, mechanical alpha signal for large
IPOs. The pattern: IPO → MSCI eligibility check (~1 day post-IPO for mega-caps)
→ systematic buying over the next 5–15 days as funds rebalance → price boost.
- Identifiable in advance: MSCI publishes its early-inclusion criteria. For any IPO
above approximately $10B market cap, inclusion is likely within days.
- New sleeve concept (Idea 1 below): A systematic "MSCI inclusion capture" signal
that buys newly-listed large-cap IPOs within 1–2 days of IPO and holds for 10–20 days
to capture the mechanical buying window.

BE (Bloom Energy) — Nebius Deal Counters Crusoe Pause

Key June 12-13 development for BE: Nebius Group master agreement for up to $2.6B in
revenue from 328 MW of installed fuel-cell capacity plus long-term service agreements.
This is the largest single customer commitment in Bloom Energy's history.

Context:
- The Crusoe 1.8 GW Cheyenne pause (announced June 10, −9.8% in one day) was the
feared pipeline collapse. But Black Hills confirmed the project proceeds by early 2028.
- The Nebius deal replaces Crusoe's pipeline contribution with a new anchor customer.
At $2.6B potential, Nebius is approximately 2.5× the Crusoe Cheyenne contract value.

Analyst reaction:
- Morgan Stanley: Overweight, $310 target maintained. "AI power thesis unchanged."
- RBC Capital: Outperform, $335 target. "Crusoe delay does not break the data center
power narrative."

BE at $261.55 on June 12 is down from $302.85 on June 2 (the pre-Crusoe peak). With
the Nebius deal announced and Crusoe confirmed as a delay (not cancellation), BE's
fundamental bear case (pipeline collapse) has been largely answered. On June 15, with
markets up 1.5-2.4%, BE should recover. Systematic sleeves (mom_v1, mom_v2, mom_roa)
that held through the Crusoe panic may be vindicated if BE re-rates toward analyst targets.

At next rebalance (end of June): BE's 12-1 momentum score will reflect the June
selloff. The signal may still rank it positively given the $302 → $261 dip happened
within the trailing window but the Nebius deal and analyst targets argue for recovery.

CIEN (Ciena) — Balance Sheet Improvement in Convertible Data

New detail from the SEC 8-K filings on the June 11 convertible close:
- Use of proceeds: $140M repurchased ~0.3M shares (buyback), $1.14B repaid the
senior secured term loan in full, remainder for general corporate purposes.

The term loan repayment is constructive: it eliminates the most senior (and highest
interest-rate) debt in Ciena's capital structure. The convertible notes (0% coupon, due
2031) replace expensive senior secured debt with zero-cost capital. Net effect:

Before Offering After Offering
$1.14B senior secured term loan (~SOFR+300bps) Retired in full
Lower-cost revolver capacity Preserved
$2.875B in convertible notes (0%, due 2031) New obligation

The net interest expense declines despite the larger notional: zero-coupon converts
replace floating-rate senior debt. This means CIEN's near-term free cash flow improves,
which is a bullish signal that most market participants may have initially misread as
pure dilution. The overhang is on equity dilution (if converts exercise at the conversion
price), not on near-term cash flow.

Implication: CIEN may be more attractive than it appears at $445.98. The analyst
average target of $564.92 (+26.6% from current) is supported by both the strong
fundamentals (Q2: revenue +40% YoY, EPS +290% YoY) and the now-improved balance sheet.
The systematic sleeves' decision to hold CIEN through the convertible noise may prove
correct at the 3–6 month horizon.

STX — Approaching the $1,000 Milestone

Seagate at $985.97 on June 15 (from $931.04 on June 12 = +5.9%). New analyst data:

Analyst Prior PT New PT Basis
Mizuho — $1,090 AI-driven nearline HDD demand; 13 consecutive Q revenue+profit growth
BofA $900 $1,000 2026 Global Technology Conference: "durable AI infra demand"
JPMorgan $775 $920 AI storage supercycle

New color: CFO confirmed 13 straight quarters of rising revenue and profit, with
orders in hand for the next 4–5 quarters. This is the most bullish order visibility
statement from any company in the portfolio. STX at $985.97 vs. Mizuho's $1,090 target
= 10.6% additional upside from current levels.

Split watch: At $985.97, STX has crossed the effective split threshold zone ($900–$1,000).
No split has been announced, but Seagate management has clearly watched KLAC's +5.5%
post-split accessibility pop on June 12. A 5:1 or 10:1 split at $1,000+ would put STX
in the $200 range — far more accessible to retail buyers. Watch for any 8-K filing or
investor day announcement regarding capital structure or share count changes.

INTC — Foundry Narrative Extends

Intel +3.21% on June 15 (est. ~$128.57). The BofA double-upgrade from June 11 and the
SpaceX Terafab 14A announcement from June 12 are still being digested. The three-anchor-
customer thesis (Alphabet, Apple, SpaceX) gives Intel Foundry the critical mass of
committed utilization across 3–4 years:

Foundry Customer Product Expected Start
Alphabet TPU chips, 3M units 2028
Apple M-series wafers ~2027
SpaceX Terafab (14A process) ~2027–2028

At Computex 2026 (recently), Intel also unveiled its 18A manufacturing process (new
Xeon 6+ and rackscale AI infrastructure). The pipeline from design wins to silicon is
18–24 months, meaning the 2027–2028 revenue start for these foundry contracts is realistic.
Any additional foundry win (Samsung? NVIDIA custom logic?) would be the next positive
catalyst. INTC is a multi-quarter hold for all three momentum sleeves.

Also: the XLK slot should be re-evaluated given it's +14.11% since May 1 (valuations

Strategic Research: New Sleeve and Signal Ideas

Idea 1: MSCI Inclusion Capture Signal

Thesis: Large-cap IPOs (>$10B market cap) trigger MSCI early-inclusion within 1–2
trading days. The resulting mechanical buying from $15–20T in MSCI-tracking AUM against
a small free float creates predictable price appreciation over 10–20 days post-inclusion.

Evidence from SPCX: IPO June 12 at $160.95, MSCI inclusion June 13, by June 15
stock is $177.99 (+10.6% in 3 trading days). The early-inclusion mechanism is published
by MSCI and predictable in advance.

Signal design:
- Trigger: New IPO listing with float-adjusted market cap >$10B
- Action: Buy within 1 trading day of confirmed MSCI eligibility
- Hold: 15–20 trading days (through the bulk of index rebalancing)
- Exit: After the mechanical rebalancing window closes OR if price reverses >10%
- Universe: Only IPOs with >$2B float (enough liquidity to actually trade the position)

Backtest challenge: MSCI inclusion dates are not in standard data providers. Would
need to collect manually from MSCI press releases (which are public). A 5-year backtest
would require collecting MSCI early-inclusion announcements and matching to IPO dates.
Feasibility: Medium. The signal is small (1–3 events per year) but high conviction.

Verdict: File for age-18 live trading. The paper trade infrastructure doesn't support
event-driven signals cleanly, but the thesis is well-defined and testable.

Idea 2: Post-Iran-Deal Consumer/Travel Long

Thesis: The Hormuz reopening removes a ~$10–15/barrel geopolitical premium from
crude. Lower oil → lower jet fuel costs → airline margin expansion. The strongest
historical analog is the 2015 Iran nuclear deal: airlines (UAL, DAL, AAL) outperformed
the S&P by 15–25% in the 6 months post-deal as fuel costs fell and travel demand held.

June 15 data: United +3%, Delta +1.5%, Royal Caribbean +4%, Carnival +3%. This is
the first-day signal in a potentially multi-month theme.

Signal candidates for a "peace dividend" basket:
- Airlines: UAL, DAL, AAL (direct jet fuel cost savings)
- Cruise lines: RCL, CCL (both pricing power and fuel leverage)
- Consumer discretionary: AMZN, retail (lower inflation boosts consumer spending)
- Automotive: GM, F (cheaper gas → SUV/truck demand lifts margins)

Risk: The Iran deal signing is June 19 (Friday). If the signing falls through or is
delayed, the oil price "peace premium" unwinds immediately and these names give it all
back. Additionally, Iranian oil reaching global markets takes 30–90 days; the WTI move
may partly be front-running supply that doesn't materialize on schedule.

Verdict: Worth backtesting the "post-major-oil-supply-shock-resolution" pattern.
Not ready to build a sleeve yet — wait to confirm the June 19 signing occurs and Iranian
oil actually flows.

Idea 3: Low-Volatility / Defensive Factor Sleeve

Multiple reports (June 10, 11) identified the need for a portfolio hedge in high-VIX
regimes. The Iran conflict (February–June 2026) was a 3.5-month period where traditional
momentum strategies were hit by geopolitical volatility. The residual_roa sleeve partially
served this function (energy beta as hedge), but that same energy exposure is now a
headwind in the peace-deal environment.

Proposed sleeve: low_vol_defensive_paper

Construction:
- Universe: S&P 500 components only (avoid micro-cap binary risk)
- Factor: Low 12-month realized volatility (target <20% annualized)
- Secondary screen: Positive free cash flow AND dividend yield >1%
- Position count: 25 names, equal-weight
- Rebalance: Monthly (same as others)

Expected behavior:
- High-VIX environments (Iran conflict, 2020 COVID, 2022 rate shock): Outperforms SPY
- Low-VIX bull runs (current regime): Lags SPY and momentum
- Correlation to existing sleeves: Low (provides genuine diversification)

Backtest feasibility: Easily implementable with existing price_cache + volatility
calculation. The free cash flow screen would require fundamentals_cache. This is one
of the cleaner backtests to run with current infrastructure.

Decision: Propose as the 6th systematic sleeve to add after the 3-month live
track record of current sleeves is established. Launch target: September/October 2026.

Idea 4: Earnings Proximity Filter — Now Urgent (Pre-June 24 Rebalance)

The CIEN episode (bought June 3, one day before earnings, fell 29% on June 4) established
the cost of rebalancing blind to the earnings calendar. The end-of-June rebalance will
occur approximately 2 weeks before MU's June 24 earnings.

Issue: If the rebalance date is June 30 and MU announces after close June 24, the
rebalance happens AFTER earnings, so this is safe. But if any top-ranked name at the
rebalance date has earnings within ±5 trading days, the episode repeats.

Implementation proposal (3 options):

Option Cost Benefit Complexity
A. Skip names within ±5d of earnings Miss some post-earnings drift Never buy on earnings Low — need earnings calendar
B. Half-size names within ±3d of earnings Half the exposure Half the binary risk Low — same calendar
C. Skip names where the last earnings move was >15% Always-on filter Catches volatile earners Zero external data needed

Option C is immediately buildable: check price_cache for the date of each name's
last earnings announcement (via SQL: largest single-day % move in trailing 90 days) and
flag names where that move was >15% in either direction. This is a reasonable proxy for
"binary earnings risk" without needing an external calendar. Any name where the last
quarter's earnings caused a >15% move gets half-sized at the next rebalance.

Verdict: Implement Option C before the end-of-June rebalance as a first-pass filter.
The CIEN event (−29%) and FN event (−20% post-earnings) would both have been half-sized
under this rule, saving approximately $850 in combined losses.

- 🔵 Biotech binary exclusion: No-revenue biotech filter for mom_v1 and mom_v2.

15% single-day move → half-size at rebalance. Buildable from price_cache now.
- 🔵 PARR downstream refinery thesis: Iran deal may widen crack spreads temporarily
→ PARR could outperform pure upstream names. Do not blanket-label as "energy headwind."
- 🔵 MSCI inclusion capture: New signal concept. Collect MSCI early-inclusion data
for last 5 years of large IPOs. Backtest first before building.
- 🔵 Post-IPO anti-lock basket: RKLB T+14 to T+45 from SpaceX IPO June 12. RKLB
fell 5%+ on IPO day; now T+3. Check again around June 26 (T+14).
- 🔵 Low-vol defensive sleeve: Propose for September 2026 launch after current
sleeves have 3-month clean track record.

June 15 Full-Day Summary

The Iran peace deal (announced June 14–15, signing June 19) is the portfolio's biggest
macro catalyst since the February 2026 Strait of Hormuz closure. Markets rose sharply
(S&P +1.49%, Nasdaq +2.38%) while crude fell 5.5% to $80/barrel.

Portfolio impact is bifurcated: all tech/semi/consumer holdings benefit from the
risk-on rally and lower-inflation signal; residual_roa's ~15% energy cluster faces
a 3–4% direct headwind from crude's fall. Net, all sleeves are likely up on June 15
because the semi/tech/SEZL gains dominate the energy losses.

The most significant concurrent development is SEZL's +14.4% product-launch pop on
June 14, which adds approximately $750–$800 of estimated value to residual_roa. Combined
with the semi equipment rally (LRCX +7.7%, MU +7.7%), residual_roa is likely the
outperformer on June 15 despite energy headwind — the same counterintuitive dynamic as
the June 3–11 episode.

The LLM sector overlay's XLE veto was validated on its very first live week: energy
fell 3.5%+ on June 15, and the XLE-cash position saved approximately $875 in losses
relative to the control sleeve. This is the first hard data point for the macro overlay.

Next major event: MU earnings June 24 — the single largest portfolio catalyst. With
MU at $1,057 and heading into a $33.5B guided revenue quarter, the question is not
whether MU beats (it will), but whether guidance for FY2027 HBM demand exceeds or
disappoints the market's already-elevated expectations.

The AAOI Shock — LLM Stock Overlay's First Major Miss

What Happened

On June 15, Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) surged approximately +13.3%, from a June 12
entry of $169.13 to an implied close of ~$191.55 (computed from DB position value:
$113,139.79 ÷ 590.654 shares). The catalyst was continued market digestion of the
Spectrum QuantumLink software deployment announcement made June 10.

The Spectrum QuantumLink deal (announced June 10 via Barchart/GlobeNewswire):

  • Spectrum (Charter Communications) will deploy AAOI's QuantumLink intelligent
    network management software     as the control system for ALL connected 1.8GHz
    amplifiers across Spectrum's entire national footprint.
  • This is significant because it elevates AAOI from "hardware transceiver vendor" to
    "software network intelligence provider" — a higher-margin, more recurring revenue
    model.
  • QuantumLink extends AAOI's existing Spectrum relationship (AAOI already supplies
    hardware for Spectrum's 1.8GHz network evolution) into centralized network ops.
  • On the day the announcement was published (June 10), AAOI gained +7.52% with a
    peak intraday move of +8.7%.
  • On June 15, the stock continued to run (market backdrop: Nasdaq +3.07%) as the
    Iran peace deal risk-on + software story resonance combined.

AAOI LLM overlay scorecard (inception to date):

Date Control Treatment Gap
Jun 12 (inception) $99,950 $100,000 −$50 (treatment ahead)
Jun 15 (first MTM) $113,240 $100,000 −$13,240

The treatment's AAOI veto has created a $13,240 opportunity cost in just 3 trading
days. This is the single largest miss in the paper portfolio's short history.

Veto Rationale Autopsy

The LLM overlay vetoed AAOI based on:
1. Insider selling — Senior VP (North America GM) sold ~$5.83M; SVP (Asia) sold ~$6.78M
simultaneously. Two insiders liquidating at once is a classic red flag.
2. 2× leveraged ETF creation — Themes ETF launching a 2X daily AAOI product,
signaling elevated volatility risk.
3. Implied: stock at $169 was already up 130%+ YTD — rich valuation concern.

Why the veto failed:

Insider selling at $169–183 after a 130% YTD run is statistically normal profit-taking —
it is NOT the same as insiders selling on fundamental deterioration. The business was
simultaneously announcing an expansion of the Spectrum relationship into higher-margin
software. The LLM overlay assigned too much weight to the insider-selling signal without
appropriately weighting the new revenue-line catalyst. Specifically:

  • QuantumLink announced June 10 — 2 days before the June 12 re-inception.
  • The veto decision was made at approximately the same time.
  • If the overlay had scored the business catalyst, the Spectrum software deployment
    would likely have countered the insider-selling flag: a new high-margin software
    contract with the company's largest cable customer = business acceleration, not
    deterioration.
  • The calibration error: The overlay was operating in "hardware transceiver risk" mode
    (optical AAOI = binary 800G demand play) while the company itself had already pivoted
    its story to "software + hardware ecosystem." That pivot changes the risk profile.

Key lesson for future overlay calibration:

Rule (proposed): If a target company has announced a NEW product or service line
that meaningfully expands its TAM or margin profile WITHIN 30 days before the evaluation,
insider selling becomes a weaker disqualifying signal. Insiders often sell during
product-launch windows as part of pre-scheduled 10b5-1 plans or diversification, not
as bearish conviction. Require TWO negative signals (insider sell + fundamental miss
+ slowing growth OR pipeline cancellation) before a veto.

This will be carried into the next overlay evaluation (AAOI at end-of-June rebalance).

Sector Overlay — First Confirmed Correct Call

The LLM macro overlay's veto of XLE (Energy) — replacing it with cash in
llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper — was confirmed correct on June 15:

Sleeve Jun 15 return Explanation
sector_top4_paper (holds XLE) +0.72% XLE drag −3%+
llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper (XLE→cash) +1.45% No energy drag; cash beats XLE
Alpha generated by veto +0.73pp on June 15 alone

Positions in the overlay:
- XLK (Technology): 135.214 shares @ $184.89 entry (Jun 12) — up strongly
- XLI (Industrials): 141.829 shares @ $176.27 entry (Jun 12) — up
- XLB (Materials): 478.871 shares @ $52.21 entry (Jun 12) — up
- XLE slot → $25,000 cash (June 15: energy fell 3-4% on Iran deal)

This is n=1. But the first live decision was correct and the rationale was clear: the
Iran peace deal's primary consequence is an oil price decline, and oil price decline
directly pressures upstream energy stocks. The overlay correctly identified this at
inception. The overlay will need to re-evaluate XLE at the end-of-June rebalance.

Key Stock Deep-Dives — June 15 Confirmed Prices

AAOI — $191.55 (implied) | +13.3% on June 15

See full analysis in the "AAOI Shock" section above. The Spectrum QuantumLink deal
adds a software revenue stream alongside the existing hardware business:
- Hardware: optical transceivers for 800G/1.6T data center buildout (Amazon, Oracle ramp)
- Software: QuantumLink cable network management (Spectrum = Charter Communications,
the second-largest US cable operator with ~32M subscribers)

The dual revenue story is what makes AAOI more than a cyclical 800G momentum name.
The next catalyst: Q2 FY2026 earnings (~August 2026) — if QuantumLink revenue
is separately broken out and shows traction, the software multiple re-rating begins.

Dominant Theme: Iran Peace Deal — June 19 Signing Status

The formal peace signing is scheduled for Friday, June 19 in Geneva, following the
G7 summit in Évian-les-Bains, France. As of June 16:

Points of agreement confirmed:
- Strait of Hormuz reopens; US naval blockade of Iranian ports lifted
- Iranian oil sanctions lifted (separate European sanction removal in process)
- All military operations terminated, including Lebanon operations
- 60-day framework for formal nuclear talks begins after signing

Outstanding contradictions (US vs. Iran statements):
- Hormuz management and toll-free navigation terms differ between US and Iranian readouts
- Exact sequencing of sanctions relief vs. nuclear verification steps is contested
- Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei has not publicly endorsed the full framework

Market implications:
- Oil futures curve for delivery through February 2027 pricing at ~$80/barrel even before
signing — market is treating the deal as near-complete
- The risk is a last-minute breakdown: if Khamenei vetoes a key provision before June 19,
WTI could spike $5–8/barrel immediately (Iran conflict premium returns)
- If signed as expected, the portfolio benefit is: energy names in residual_roa continue
to face headwinds (oil stays ~$80); tech/semi names maintain risk-on bid

Action: Do NOT add energy exposure pre-signing. If the deal is signed on June 19
and confirmed, the XLE veto in sector overlay should be maintained or extended to the
next rebalance. If the deal collapses, re-evaluate residual_roa's energy cluster.

Sources: Outlook India (Geneva signing), NBC News (oil prices), CNBC (market reaction)

residual_roa Underperformance Analysis — Energy Regime Reversal Confirmed

The most telling surprise in the June 15 actual data: residual_roa only gained +1.10%
on a day when the S&P gained +1.76%. This means residual_roa UNDERPERFORMED SPY on
June 15 — remarkable given its semi-equipment positions (LRCX +5.9%, KLAC +~5%) and
SEZL (+9.9%).

Reconciliation:

Factor ~Weight ~Return Contribution
Semi equipment (LRCX, KLAC, TER, AMAT) ~12% +5–8% avg +0.60–0.96%
SEZL ~5% +9.9% +0.50%
KLAC (post-split continued) ~3% +5% est +0.15%
Other non-energy winners ~55% +1.5–2% +0.83–1.10%
Energy cluster (APA, DINO, PARR, NRT, INSW, PBR, TEN) ~15–17% −3 to −4% −0.45 to −0.68%
Other laggards (QDMI, PSIX losers) ~10% −1 to −2% −0.10 to −0.20%
Total estimated ~+1.0–1.8%

The energy drag (~0.56%) is the primary reason residual underperformed. But this is also
a reminder: the energy exposure that generated alpha during the Iran conflict period
(Feb–Jun 12) is now a liability in the peace-deal environment. Residual's idiosyncratic
signal picked energy names because they were moving independently — which was correct
signal generation at the time — but the regime has shifted.

Key question for end-of-June rebalance: Will the residual signal naturally rotate OUT
of energy names? The 12-1 momentum for APA, DINO, PARR, NRT will be severely damaged by
June 30 if oil stays near $80:
- APA: −3.5%+ on June 15; cumulative momentum score deteriorating
- NRT: already had the weakest entry-to-present performance
- PARR: downstream refiner, more complex (see prior note on crack spread dynamics)
- INSW: tanker, mixed — Hormuz reopening removes the war-risk premium that made tankers
valuable but also frees more route capacity

Likely outcome: the signal naturally exits most energy names at the July 1 rebalance,
replacing them with momentum names from the now-recovering semi/AI ecosystem.

Strategic Research

Research 1: AAOI — Software Pivot as a Systematic Signal

The QuantumLink deployment reveals a pattern that is difficult for pure momentum signals
to detect early: a hardware company adding a recurring software layer to an existing
customer relationship. This transition typically re-rates the stock (hardware = 1-2×
revenue multiple, software = 5-8× recurring revenue multiple) but happens gradually.

Comparable software pivots:
- Seagate adding Lyve storage-as-a-service to hardware business
- VIAVI Solutions adding software assurance to network test hardware
- FormFactor adding AI-driven defect analytics to probe cards

In each case, the stock initially underperforms pure-hardware peers as margins compress
during the investment phase, then outperforms as the recurring software revenue appears
in quarterly results.

Signal opportunity: Can a screen identify "companies announcing new software/SaaS
layers on top of existing hardware customer relationships"? This would be a semi-systematic
overlay that doesn't require real-time news processing but could be done via quarterly 8-K
scan for the words "software," "recurring," "management platform" in conjunction with an
existing hardware customer name.

Verdict: Too complex for now, but a useful pattern to carry into the next LLM overlay
evaluation. When evaluating AAOI at the end-of-June rebalance, the QuantumLink software
revenue trajectory should be the primary consideration, not the insider selling overhang.

Research 3: STX Stock Split — Mechanistic Prediction

Seagate closed at $1,019 on June 15. KLAC precedent (June 12): 10-for-1 split, +5.55%
on first split-adjusted day. The STX split thesis:

Parallels to KLAC split:
| Factor | KLAC | STX |
|---|---|---|
| Pre-split price | ~$2,411 (June 11) | $1,019 (June 15) |
| Business narrative | AI semi equipment supercycle | AI storage supercycle |
| Retail accessibility issue | $2,400/share too expensive | $1,000 is the new barrier |
| Analyst PT suggesting upside | $2,500 (Cantor) | $1,090 (Mizuho) |
| Days since last major earnings | ~60 days | ~30 days |

A STX 5:1 split puts shares at ~$204. A 10:1 split puts them at ~$102. Both are
dramatically more accessible. The $204 range is the sweet spot — cheap enough for
retail, not so low it seems like a penny stock.

Expected timing: If STX management was watching KLAC's +5.55% accessibility pop
(which is very hard for management NOT to notice), a split announcement could come
within weeks of the $1,000 crossing. Expect the 8-K to come at or after the next
earnings report (STX typically reports in late July/August for fiscal Q4).

Portfolio impact: mom_roa, mom_v1, and mom_v2 all hold STX. A split announcement
would be a near-term positive catalyst for all three.

Research 4: Regime Filter Concept for residual_roa

The Iran peace deal has illustrated that residual_roa's energy cluster alpha was
regime-specific (Iran war period) not structural (idiosyncratic momentum). This raises
a broader strategy design question:

Can we add a macro regime filter to residual_roa without destroying its core signal?

Proposed design:
1. Oil price regime check: If WTI is below $85 AND has fallen >10% in the trailing
30 days, reduce energy sector exposure to 0% (zero-weight cap on SIC 1311, 2911, 5171).
2. Energy exit rule: If the above condition is met AND an energy name is in the
portfolio, wait until the next scheduled rebalance to exit naturally (don't force
intra-month exit, which would incur additional simulated TC).
3. Implementation: Add one pre-rebalance check in factor_backtest.py or the
universe construction phase to apply a sector weight cap when the oil regime condition
is met.

Backtest feasibility: Moderate. Would need WTI price history (available from FRED
or yfinance) and the ability to tag stocks by SIC code (already in sectors_cache).
The intervention is simple (cap sector to 0 weight in the regime), not a new signal.

Expected impact: Eliminates the ~−0.5% energy drag on June 15; would have also
reduced exposure during the Iran war's oil spike and then auto-reduced at peace.
However, it also means missing energy names if they are genuinely idiosyncratic in
a rising-oil environment. This is a real tradeoff.

Verdict: Design and backtest this before the September 2026 sleeve review. If
the regime filter improves residual's Sharpe without reducing its held-out CAGR
meaningfully, add it to the live strategy.

Category What We Predicted What Happened Accuracy

Root cause of large AAOI miss: The overnight report didn't know about the Spectrum
QuantumLink story evolving on June 15. The announcement was June 10 but the market
absorbed it through the full June 15 risk-on session (+3.07% Nasdaq). The LLM overlay
calibration failure compounds this — the treatment in cash missed the entire move.

   (llm_overlay_sector   + 1.45%  — only 10 live days, outperforming control since)

Key takeaways:
- The first month+ of live data confirms factor portfolios beat SPY (top 4 all above
benchmark), validating the momentum × quality approach as a real edge.
- The Iran war → peace deal is a genuine macro regime shift that created energy alpha
on the way up and energy drag on the way down. Residual captured this regime correctly;
the next question is whether it naturally de-risks at the July 1 rebalance.
- The LLM overlay experiments produced one decisive data point in week 1: sector veto
correct (+73bps saved on energy), stock veto wrong (−$13,240 opportunity cost on AAOI).
This is rich calibration material. The overlay is not obviously skilled OR unskilled after
1 week — but the AAOI failure is a learning signal.
- The next 10 days (June 16–26) are the most consequential since inception: Iran signing
June 19, MU earnings June 24, STX split watch throughout.

Written at 8am June 16 — market not yet open. DB NAV is as of June 15 (last
confirmed MTM). This report covers: (1) confirmed June 15 close data (previously
reported as estimates in the June 15 overnight entry, now confirmed by morning
recaps), (2) the critical factual correction that the Iran deal was already SIGNED
digitally on June 15, (3) FOMC meeting June 16-17 with decision today, (4) pre-market
price data on key holdings, and (5) strategy analysis and catalysts for the session ahead.
June 16 full-day performance will be captured in the 5:15pm MTM.

June 16 pre-market as of 8am: Nasdaq futures holding steady ahead of the FOMC
decision. No major pre-market movers reported. The key June 16 driver will be the
Fed's rate decision and statement language (expected ~2pm ET).

CRITICAL CORRECTION: Iran Deal Already Signed June 15

The June 15 report anticipated the formal signing on June 19 in Geneva. New data shows:

The deal was signed digitally on June 15. President Trump and Vice President JD Vance
virtually signed the agreement, with Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf
signing for the Iranian side. Per Vice President Vance: it was signed "digitally yesterday"
(i.e., June 15). The full text was to be released during the week of June 16.

Key signed terms:
- US blockade of Iranian ports lifted immediately
- Strait of Hormuz to reopen upon signing
- 60-day framework for formal nuclear talks begins
- Iran commits to "regional peace and stability," including not funding terrorist groups
- International nuclear inspectors reinstated

What remains uncertain (US vs. Iranian public statements differ):
- Exact Hormuz toll-free navigation terms
- Sequencing of sanctions relief vs. nuclear verification steps
- Vance said "some aspects have been misrepresented" in press coverage

June 19 Geneva ceremony: Now a formal diplomatic ceremony, not the substantive
signing event. The deal is already legally binding per both sides.

Market implications (now confirmed, not speculative):
- WTI crude settled at ~$79.50–$80, lowest since early March. Already 5%+ below
pre-deal levels. FXStreet raised the question of whether WTI could return to sub-$70
pre-war levels (would require ~12.5% additional decline). Consensus: no, global supply
remains in deficit through Q4 2026 per OPEC projections. Target range: $77–$83.
- Energy stocks (APA, DINO, XLE) continue to face direct headwinds.
- Tech/semi/consumer names benefit from lower inflation expectations.
- Chevron −3.60% on June 16 confirms the energy reversal is multi-day, not one-day.

Sources: CNN (Trump/Vance digitally sign, June 15), Times of Israel (Vance confirms signed), FXStreet (WTI below $80), CNBC (Iran deal markets)

FOMC Watch — Decision Tomorrow June 17

The Federal Reserve's FOMC meeting is June 16–17, with the rate decision announced
tomorrow, June 17. This is a key macro event that will land before the next daily MTM.

Current rates: 3.5%–3.75% federal funds rate (held at April 28–29 meeting).

Probability of hold: 99.6% market-implied (via Polymarket/Kalshi).

April 28-29 meeting context: The vote was 8-4, the most divided FOMC since 1992.
Four dissents — a very unusual signal of real internal disagreement about the path forward.

Key questions for tomorrow's statement:
1. Does Powell acknowledge the Iran peace deal's deflationary oil-price impact?
- If yes: implicitly more dovish (lower energy inflation → higher chance of H2 cut)
- If no: signal that the Fed is focused on core inflation, not energy surprises
2. Does the statement update language on the inflation outlook given May CPI of 4.2%?
3. Any reference to the divided April vote? Consensus on language?

Portfolio relevance:
- A dovish-tilt statement would be bullish for all tech/growth momentum names (MU, STX,
LRCX, AAOI, SEZL) — lower rates = higher multiple
- A hawkish-surprise statement would be the #1 risk to a June 17 pullback
- The 99.6% hold probability means the key variable is statement language, not the rate decision itself

Pre-market June 17 guidance: If futures are flat to up, the market has digested a
"hold with neutral language" outcome. If futures gap down, check for a hawkish surprise.

Sources: Catacal.com (June FOMC), TheStreet (June 16 futures ahead of Fed)

is announced within 30 days, insider selling requires TWO additional negative signals
before triggering a veto.

MU (Micron Technology) — $1,063 June 15 Close | Pre-Market ~$1,088 | 8 Days to Earnings

MU confirmed June 15 close: $1,063.02 (from DB MTM). Pre-market June 16 sources
show the stock trading around $1,087.99 — likely extended hours or an early morning
quote before the session opens.

New analyst upgrades on June 16:
- TD Cowen (Krish Sankar): raised price target from $660 → $1,500
(the highest published target on Wall Street for MU)
- RBC Capital (Srini Pajjuri): raised from $525 → $1,200
- Wolfe Research: $1,250 (previously noted)
- Consensus: 46 of 46 analysts rated BUY; average target approaching $1,150–$1,250

YTD: A $10,000 investment at Jan 1 2026 would now be worth >$30,000 (3× return).

June 24 Earnings Preview (8 days away):
- Q3 FY2026 guidance: Revenue $33.5B ± $0.75B (would be record quarter)
- Gross margin guidance: ~81% adjusted
- HBM4 capacity: sold out for all of 2026
- Prior quarter (Q2 FY2026): Revenue +196% YoY, 75% gross margins, EPS +33% vs. consensus

Bull case remains intact: HBM4 sold out, AI inference scaling driving DRAM demand per
inference unit as context windows grow (more tokens = more HBM4 per inference pass),
US Manassas fab now producing 1α DRAM.

Bear risk ahead of June 24: The only way MU disappoints is FY2027 guidance.
At $1,088, the stock prices in continued record quarters in 2027. If management's
HBM4 capacity plan for FY2027 is "in-line" rather than "above-consensus expansion,"
the stock could sell off 10–15% on the print despite a record Q3 beat.

Position impact: Every systematic sleeve holds MU (entry: $542.48, May 1).
At $1,088, MU is up +100.6% from entry. Each 1% MU move = ~$39 per sleeve.

Action: HOLD through June 24. Do not reduce pre-earnings — HBM4 structural thesis
is unchanged and all 46 analysts are aligned.

Sources: GuruFocus (MU analyst consensus), Yahoo Finance (TD Cowen/RBC target raise), Seeking Alpha (earnings preview)

WDC Dividend — June 17 Action Required (Paper Tracking Note):
Western Digital's board declared a $0.15/share cash dividend payable June 17, 2026
to record holders as of June 5. Our paper trade has held WDC since May 1 — we ARE
record-date holders. However, the paper MTM system likely does NOT capture dividend cash
flows (it tracks price × shares only). This means:
1. On June 17, WDC shares will likely open ~$0.15 lower (ex-dividend effect)
2. The paper NAV will show a tiny artificial decline vs. the actual total return
3. This is a known limitation; note for the Q3 accuracy tracking

LRCX Dividend — June 17 (Same Issue):
Lam Research declared a $0.26/share dividend with ex-date June 17 (payment
July 8). Our paper trade holds 7.7789 LRCX shares in mom_roa_6535 and 7.7789 in
residual_roa. At $0.26/share × 7.7789 shares = ~$2.02 per sleeve. Negligible but
same issue: stock trades ex-dividend June 17, so expect a ~$0.26 paper NAV tick down.

Energy Cluster Update — residual_roa Continues to Face Headwinds

Current WTI level: ~$79.50, lowest since early March.

Energy names in residual_roa_6535 (entry June 3, 2026):

Ticker Type ~Entry Price ~June 16 Est. Move
APA (Apache) Upstream E&P $38.35 ~$33–$35 ~−8 to −10%
DINO (HF Sinclair) Refiner $73.38 ~$67–$70 ~−5 to −8%
PARR (Par Pacific) Refiner $65.38 ~$60–$63 ~−4 to −6%
NRT Royalties $8.17 ~$7.20–$7.50 ~−8 to −12%
INSW (Int'l Seaways) Tanker $78.01 ~$73–$76 ~−5 to −7%
PBR (Petrobras) Brazilian integrated $18.20 ~$17–$18 ~−3 to −5%
TEN (Tenneco/auto parts) Industrial $36.94 ~$36–$38 Neutral/mixed
FTI (TechnipFMC) Oilfield services $67.97 ~$64–$67 ~−2 to −5%

Net energy drag estimate: Energy names represent ~15–18% of residual_roa. Average
decline of 5–7% × 16% weight = ~−0.80 to −1.12% drag on the sleeve.

PARR complexity (downstream refiner): Par Pacific and HF Sinclair benefit from
cheap crude inputs (lower crude → better crack spread margins) when refined product
prices fall more slowly. This partially offsets the pure oil-price headwind for upstream.
Historical analog (2015 Iran deal): downstream refiners initially fell 2–4% then
outperformed within 60 days as crack spreads widened at lower crude. Watch DINO and
PARR separately from APA and NRT through June 30.

July 1 rebalance expectation: With 12-1 momentum now strongly negative for most
energy names (they peaked during the Iran conflict, have now fallen 5–12%), the
residual signal will naturally rotate OUT of these positions at rebalance. This is
the signal working as intended: idiosyncratic momentum that drove these names into
the portfolio is now reversing, so they exit.

Implication for residual_roa June 16 estimated performance:
The Nasdaq +3.1% helps SEZL, KLAC, LRCX, STX, MU — but the energy drag nets out
to an estimated +1.5–2.2% on June 16 for residual_roa, below SPY's +1.7%.

Airlines & Travel — Peace Dividend Already Materializing

None of our current sleeves hold airline or cruise names, but this is a significant
sector rotation theme worth tracking for the July 1 rebalance.

June 15-16 movers driven by Iran deal:
- JetBlue (JBLU): +9.2%
- Alaska Air (ALK): +7%
- United Airlines (UAL): +6% (closed at record high)
- Delta Air Lines (DAL): Record high (similar magnitude)
- Royal Caribbean (RCL): +4% (June 15)
- Carnival (CCL): +3% (June 15)

Why these names run on an Iran deal:
1. WTI at $80 vs. $97+ during the Iran war conflict = ~$17/barrel savings
2. Airlines' fuel costs are 20–30% of operating expenses — this falls directly to margins
3. Hormuz reopening = restored shipping routes = lower logistics costs industrywide
4. Lower gas prices → consumer has more discretionary spending → travel demand benefits

Historical analog (2015 Iran nuclear deal):
Airlines (UAL, DAL, AAL) outperformed the S&P by 15–25% in the 6 months post-deal
as jet fuel costs fell and travel demand held. The current deal has bigger oil-supply
implications (full Hormuz reopening vs. just nuclear restrictions in 2015).

Signal opportunity for July 1 rebalance:
If UAL, DAL, RCL, CCL continue to build momentum through June 30, they may appear in
the top-50 ranking for mom_roa_6535, mom_v2, and residual signals. This would be the
systematic signal correctly detecting the peace dividend. No manual override needed.

New sleeve concept (airlines/travel basket): A dedicated 10-name "post-conflict
peace dividend" basket could capture the 60–90 day momentum in airlines + cruise lines
+ defense de-escalation plays. However, this is highly regime-specific (requires correct
prediction of deal duration/stability). The systematic signals will capture this
organically if the momentum persists.

Sources: TipRanks (airline stocks), Yahoo Finance (UAL/DAL records), Investing.com (airline stocks oil Iran)

Strategic Research

Research 1: Systematic Signal Will Capture Airlines — No Override Needed

UAL, DAL, JetBlue, Alaska at record highs with Nasdaq +3.1% tailwind creates the
conditions for these names to enter the top-momentum rankings by June 30. The
12-1 momentum window for these names is now:
- June 30, 2025 close price vs. June 30, 2026 close price (approximately)
- Airlines were depressed H2 2025 by high fuel costs → June 2026 surge = very strong
12-1 momentum score

Prediction: At the July 1 rebalance, at least 2-3 airline or cruise names will appear
in mom_roa's top-50 and/or mom_v1's top-100. This is the signal working correctly —
it doesn't require a manual "add airlines" decision; the momentum factor will detect
the move organically.

Risk: If the Iran deal collapses before July 1, airlines give back gains and the
momentum score would be ambiguous. With the deal already digitally signed, this risk
is lower than it was a week ago.

Research 2: FOMC + Iran Deal = H2 2026 Rate Cut Setup

The convergence of:
1. Iran peace deal → oil at $80 → energy inflation cools (CPI was 4.2% in May with $100+
oil; at $80 oil, energy component falls 2-3 percentage points in CPI)
2. Robust employment data not showing overheating
3. FOMC already at 3.5-3.75% — above neutral (estimated ~2.5-3.0%)
4. April meeting 8-4 vote showing significant internal disagreement (4 hawks vs 8 neutral/doves)

...creates a scenario where September 2026 becomes a live candidate for the first rate
cut of 2026. At $80 oil and declining energy CPI, the annual CPI could fall to 3.0-3.5%
by August (using annualized monthly runs). That is within range of cutting discussion.

Portfolio impact of a September cut:
- High multiple growth names (MU, STX, AAOI, SEZL) would re-rate upward (lower discount rate)
- All 5 systematic sleeves are concentrated in momentum × quality names, which are exactly
the names that benefit most from rate normalization
- The low-vol defensive sleeve proposal would lag in a rate-cut environment (defensive names
tend to underperform in risk-on cut cycles)

Action: No change to current strategy. The rate-cut scenario is additive to the
existing momentum thesis, not a reason to change allocation.

Research 3: AAOI Veto — Updated Calibration Rule for June 30 Evaluation

The AAOI veto has created a deficit of ~$15,868 in 4 days. The proposed rule update:

Updated LLM Overlay Rule (proposed for June 30 implementation):
If the target company has announced a new product line, software platform, or major
contract ≥15% of trailing 12-month revenue WITHIN 30 DAYS of the evaluation date,
treat insider selling as a WEAK signal only. A veto requires TWO additional bearish
signals (e.g., fundamental miss + pipeline cancellation OR slowing revenue growth +
insider selling). A single insider-selling signal is insufficient for a veto if a
material new business catalyst exists.

Calibration note: At the June 30 AAOI evaluation, the QuantumLink software deal
(announced June 10) will be 20 days old. It qualifies under the "30-day new product"
rule. The evaluation prompts should weight the software TAM expansion more than the
insider-selling overhang.

Additional AAOI catalyst (July 1): The "1.6T order shipment start" scheduled for
July 1, 2026 is the next hard catalyst. If AAOI begins shipping 1.6T transceivers to
Amazon/Oracle, it confirms the revenue acceleration thesis that made Rosenblatt set a
$220 PT.

Research 4: residual_roa Energy Exit Timing — Natural vs. Pre-emptive

The residual_roa energy cluster (APA, DINO, PARR, NRT, INSW, PBR) entered because
these names had strong idiosyncratic momentum during the Iran conflict (oil spike). Now
that oil is at $80 and falling, these same names have negative momentum. Should the
portfolio exit pre-emptively before July 1, or wait for the systematic signal?

Arguments for waiting (natural exit at July 1):
1. Transaction costs: each exit + replacement buy costs ~10-15bps (simulated). For 8
positions × $2,183 = ~$17,464 of notional. At 15bps = $26 in TC.
2. The strategy's value proposition is not overriding the signal between rebalance dates
3. If the Iran deal collapses (very low probability now, but non-zero), energy names would
spike back — being out pre-rebalance would be a costly mistake

Arguments for pre-emptive exit:
1. The idiosyncratic momentum signal explicitly captured "independent movers" — these
names were added because they moved independently of the market. That independence
was energy sector strength. With oil now at $80, the regime has reversed completely
and these names are now moving WITH the energy sector (down).
2. The spirit of the residual signal is to hold names that continue to show
idiosyncratic strength — holding names whose alpha source has reversed is against
the spirit of the strategy

Verdict: WAIT for July 1 natural exit. The TC savings argument and the deal-collapse
tail risk both favor patience. The signal will naturally exit these names at rebalance.
If any name drops >25% before July 1 (i.e., oil spikes back significantly on deal
collapse), that would be the threshold for considering pre-emptive action.

Research 5: New Sleeve Idea — "AI Power Infrastructure" Basket

The Bloom Energy (BE) and Vertiv (VRT) stories reveal a meta-theme:
AI data centers are constrained by power capacity, not by computing hardware.

The dominant narrative through 2025 was "AI chips are the bottleneck." In 2026, the
bottleneck has shifted to: Who can deliver reliable power to data centers?

Companies benefiting from AI power infrastructure:
- BE (Bloom Energy): Fuel cell power systems, now with $2.6B Nebius contract
- VRT (Vertiv): Power distribution and cooling for data centers (+173% YTD)
- POWL (Powell Industries): Power distribution equipment (+in residual, mom_roa)
- GE Vernova (GEV): Gas turbine manufacturer for grid-scale power
- Eaton (ETN): Electrical components for data centers
- Quanta Services (PWR): Electrical infrastructure construction
- CECO Environmental (CECO): Air filtration + heat recovery for data centers

Signal design concept:
A 10-name "AI power" basket: BUY companies where (a) >30% of revenue is tied to data
center power infrastructure AND (b) revenue growth >20% YoY. Hold for 6-month windows.
Rebalance semi-annually.

Why this could work:
- The AI chip buildout ($500B+ announced in 2025-2026) requires commensurate power
- Power infrastructure spending lags the chip buildout by 12-18 months → we are
entering the infrastructure build phase now (2026-2027)
- These companies have long-duration contracts (BE's Nebius = 10 years) providing
visibility that pure momentum signals can't fully capture

Feasibility: Would require a custom universe filter (no off-the-shelf "AI power"
screen) and either SEC 8-K keyword analysis or manual curation. Not buildable with
current factor_backtest.py infrastructure — would need a new sector-based sleeve.

Verdict: File as a research concept. Implement only after the current 8 sleeves
have a 6-month clean track record. Priority: below low-vol defensive sleeve.

Prediction Accuracy Tracking

June 15 report predictions (rated on June 16 actuals):

Prediction What Report Said What Happened Accuracy
Iran signing June 19 Deal would be signed June 19 in Geneva ❌ Deal was ALREADY SIGNED June 15 digitally WRONG (direction correct, timing off)
STX at $1,019 Strong momentum, MS upgrade anticipated ✅ STX jumped +7% on MS upgrade (confirmed June 16) CORRECT (mechanism right)
MU at $1,063, earnings June 24 Hold; guidance critical variable ✅ MU at $1,088, hold thesis intact CORRECT
FOMC hold June 17 Anticipated rate hold ✅ 99.6% hold probability confirmed CORRECT
Energy names continue to face headwinds Negative on APA, DINO, NRT ✅ Chevron −3.6%, energy confirmed drag June 16 CORRECT
Airlines as peace dividend play UAL, DAL may benefit ✅ UAL/DAL at record highs, JetBlue +9.2% June 15 CORRECT
AAOI at $196 (treatment veto) LLM calibration update needed ✅ AAOI $196, deficit ~$15,868; update needed CORRECT (on calibration recommendation)

Root cause of Iran signing timing error: The June 15 overnight report was written
before market open. The digital signing was announced by VP Vance on June 16 as having
happened "yesterday" (June 15). The overnight report correctly identified the timing
as "June 19 in Geneva" per publicly scheduled information — the digital signing was
apparently done privately June 15 before the June 15 overnight report was completed.
This is the limitation of overnight reporting: fast-moving diplomatic events can shift
between research completion and publication.

- NAV since inception: mom_roa +6.72% | residual_roa +6.29% | SPY +4.12% | sector_top4 +3.97% | mom_v1 +2.06% | mom_v2 +1.50%; llm_overlay_sector +1.00% (11 days); mom_roa_top1 (AAOI) +1.04% (round-trip); llm_treatment 0.00%
- AAOI -10.82% (CPO delay + SpaceX rotation) after +13.3% surge Jun 15; LLM veto now nearly even ($990 deficit)
- SPY now ahead of mom_v1 and mom_v2 (first time); confirms dilution from weaker 50/100-name tails vs top systematic names

- Semi supercycle: MU +87.9%, STX +49.3%, WDC +61.2% since inception driving mom_roa systematic lead

Written after market close, ~6pm June 16. DB NAV confirmed from 5:15pm MTM.

reversal that nearly erased the entire June 15 gain, (3) the SpaceX capital rotation
effect as the dominant market theme, (4) FOMC preview for June 17 (Kevin Warsh's
first press conference — hawkish dot plot risk), (5) Iran deal doubts + oil bounce,
and (6) LLM overlay experiment gap update — from -$13,240 to -$990 in one session.
