June 16 Market Recap — SpaceX Rotation Day

Index Closes (Confirmed June 16)

Index June 16 Close 1-Day Change Notes
S&P 500 ~7,543–7,549 ~−0.08% to −0.14% Roughly flat; tech drag offset by Dow
Nasdaq Composite ~26,376–26,560 ~−0.46% to −1.15% Tech/growth selloff
Dow Jones ~51,980–52,000 ~+0.64 to +0.78% Industrials/financials leadership
S&P 500 (SPY DB) — −0.60% DB-confirmed (May 1 NAV tracking)

The Nasdaq's soft session on June 16 was primarily driven by capital rotation into SpaceX,
which surpassed Amazon to become the fifth-most valuable company in the world at $2.7–2.8T.

SpaceX June 16 headline events:
- Acquired AI coding startup Cursor in an all-stock deal worth $60 billion (deal
size disclosed June 16; details not yet fully public)
- Added compute leasing deals with Anthropic and Google (orbital AI infrastructure)
- Stock up +8-10% on June 16 (after +20% on June 15 post-IPO), climbing past $212/share
- Valuation overtook Amazon, making SpaceX the 5th largest company globally within 4 days
of its historic IPO

The rotation dynamic: Retail and institutional investors who wanted SpaceX exposure
in the post-IPO window sold existing AI/tech positions (MU, LRCX, KLAC, AAOI) to fund
SpaceX purchases. This created a synchronized sell-off in high-momentum names that had
no fundamental news on June 16 — a textbook "new IPO oxygen vacuum" effect. SpaceX is
NOT in our momentum universe (insufficient 12-month price history), so we cannot benefit
directly. But the rotation headwind hit our concentrated semi positions.

Sources: Motley Fool (SpaceX market day June 16),
TechCrunch (SpaceX passes Amazon at $2.7T),
NBC News (SpaceX tops Amazon)

AAOI — The Dramatic Reversal

This is the single most important event for the portfolio on June 16.

Metric Value
AAOI June 15 DB-confirmed close $191.55 (implied: $113,139 ÷ 590.654 shares)
AAOI June 16 DB-confirmed close ~$170.81 (implied: $100,890 ÷ 590.654 shares)
1-Day change −$20.74 = −10.82%
Intraday high (per pre-market data) ~$196–$199 (pre-report level)
Intraday reversal (peak to close) ~−12.8% intraday

The morning's pre-market data showed AAOI at $196–$199, continuing the June 15 surge.
A bearish report hit during the trading session, triggering a sharp reversal back to
~$170.81 by the 5:15pm MTM. The GuruFocus article titles "Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI)
Shares Drop 14% Amid Bearish Report" and the Seeking Alpha piece "Applied Optoelectronics
leads networking stocks down following report on CPO rollout delay" both match this pattern.

What likely happened: The co-packaged optics (CPO) delay thesis from Semi Analysis
(which caused the original June 9 -14% drop) received further attention or amplification
on June 16. The SpaceX-driven rotation away from optical/AI networking names compounded
the technical selling. The combination of:
1. Elevated valuation ($196 = +15.9% above June 12 entry of $169.13)
2. CPO delay narrative resurfacing
3. SpaceX rotation (investors exiting AI optical names for AI space names)
...created the sharp intraday reversal.

Three-day AAOI oscillation (mom_roa_top1 control):

Date NAV 1-Day Return Explanation
Jun 12 (inception) $99,950 — Entry at $169.13
Jun 15 (MTM) $113,240 +13.29% Spectrum QuantumLink software deal catalyzed broad AI optical rally
Jun 16 (MTM) $100,990 −10.82% CPO delay thesis + SpaceX rotation + bearish report

Net 4-day round-trip: +$1,040 = +1.04%. The stock went from $169.13 → $191.55 → $170.81.

Lesson for the portfolio: Single-name momentum positions at high valuations are
violently mean-reverting when the narrative turns. The mom_roa_top1 strategy deliberately
accepts this volatility as the price of maximum alpha concentration. The June 15–16 episode
illustrates the real risk of a top-1 approach: +13.3% in one day, -10.82% the next.

Sources: GuruFocus (AAOI drops 14% on bearish report),
Seeking Alpha (AAOI CPO delay report)

LLM Stock Overlay Experiment — Gap Nearly Erased in One Session

This is the most significant development for the LLM overlay experiment to date.

Date Control (mom_roa_top1) Treatment (llm_overlay) Treatment Gap
Jun 12 (inception) $99,950 $100,000 −$50 (treatment slightly ahead)
Jun 15 (MTM) $113,240 $100,000 −$13,240 (treatment behind)
Jun 16 (MTM) $100,990 $100,000 −$990 (treatment nearly even)

In a single trading session, the treatment's deficit went from −$13,240 to −$990 — a
$12,250 swing. The AAOI veto, which appeared catastrophically wrong on June 15 (−$13,240),
has been partially vindicated in 24 hours.

Current status:
- Control is only $990 ahead of the treatment (0.98%)
- If AAOI declines another ~2.7% from $170.81 to ~$166, the treatment goes AHEAD of
the control for the first time
- If AAOI recovers and re-tests $196, the deficit widens back to ~$15,000

What this tells us about the veto:

The veto was made based on insider selling + 2× ETF creation signals (June 12 evaluation).
The stock then:
1. Rose +13.3% on June 15 (QuantumLink software deal — strong business catalyst, overlay underweighted)
2. Fell −10.82% on June 16 (CPO delay thesis resurgence — technically driven, consistent with the bearish case)

The AAOI price action is highly volatile and mean-reverting at elevated levels. The veto
rationale (bearish signals at high valuation) is partially validated by the June 16 reversal,
even if the June 15 timing was costly.

Updated overlay calibration question: Does this change the proposed rule update?

Proposed rule (from June 16 pre-market report): If a major new product/contract ≥15%
of trailing revenue was announced within 30 days, treat insider selling as weak signal only.
Require TWO negative signals for a veto.

June 16 data suggests: The QuantumLink deal was real and material (+13.3% day), but it
did not fundamentally change AAOI's risk profile (CPO delay concern is structural, not
company-specific). The revised rule remains appropriate BUT must include a CPO/optical
delivery delay as an independent bearish signal category, separate from insider selling.

New proposed combined rule for June 30 evaluation:

A veto requires either: (A) insider selling PLUS CPO/delivery delay risk evidence OR
(B) insider selling PLUS new-product contract fails to close within 60 days. A new
product announcement alone does not override both signals — it only overrides one.

KLAC (KLA Corporation) — −5.15% on June 16

KLAC declined 5.15% on June 16, the largest confirmed single-name decline among the
key holdings tracked in this report.

Drivers of the decline:

  1. China export restrictions: Strict government export controls on semiconductor
    technology shipments to China are estimated to cost KLAC $300–$350M in forfeited
    revenue     for fiscal year 2026. As macro uncertainty around US-China trade policy
    spiked slightly ahead of the FOMC meeting, this concern resurfaced.

  2. Unusual options activity (June 15 setup): Traders acquired 39,161 put contracts
    on June 15    , a 1,456% increase above the typical daily average. This massive
    bearish options positioning preceded the June 16 decline — the market had telegraphed
    the move.

  3. Post-split normalization: The June 12 10-for-1 split generated a +5.55%
    accessibility pop. Some of that split premium was being worked off on June 16 as
    the novelty faded.

  4. SpaceX rotation: Capital flowing into SpaceX came partly from semiconductor
    equipment names (KLAC, LRCX, AMAT) — the "infrastructure play" baton passed from
    wafer-fab equipment to orbital AI compute.

Portfolio impact: residual_roa_6535 holds 11.568 shares of KLAC (split-adjusted from
the original 1.1568 pre-split). At −5.15%, KLAC's contribution to residual_roa:
- KLAC market value pre-close: 11.568 × ~$263 (Jun 15 split-adjusted) ≈ $3,042
- Loss from −5.15%: −$157 ≈ −0.15% drag on residual_roa NAV

This was a contributing factor to residual_roa's −0.99% day, alongside the energy cluster.

Sources: TradingKey (KLAC moved down 5.15% Jun 16)

MU (Micron Technology) — $1,057.18 Close | −0.55% | June 24 T−8 Days

MU was remarkably resilient on June 16 given the tech selloff:

  • June 15 DB-implied close: ~$1,063.02
  • June 16 close: $1,057.18 (per search data; intraday range: low $1,020, high $1,133)
  • 1-Day change: −$5.84 = −0.55%

The intraday range of $113 ($1,020–$1,133) tells the real story: MU whipsawed dramatically
within the session. The high of $1,133 was likely achieved early as the Iran deal tailwind
and pre-FOMC optimism briefly pushed semis up; the selloff into the close (closing at
$1,057 near the lower end of the range) reflects the SpaceX rotation + FOMC caution.

Earnings countdown: June 24 is 8 days away.

The street's optimism remains intense:
- TD Cowen target: $1,500 (Street high)
- RBC target: $1,200
- Wolfe Research: $1,250
- All 46 analysts rated BUY

The key question for June 24: FY2027 HBM4 capacity guidance. MU at $1,057 has a
$1.1T+ market cap. Even a record Q3 FY2026 beat (revenue $33.5B, gross margins 81%) will
not sustain the stock if FY2027 guidance is "in-line" rather than "above-consensus expansion."
Given DRAM prices are expected +30–40% QoQ in calendar Q3 2026 (per RBC), the bar for
FY2027 guidance commentary is extremely high.

Monitoring flag: If the June 17 FOMC dot plot shows a hawkish surprise (rate HIKE
projections for 2026), MU could face meaningful pre-earnings pressure on June 17.
High multiple tech names are most sensitive to rate path shifts.

Sources: TradersUnion (MU stock June 16 close),
GuruFocus (MU analysts optimistic)

Energy Cluster — Oil Bounced on Iran Deal Doubts

WTI crude oil saw a small bounce on June 16, with the open price at ~$81.19,
slightly above the June 15 settled price of ~$79.50. The primary driver: growing
doubts about the Iran deal's durability.

What caused the Iran deal uncertainty on June 16:

  1. Trump linked the deal to Abraham Accords: President Trump publicly added new
    conditionality — stating any PERMANENT peace deal would require Iran's participation
    in the Abraham Accords framework. This was NOT in the original digitally-signed terms,
    reducing market confidence in the deal's finality.

  2. U.S. forces struck Iranian missile sites in the Strait of Hormuz during or after
    the deal signing, undermining confidence that the ceasefire was holding.

  3. The NBC News headline: "Oil prices fall on Iran deal, but whether they go much lower
    'is highly questionable'" — IEA projects the global market remains in supply deficit
    through at least Q4 2026. 14M barrels/day of supply has been shut in since February.

Portfolio impact (residual_roa energy cluster):

An oil bounce to $81 is mixed for the energy names in residual_roa. Upstream names (APA,
NRT) benefit from higher crude; downstream refiners (DINO, PARR) benefit less (crack spreads
narrow when crude rises). On net, a $1.50 oil bounce from $79.50 to $81 is a +1-2%
tailwind for upstream, partially offsetting the broader tech/momentum selloff.

This partially explains why residual_roa (−0.99%) held up better than mom_roa_6535
(−3.65%) on June 16: the energy cluster, which was a drag on June 15, partially reversed
course as oil doubt-bounced. The idiosyncratic momentum from energy names' Iran-era run
is being slowly unwound rather than collapsing — consistent with the "wait for July 1
natural exit" decision.

Key takeaway on Iran deal: The deal is signed but its terms are contested and its
durability is uncertain. WTI is likely to oscillate between $78–83 rather than falling
to sub-$75 in the near-term. This regime is more "uncertain peace" than "confirmed peace."

Sources: IG (Oil falls on US-Iran deal, Jun 16),
NBC News (oil prices Iran deal doubts)

FOMC Preview — Kevin Warsh's First Press Conference (June 17 2pm ET)

This is the single most important event for the portfolio in the next 24 hours.

Background:
- Kevin Warsh was confirmed as Fed Chair on May 13, 2026, succeeding Jerome Powell
- June 16–17 is Warsh's FIRST FOMC meeting as Chair
- Rate hold at 3.5–3.75% is virtually certain (97% implied probability)
- This meeting INCLUDES a dot plot (Summary of Economic Projections — SEP)

What to watch on June 17:

1. The Dot Plot — Hawkish Shift Risk (Primary Portfolio Risk)

The March 2026 dot plot showed one small rate cut. With:
- May 2026 CPI at 4.2% (elevated)
- Iran deal reducing energy inflation going forward (but not yet in data)
- April FOMC was 8-4 (most divided since 1992)
- At least 3 FOMC members may now project rate hikes for H2 2026

If the June dot plot shifts to "no cuts" or shows a hike projection, this is bearish for
all high-multiple growth names in the portfolio — specifically MU, STX, AAOI, SEZL.
A rate hike scenario compresses the terminal multiple for HBM4 thesis, HDD supercycle,
and BNPL fintech names. Estimated impact: −5 to −10% on high-beta momentum names if
the dot plot signals a hike by year-end.

2. Warsh's Communication Style — New Variable

Warsh is known for being more hawkish than Powell and for prioritizing inflation
credibility over growth support. His language at the press conference will establish
a new communication benchmark. Key phrases to watch:
- "Sufficiently restrictive" (hawkish language retained) vs. "appropriately positioned"
(neutral stance shift)
- Any acknowledgment of the Iran deal oil-price deflation (dovish implication) vs.
"we need to see sustained progress in core inflation" (hawkish)
- Dot plot unanimity (cohesive hawkish signal) vs. dot plot dispersion (intra-FOMC
disagreement, ambiguous)

3. Portfolio Positioning Pre-FOMC (No Action Needed)

All positions should be HELD through June 17. Pre-emptive reduction based on FOMC
outcome speculation is not recommended because:
- The hold is 97% priced
- If the dot plot is hawkish but rates hold, the market reaction may be temporary
(the rate LEVEL hasn't changed)
- June 24 MU earnings is the more important near-term catalyst than FOMC language
- Energy names benefiting from Iran deal uncertainty (oil bounce) should continue
to buffer residual_roa against tech weakness

Action: Monitor pre-market June 17 futures after 8am. If Nasdaq futures are down

1%, the FOMC dot plot was more hawkish than expected. If futures are flat-to-up, the
dot plot was benign.

Sources: FXStreet (Warsh opens first FOMC meeting),
IndexBox (Fed holds rates, Warsh),
StockTitan (Fed rate decision June 17),
CBS News (Warsh first FOMC)

SEZL, STX, WDC, INTC, CIEN, BE — Supporting Cast June 16

SEZL (Sezzle): No material news on June 16. The stock was trading at an estimated
fair opening of ~$143.84 (per pre-market data from StockInvest), modestly below the
June 15 close of $145.87. In a -1% Nasdaq session, SEZL likely declined modestly.
The fundamental story — Q1 GMV $1B+, revenue guidance raised to 30–35% growth — has not
changed. Strong performer since inception (+70.6% from $85.50 entry). Watch B. Riley's
"tougher comparisons" flag for Q2 2026 earnings (likely August).

STX (Seagate): The June 16 data is ambiguous. One source shows STX at $1,018.80
(which matches the June 15 close); another shows it rising +9.43% on June 15 (already
confirmed). The Morgan Stanley upgrade ($1,035 PT) hit this morning. Net: STX likely
was roughly flat to slightly down on June 16 in line with the Nasdaq. The MS upgrade
provides a floor, but KLAC's −5.15% and the SpaceX rotation created headwinds for all
semi equipment/storage names. No material news specific to STX on June 16.

WDC (Western Digital): Trading range shown as $592.63–$658.80, current $653.53.
WDC appears to have retraced slightly from June 15's strong session (where it likely
traded near $640–$660 given the Nasdaq +3.1% day). The June 17 dividend of $0.15/share
is tomorrow — expect a mechanical ~$0.15 ex-dividend dip in the opening price. Paper MTM
will show a minor artificial NAV tick down on June 17.

INTC (Intel): One source shows Intel at $114.57. This is notably BELOW the estimated
$132+ from the June 16 pre-market report. This could reflect a significant Intel-specific
decline, a data source artifact, or a pre-market price from an earlier day. Intel at $115
vs. the BofA $135 target and $112.77 entry price would represent +1.97% net gain from
entry — very different from the +17% estimated yesterday. If confirmed, INTC had a large
intraday decline on June 16, possibly related to the China export restriction concern that
hit KLAC (INTC foundry also has China exposure). Worth monitoring June 17 for confirmation.

CIEN (Ciena): The Colt Technology quantum-safe transatlantic demo (announced June 16
pre-market) should have provided support, but in a broad Nasdaq selloff, optical names
faced headwinds. CIEN's June 16 performance likely tracked its recent -12.4% from entry
trajectory — flat to slightly down. At the July 1 rebalance, CIEN's 12-1 momentum score
will almost certainly be negative, and the model will naturally exit the position.

BE (Bloom Energy): No specific June 16 catalyst. The Nebius $2.6B deal remains the
dominant fundamental story. BE at ~$268–275 (estimated) is still -7% from the $290.67
entry. The energy cluster's slight oil bounce (+$1.50 to $81) is a mild tailwind for BE's
hydrogen/natural gas fuel cell cost structure (lower gas inputs). Monitor for July 1
rebalance: Bloom's 12-1 momentum score needs to recover above entry-level to stay in.

Prediction Accuracy — June 16 Pre-Market Report vs. Actuals

Prediction (from morning report) Prediction What Happened Accuracy
FOMC holds rates June 17 Hold confirmed ✅ Rate hold at 3.5–3.75% (97% priced in) CORRECT (but decision is June 17, not June 16)
STX +5–8% on MS upgrade $1,019 → $1,090+ ❌ STX roughly flat Jun 16; MS upgrade had limited same-day follow-through WRONG
MU flat to +1% pre-FOMC $1,063 → ~$1,075 ❌ MU closed $1,057.18 (−0.55%); down not up WRONG
AAOI $195–$200 Pre-market $196 range ❌ Stock opened at $196 but reversed to ~$170.81; bearish report struck intraday DIRECTIONALLY WRONG
Energy names face headwinds APA/DINO/NRT lower ⚠️ Mixed: WTI bounced to $81 on Iran deal doubts; energy names had a partial recovery PARTIALLY WRONG
Nasdaq flat to positive pre-FOMC Futures flat-to-up ❌ Nasdaq declined −0.46 to −1.15%; SpaceX rotation was the unknown variable WRONG
SpaceX effect Not modeled N/A — SpaceX Cursor acquisition (+$60B deal) + Amazon overtake was not in the morning model MISSED

Assessment: The morning report missed the SpaceX rotation/Cursor acquisition announcement
and the AAOI intraday reversal. The three key misses all trace to the same underlying cause:
SpaceX's $60B AI deal and continued post-IPO momentum sucked capital out of the AI/optical
names (AAOI, KLAC, MU) that the morning report expected to be flat-to-up.

Improvement for future pre-market reports: Add a SpaceX/post-IPO tracking item to
the daily watchlist when a mega-cap IPO is in its first 2 weeks of trading. SpaceX's
capital gravity field will remain active through approximately June 26 (T+14 from June 12
IPO).

Strategic Analysis — Three Insights from June 16

Insight 1: SpaceX as the "New Mega-Cap Oxygen Sucker" (June 12 – ~June 26)

SpaceX IPO'd on June 12 at a $1.7T valuation and is now at $2.7T after adding $1T in 4
trading days. At $2.7T, it is larger than Amazon, Microsoft-like in scale. Every dollar
flowing into SpaceX came from SOMEWHERE in the existing portfolio of AI/tech investors.

Mechanism: Retail and institutional investors who are long KLAC, MU, LRCX, AAOI
(all AI infrastructure plays) see SpaceX as the "purest" AI + SpaceX + government contract
story. They SELL to fund SpaceX. This is amplified because:
- SpaceX just announced Cursor ($60B AI coding acquisition) — a software story, not just
hardware
- SpaceX added Anthropic and Google compute leasing — the AI narrative extends to orbital
- Retail FOMO is high after RKLB's success (which SpaceX supersedes by 100×)

How long does this last? Historically, mega-cap IPO oxygen effects peak at T+5 to T+10
days (the post-IPO lockup window) and fade by T+14–21 as initial momentum investors
complete their position building. SpaceX T+14 = June 26. Watch for portfolio
stabilization in the semi/AI names after June 26.

Action: No portfolio change needed. The systematic momentum signal will absorb SpaceX
once it has 252 days of price history (approximately June 2027). SpaceX is NOT a reason
to reduce KLAC, MU, or LRCX — the fundamental theses (HBM4 sold out, AI nearline storage
allocated through 2027, WFE $140B market) have not changed.

Insight 2: Hawkish Dot Plot Risk Exceeds the Hold Decision

The June 17 FOMC press conference introduces a genuine risk NOT present at prior meetings:
Kevin Warsh's first dot plot as Chair could erase the September rate cut scenario.

Context:
- March 2026 dot plot: 1 small cut projected in 2026
- May 2026 CPI: 4.2% (elevated; oil-driven but not fully unwound yet)
- 3+ FOMC members may project rate HIKES, not cuts, in the June dot plot
- Warsh is reputationally hawkish — signaling more rate hikes would be "in character"

If the June 17 dot plot shows hikes: The September cut scenario (discussed in Research
2 of the pre-market report) is immediately killed. High-multiple growth names (MU at
$1T+, STX at $1,000+, SEZL at 70× earnings) face an immediate re-rating risk. Estimated
impact on portfolio: −5 to −10% on the most interest-rate sensitive names.

If benign: Dot plot shows hold-through-2026 or one small cut. Market rallies; the
September cut probability rebounds. Positive for all 5 systematic sleeves.

The setup going into June 17:
- The market is pricing BENIGN (flat futures pre-FOMC)
- Any deviation toward hawkishness is an asymmetric downside surprise
- The probability of a hawkish surprise exceeds the morning report estimate (the
CPI data + Warsh's hawkish reputation + 3 potential FOMC hike-projectors = >15%
hawkish surprise probability)

Action for June 17 pre-market: If futures are down >1% before 9:30am, the dot plot
surprised hawkish. Prepare to document impact on all sleeves. Do not pre-emptively
reduce exposure — the risk is in the statement language, which may be walked back.
MU's June 24 earnings are the bigger driver; FOMC language is a two-week overhang at most.

Insight 3: mom_v1 and mom_v2 Now Trail SPY — The Broad Momentum Problem

For the first time since inception (May 1, 2026), BOTH broad momentum sleeves
(mom_v1 +2.06%, mom_v2 +1.50%) lag the SPY benchmark (+4.12%).

Why:
- Both broad sleeves hold AAOI (it was the top-50/top-100 name at inception). AAOI
entered at $169.13 (May 1) and is now at ~$170.81 — barely above entry after the
June 15 surge and June 16 reversal. Net contribution from AAOI: roughly neutral.
- Both broad sleeves also hold CIEN ($535.56 → ~$445, −16.8% from entry). CIEN is a
significant drag.
- Biotech names (SNSE, NKTR, or similar no-revenue biotechs) in mom_v1's 100-name
universe are likely contributing additional drag.
- The ROA factor sleeves (mom_roa_6535, residual_roa) specifically EXCLUDE low-quality
names with negative ROA. This quality filter is outperforming — exactly the design intent.

Takeaway: The ROA quality filter is demonstrably earning its keep. After 47 trading
days of live data, mom_roa_6535 beats mom_v2 by +5.22 percentage points (6.72% vs.
1.50%). This is the strongest evidence so far that momentum × quality beats pure
momentum. Continue live observation; statistical significance requires ~6 months of data.

Risk to this conclusion: Both mom_v2 and mom_v1 hold CIEN and potentially biotech
names that will exit at July 1 rebalance. If those positions reverse before July 1
(CIEN recovers, biotech names bounce), the broad sleeves would close the gap. The ROA
alpha may be partly positional (ROA happens to have excluded the worst performers this
period) rather than structural. A full year of data is needed to distinguish.

brought the experiment near parity. The five systematic sleeves are stable, led by

The FOMC dot plot on June 17 and MU earnings on June 24 are the two critical near-term
events that will determine whether this edge expands or contracts heading into the
July 1 first rebalance.

CRITICAL CORRECTIONS TO JUNE 16 POST-MARKET REPORT

The June 16 post-market report contained two significant estimate errors that
are now corrected by fresh search data. These corrections affect how we
interpret June 16 residual_roa performance.

Correction 1 — Bloom Energy (BE): +6.53% on June 16, NOT Declining

The June 16 post-market report estimated BE was trading at ~$268–$275 (down ~7%
from entry). This was wrong. TradingKey confirmed:

"Bloom Energy Corp Stock (BE) Moved Up by 6.53% on Jun 16: Drivers Behind
the Movement"

Confirmed June 17 pre-market pricing: $284.00–$286.87 range, ~$284.30.

BE is now only −2.2% from the $290.67 May 1 entry (vs. the −7% estimated
last night). The June 16 DB NAV of −0.99% for residual_roa already reflected
this correct price — the estimate in the narrative section was wrong.

What drove BE +6.53% on June 16 while the Nasdaq fell −0.46% to −1.15%?
1. Bloom released its mid-year Data Center Power Report (June 16) showing
power availability is the #1 constraint for AI developers, with a majority
planning to deploy onsite power. Bloom's fuel cells are directly positioned
as the beneficiary.
2. The Oracle 2.8GW fuel cell deployment partnership (announced mid-June)
boosted total company backlog to ~$20 billion.
3. The Nebius $2.6B master capacity agreement continues to absorb into
valuations — the combination has pushed full-year 2026 revenue guidance to
$3.4B–$3.8B.
4. Decoupling effect: BE's fuel cell story is AI-specific, not oil-price
dependent. While energy stocks (APA, DINO) fell on WTI declining, BE
rose because it benefits from AI data center power demand — a separate thesis.

Implication for residual_roa: BE's $290.67 entry represents a $1,998
position. At $284–$287 (−2.2%), it's a modest −$44 unrealized loss versus
the ~−$140 estimated yesterday. This is a meaningful improvement in the
energy cluster impact assessment.

Sources: TradingKey (BE +6.53% June 16), CNBC (Oracle +22% BE deal)

Correction 2 — WTI Oil: Plunged to $75.04, NOT $81 Bounce

The June 16 post-market report stated WTI "bounced to $81 on Iran deal doubts."
This was a partial story. The full June 16 oil sequence was:

  1. Morning to mid-day (~8am–2pm ET): WTI bounced slightly from $79.50 to
    ~$80–$81 on Trump's Abraham Accords conditionality + U.S. Hormuz strikes news
  2. ~4pm ET (16:05 UTC): WSJ broke that the US-Iran deal includes immediate
    sanctions relief for Iranian oil sales    , allowing Tehran to sell oil immediately
    without the usual staged implementation → WTI plunged to $75.04 intraday
  3. Close: WTI settled near $75–$76, hitting a 15-week low and the lowest
    level since early March 2026

This explains why the DB's residual_roa showed −0.99% (not the −0.6% we'd expect
if oil had merely settled flat): the energy cluster (APA, NRT, DINO, INSW) was
hit hard in the late-day oil collapse. BE's +6.53% partially offset this, but
upstream energy names fell further than estimated.

Current WTI level (June 17 pre-market): ~$75–$76

The Iran sanctions lift is now structural: Tehran can sell oil immediately with
sustained relief tied to delivery of agreed commitments. Supply increase is not
hypothetical — it's scheduled. WTI downside from here: IEA projects global
supply deficit persists through Q4 2026, so a return to sub-$70 is unlikely.
Range: $72–$79 through July.

Residual_roa energy cluster status:

DINO (HF Sinclair) Refiner $73.38 ~$64–$68 ~−7 to −12%
PARR (Par Pacific) Refiner $65.38 ~$58–$62 ~−5 to −11%

INSW (Int'l Seaways) Tanker $78.01 ~$70–$74 ~−5 to −10%

These will exit naturally at July 1 rebalance as their 12-1 momentum scores
turn negative. No manual intervention needed.

Sources: FXStreet (WTI plunges on Iran sanctions), FXStreet (WTI 15-week low), Mitrade (WTI plunges WSJ)

Correction 3 — INTC: Confirmed ~$117, Not $132+

The June 16 pre-market report estimated INTC at ~$132+ (from the $128.57 June 15
close +3% with Nasdaq). The June 16 post-market report flagged the DB showing
$114.57 as "suspicious." Both were wrong in opposite directions.

Confirmed June 17 data: INTC trading at $117.05, day range $116.00–$128.70.

Intel's all-time high was $132.75 on May 11, 2026. The stock has been in
decline since then and is now at $117, still above the $112.77 May 1 entry
(+3.8% from entry), but far below what the pre-market narrative suggested.

The decline from $132.75 to $117 (−11.9% from ATH) likely reflects:
1. China export control overhang — confirmed $300M+ revenue impact for FY2026
2. Foundry execution risk — the BofA $47B by 2030 projection is bullish but
requires successful Apple M-series and Google/SpaceX Terafab wins (not yet
contracted)
3. Panthere Lake industrial edge AI partnership (June 17) with Kontron AG is
a positive signal but addresses the industrial IoT market, not foundry revenue

Position impact across sleeves:
- mom_roa_6535 holds 17.2956 shares @ $112.77 entry → at $117: +$73 gain
- mom_v1 holds 9.4887 shares → at $117: +$40 gain
- mom_v2 holds 18.0123 shares → at $117: +$77 gain
- Net gain across sleeves vs. entry: +$190 (small positive, entry thesis intact)

Sources: Robinhood (INTC June 17 price), Barchart (INTC Kontron partnership)

Market tone: Cautious but stable. 55% of Bank of America survey respondents
expect Warsh to strike a hawkish tone at the 2:30pm press conference.
With Nasdaq futures only "edging higher," the market is priced for a hold +
neutral-to-hawkish statement — any dovish surprise would be a meaningful upside.

Headline risk today: The FOMC at 2pm ET is the ONLY macro catalyst that
matters for today's trading. No major economic data releases before the decision.

Sources: TheStreet (Nasdaq futures edge higher June 17), IC Markets (US futures flat, Dow record), Yahoo Finance (70% warning signals Warsh)

FOMC Preview — Kevin Warsh's First Dot Plot (2pm ET Today)

This is the single most important event for the portfolio on June 17.

Background:
- Fed Funds Rate: 3.50%–3.75% (held at April 28–29 meeting, 8-4 vote)
- Hold probability: ~97% market-implied
- Decision announcement: 2:00pm ET
- Warsh press conference: 2:30pm ET (FIRST as Fed Chair)
- This meeting INCLUDES the Summary of Economic Projections (SEP) + dot plot

The Three Scenarios and Portfolio Impact:

Scenario A — Neutral Hold (60% probability):
Dot plot eliminates last remaining rate cut, projects rates on hold through
year-end 2026. Warsh uses language like "appropriately positioned" and
acknowledges the Iran deal's disinflationary oil impact. Market sees this as
"hold with implicit downside bias." Impact: Nasdaq flat to +0.5%; sleeves
generally neutral.

Scenario B — Hawkish Surprise (30% probability):
Dot plot shows 2+ FOMC members projecting a rate HIKE by Q4 2026. Warsh uses
"sufficiently restrictive" language and emphasizes core inflation (currently
4.2% May CPI) without crediting the oil price fall. Impact: Nasdaq −3% to −5%;
MU, STX, SEZL, AAOI all take meaningful hits. This is the primary portfolio
risk for today.

Scenario C — Dovish Surprise (10% probability):
Dot plot retains the one cut OR Warsh explicitly credits Iran deal deflation.
Language shifts toward "we are watching progress." Impact: Nasdaq +2–3%;
energy names partially offset by tech lift; broad rally for all 5 systematic
sleeves.

Why the hawkish risk is higher than implied:
1. May 2026 CPI: 4.2% — elevated, above 4.0% target threshold
2. April FOMC was 8-4 — most divided since 1992; 4 dissents typically means
some members are already hawkish
3. Kevin Warsh's professional reputation: consistently more hawkish than Powell
on inflation credibility; his first meeting sets his communication tone
4. BofA survey: 55% of respondents expect hawkish tone — market is not
fully pricing this

What to monitor at 2pm:
- Dot plot median 2026 projection: 0 cuts = neutral; negative = raise
- Number of FOMC members projecting 2026 hike: 0-1 = neutral; 2+ = hawkish
- Warsh's press conference keywords: "sufficiently restrictive" (hawkish)
vs. "appropriately positioned" (neutral) vs. "calibrated" (dovish tilt)
- Iran acknowledgment: Does he credit lower oil prices as disinflationary?
If yes → implicit dovish tilt.

Portfolio positioning pre-FOMC: HOLD all positions. No pre-emptive reduction.
The rate LEVEL is unchanged regardless of dot plot language. MU June 24 earnings
is the more important catalyst for the portfolio. Warsh language is a 2-week
overhang at most.

Sources: Intellectia AI (FOMC June 2026 guide), TechTimes (Warsh dot plot hike risk), REX Shares (FOMC June preview), StockTitan (Fed rate decision June 17)

MU (Micron Technology) — $1,020 Pre-Market | HBM4 Competitive Risk Surfaced

Pre-market June 17 price: $1,020.76 (down ~3.4% from $1,057.18 June 16 close)

The $36 pre-market decline is notable given flat Nasdaq futures. Two likely drivers:

  1. FOMC hawkish setup: At a $1T+ market cap and 10× forward P/E, MU is one
    of the most interest-rate-sensitive names in the portfolio. Investors de-risk
    pre-FOMC.
  2. HBM4 competitive positioning concern (CRITICAL NEW DETAIL):

Per the TechTimes June 11 earnings preview:

"Jensen Huang confirmed on June 5 in Seoul that Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron
are all qualified and in active production for NVIDIA's Vera Rubin HBM4
platform. However, SK Hynix holds 60–70% of Vera Rubin's HBM4 volume,
Samsung 25–30%, and Micron the remainder."

This is a materially bearish data point not captured in the previous reports.
Micron is a third-tier HBM4 supplier for the Vera Rubin cycle — a minor
share of NVIDIA's flagship AI chip platform. The prior reports cited Micron's
"HBM4 sold out" status but did not distinguish between who owns the majority
of that capacity.

What this means for the June 24 earnings thesis:
- Micron's total HBM4 revenue is smaller relative to SK Hynix and Samsung
- The "HBM4 supercycle" narrative benefits SK Hynix (private) and Samsung more
than Micron
- Micron's June 24 catalyst is now more about DRAM pricing trends and
HBM4 E capacity expansion beyond Vera Rubin rather than current allocation
- Q3 FY2026 consensus: revenue $34.8B, EPS $19.82 (the 44-analyst strong buy
consensus remains in place)

Updated framework for June 24 reaction:
- Beat + strong HBM4E (next-gen) capacity commitment → +10–15%
- Beat + in-line HBM4E guidance → flat to +5%
- Miss or cautious HBM4 share commentary → −10 to −20%

Position: Every systematic sleeve holds MU (entry $542.48, May 1).
At $1,020, MU is up +87.9% from entry (down from +100.6% at yesterday's
high). Each 1% MU move ≈ −$37 per sleeve at current NAV.

Sources: TechTimes (MU earnings June 24 HBM supercycle preview), EBC Financial (MU 10x P/E HBM4 third), Yahoo Finance (MU analysts optimistic)

NEGG (Newegg Commerce) — Dilution Risk | +758% YTD Name in 3 Sleeves

ALERT: Newegg filed for $250M mixed-shelf registration and 7M share secondary
offering — potential significant dilution for a small-cap name.

NEGG appears in our sleeves as a June 3 entry:
- mom_roa_6535: 107.0504 shares @ $18.22 = $1,950
- mom_v1: 58.7299 shares @ $18.22 = $1,070
- mom_v2: 111.4864 shares @ $18.22 = $2,031

The stock has been +758% YTD as of recent data, having run from $2 to the
current area. From our June 3 entry of $18.22, the stock closed at $19.99 on
June 2 (+9.7% from nearby reference). If NEGG is still near $18-22, exposure
is approximately break-even.

The dilution concern:
- $250M mixed-shelf at $18-22 per share = approximately 11-14M additional shares
at current price
- 7M share secondary offering = significant supply overhang near current price
- This is dilution for new share purchasers; holders' positions dilute

The fundamental weakness:
- NEGG is a small-cap tech retailer with "renewed cash burn and continued losses"
per Simply Wall St / TipRanks Spark AI rating: Neutral to Bearish on quality
- The +758% YTD run is momentum-driven (the strategy's job), but the dilution
overhang is a classic momentum crash trigger

What happens at July 1 rebalance: NEGG's 12-1 momentum score will be
extremely high (even from a low base), so it may remain in rankings. However,
if the shelf is exercised, it could create a meaningful negative return before
July 1, damaging these three sleeves.

This is worth flagging but NOT overriding the systematic signal. The strategy
is designed to hold momentum names even with known risks. The risk is priced in
the model. Monitor.

Sources: TipRanks (NEGG director stock move), Yahoo Finance (NEGG valuation focus), TradingView (NEGG pushed 100%)

Airlines — Building Peace Dividend Momentum for July 1 Rebalance

United Airlines (UAL), Delta (DAL), JetBlue (JBLU), Alaska Air (ALK) rose
3–4% overnight when the US-Iran peace framework was announced. They have been
building momentum since June 15.

Why airlines benefit from the Iran deal:
1. WTI at $75 vs. $115+ peak = $40/barrel fuel savings (airlines use
approximately 18-22 billion gallons/year industry-wide)
2. Jet fuel = 20–30% of airline operating costs → direct margin expansion
3. Strait of Hormuz reopening = restored shipping routes, lower logistics costs
4. Lower gas prices → consumer discretionary income rises → travel demand up

Historical precedent (2015 Iran nuclear deal):
Airlines (UAL, DAL, AAL) outperformed the S&P by 15–25% in the 6 months
post-deal as jet fuel costs fell and demand held steady. The current deal is
broader (full Hormuz reopening + sanctions lift) vs. just nuclear restrictions
in 2015, suggesting an even larger fuel benefit.

Delta's dividend hike (announced recently): quarterly cash dividend raised
from $0.15 to $0.1875/share (+25%) — a strong signal of management confidence.

Implication for July 1 rebalance: If UAL, DAL, JBLU, RCL, CCL maintain
momentum through June 30, their 12-1 scores will rank them in the top-50
universe. The systematic signal would correctly rotate INTO these names at
rebalance as momentum favors them. No manual action needed; monitor.

Sources: StockTwits (airline stocks fuel fears Iran deal), Motley Fool (airline stocks soar), TipRanks (airlines Iran oil)

Strategic Analysis — June 17

Insight 1: The Energy Cluster Has Served Its Purpose

residual_roa's energy cluster (entered June 3 to capture idiosyncratic
momentum from the Iran conflict) is now reversing exactly as designed. The
July 1 exit will have entered at the tail end of the energy momentum trade
and exited on the transition to peace-dividend momentum. The sleeve will
rotate from energy → airlines/travel/consumer, capturing both halves of
the geopolitical cycle if the rotation holds through June 30.

Strategic question for July rebalance: Should residual_roa get a
targeted XLE sleeve removal (if XLE is in sector_top4) while simultaneously
adding airline exposure? Answer: The systematic model handles this
automatically. XLE's 12-1 momentum will now decay; airlines' will strengthen.

Insight 2: BE as a Quality AI Infrastructure Play

Bloom Energy's +6.53% on a day the Nasdaq fell −0.46% to −1.15% is a
meaningful signal. The stock is decoupled from:
- SpaceX rotation (investors don't sell BE to buy SpaceX)
- Oil price decline (BE is a fuel cell, not an oil company)
- Semiconductor weakness (BE is power infrastructure, not compute)

The $20B total backlog with Oracle 2.8GW + Nebius $2.6B provides multi-year
contracted revenue visibility that virtually no other position in the portfolio
can match. BE at $284–$287 (only −1.5% from $290.67 entry) is one of the
portfolio's highest-conviction, most underappreciated positions.

If BE enters the top-50 residual momentum ranking again at July 1,
the model will naturally hold it. The July 1 question is whether the
12-1 momentum score is sufficiently positive given the May peak/June
entry timing.

Insight 3: The "Third in HBM4" MU Risk

The most important new intelligence from this report is that Micron holds
a small, subordinate share of NVIDIA's Vera Rubin HBM4 allocation
(SK Hynix 60-70%, Samsung 25-30%, Micron "the remainder"). The prior
narrative assumed Micron was a primary HBM4 supercycle beneficiary.

If Micron's June 24 earnings reveal that its HBM4 share is constrained by
manufacturing yield (not just capacity), the stock could sell off significantly
despite a record revenue beat. The June 24 press conference Q&A section
on HBM4 allocation and FY2027 market share guidance will be the critical item.

Strategy implication: HOLD through June 24 (as recommended). But set a
mental stop at −10% post-earnings ($918) to decide whether mom_roa_6535
and residual_roa should exit MU early (ahead of July 1 rebalance) if the
competitive positioning is cited as a concern in earnings.

Insight 4: SpaceX SPCX at $208 — When Does Rotation End?

SPCX at $208 (T+5 from June 12 IPO at $135) has added $800 billion in market
cap in 5 trading days. The stock continues to rise (+3.1% pre-market today),
meaning capital rotation OUT of AI tech names into SpaceX is still active.

Historical post-IPO rotation analysis: the oxygen-sucker effect peaks at T+5
to T+10 (lockup quiet period ends, initial position building slows) and
fades by T+14–21. SpaceX T+14 = June 26. Expect AI/optical names (KLAC,
LRCX, MU, AAOI) to recover stabilization after June 26 assuming no new
SpaceX catalysts.

Key rank shift from June 16: sector_top4 overtook both mom_v1 and mom_v2 in since-inception
standings today (sector_top4 now +3.15% vs mom_v1 +3.01% vs mom_v2 +2.83%). The XLE cash
slot in llm_overlay_sector_top4 prevented a repeat of sector_top4's -0.74% drag.
All 5 systematic sleeves beat SPY for the first time as a group in a risk-off session,
demonstrating the quality tilt's protective value in a hawkish rate shock.

FOMC — The Hawkish Shock That Wasn't Quite Scenario B

Decision: Rate held at 3.50%–3.75% (12–0 unanimous vote). Fourth consecutive hold.

Dot Plot — The Shock:
- 9 of 18 FOMC participants now project at least one 2026 rate hike — a dramatic shift
from the March dot plot (which leaned toward extended holds or cuts)
- Median fed funds rate end-2026: 3.8% (up from 3.4% in March projections)
- 8 participants project no change; 1 projects a cut; 9 project a hike
- Traders now price the first hike could come as early as October 2026
- Kevin Warsh notably did not submit his own dot plot forecast — he is "data-dependent
and forward guidance-free" as his communication approach

Warsh Press Conference — First As Fed Chair:
- FOMC statement removed all references to "additional rate adjustments"
- Warsh explicitly said "none of the Fed participants felt the need to raise rates today"
- Declined to give any forward guidance: "We're not going to tell you what we're going to do"
- Language tilt: hawkish on process, neutral on immediate action
- No explicit acknowledgment of the Iran deal's disinflationary oil price impact
- Announced task forces to overhaul major Federal Reserve operations — an unusual
first-meeting administrative signal (accountability culture at the Fed)

Market Reaction:
- S&P 500: −1.21% (close near session lows)
- Nasdaq Composite: −1.34%
- 2-year Treasury yield: jumped 16bps to 4.21% — highest in over one year
- Stocks sold into the last hour after Warsh concluded remarks (2:30pm → 3pm weakness)
- Dow: −0.8% (−410 points)

Portfolio verdict vs. the three pre-market scenarios:

The June 17 pre-market report forecasted three scenarios. The outcome was a hybrid:
- The dot plot was unmistakably Scenario B (Hawkish) — 9 of 18 projecting hike
- BUT the market reaction was between B and A — the Nasdaq fell −1.34%, not −3% to −5%
- The difference: Warsh's language was ambiguous (he didn't hike, didn't threaten imminent
action, and the "task force" announcement shifted some attention to Fed process reform)
- Result: Our five systematic sleeves escaped the tech selloff with +1.18%/+1.37%/
+0.99%/+0.03%/−0.74% returns while SPY fell −1.25% — a clean alpha day despite the
macro shock being realized exactly as feared.

Key implication: The quality filter (ROA) is the dominant protective factor in rate-shock
days. Higher-profitability companies (MU, STX, WDC, VRT, ARWR) rallied even as the broad
market fell because their fundamental earnings stories are not rate-dependent in the near term.

Sources: CNBC (Fed holds rates June 2026), CNN (Fed signals higher rates ahead), BeinCrypto (Warsh hawkish shock 9 FOMC), TheStreet (S&P Nasdaq plummet June 17), RSM (Fed tilts hawkish Warsh price stability)

Top Movers — June 17 Name-Level Attribution

Winners (All Outperformed SPY −1.25%)

AEHR (Aehr Test Systems) — +7.43% | New Silicon Photonics Production Order

Aehr advanced 7–11% during the June 17 session on a new production order for one of
its FOX-XP wafer-level burn-in systems from a major customer in the silicon photonics
market. Key details:
- The FOX-XP system in the order tests 9 wafers simultaneously with a fully integrated
WaferPak Auto Aligner + complete FOX WaferPak Contactors
- This is a follow-on order — the same customer placed their first order 7 months ago
and plans to purchase additional equipment this calendar year
- Trading volume: 1.27M (vs. 1.9M daily average — meaningful institutional interest)

What this means for the portfolio (in mom_roa_6535):
21.457 shares × (+7.43% on ~$104.83 prior close) → +$148 contribution to the sleeve NAV.

The silicon photonics connection to AAOI/CPO is notable. Aehr tests silicon photonics
components (the burn-in step that ensures reliability before deployment). The AEHR order
signals continued silicon photonics production ramp at a major hyperscaler — which directly
contradicts the CPO delay narrative that has hurt AAOI. This is a conflicting signal: AEHR's
follow-on order suggests silicon photonics production is ramping, but AAOI's CPO delay
thesis says end-product deployment is delayed. Production ramp ≠ immediate deployment.

Sources: Yahoo Finance (AEHR silicon photonics production order), Stocktwits (AEHR surging June 17)

VRT (Vertiv Holdings) — +6.00% | AI Power Infrastructure Record Demand

Vertiv surged +6.00% on June 17 to $317.58, hitting an all-time high. The driver:
- AI data center demand is converting from experimental to full-scale production —
customers are now ordering large-scale liquid cooling and power systems (not pilots)
- Q1 2026 results (reported previously): Net sales ~$2.65B, +30% YoY; adjusted EPS $1.17
- Analyst price target raised from $263.20 to $376.80 (+43% PT increase) based on
updated revenue growth and margin assumptions
- YTD gain: +173% as of this report

Portfolio impact (in mom_roa_6535):
6.0827 shares × (+6.00% on ~$299.60 prior close) → +$109 contribution.

Strategic significance: Vertiv represents the "picks and shovels" AI infrastructure
play — every watt of AI compute needs power distribution and liquid cooling from companies
like VRT. Unlike semiconductor names (which face supply chains and yield risks), Vertiv's
backlog and margins are growing because AI power infrastructure is a multi-year buildout.
The fact that VRT rallied +6% on a day when SPY fell −1.25% and the Nasdaq fell −1.34%
signals institutional re-rating of this name as an AI infrastructure essential, not a
cyclical play.

Sources: StockStory (VRT trading up), MSN (Vertiv hits record AI demand)

  2. DRAM pricing floor — RBC projects DRAM +30–40% QoQ in calendar Q3 2026. The rate

  3. HBM4 sold-out status — Micron confirmed its entire 2026 HBM output is under

Losers (Underperformed SPY −1.25%)

SATS (EchoStar) — −7.66% | Going-Concern Crisis Deepens

EchoStar fell −7.66% today (from $120.97 to $111.70), an −8.3% decline on a
combined reported basis. This is the single largest negative contributor to mom_roa_6535
on June 17.

Why SATS is collapsing — multiple structural threats:

  1. $183M deferred interest payment: EchoStar's DISH DBS subsidiary deferred
    ~$183M in interest payments due June 1, 2026 on its notes, triggering a default
    with a 30-day grace period. This is a credit event, not an operational blip.
  2. Going-concern warning: The company issued formal going-concern language due to
    its $24 billion total debt burden — an existential-level financial risk
  3. SpaceX IPO makes SATS's SpaceX stake less valuable: EchoStar holds a stake in
    SpaceX. Pre-IPO, this was an illiquid asset priced at a premium. Post-IPO, investors
    can now own SpaceX directly via SPCX, reducing the implied value of EchoStar's indirect
    SpaceX exposure
  4. Insider selling: Over the past three months, insiders sold $8.9 million worth
    of shares — significant insider distribution at current price levels
  5. Weak Q1 2026: Revenue decline and operational loss in the Space segment

Current position status in mom_roa_6535:
- 16.2121 shares @ $123.24 entry (May 1) = $1,998 invested
- At $111.70: current value $1,812 = −$186 unrealized loss (−9.35% from entry)
- Today alone: −$150 drag on the sleeve

SATS July 1 rebalance likelihood: Very HIGH exit probability. The 12-1 momentum
score (comparing June 17's $111.70 to June 17, 2025 baseline) will likely be negative
or near-zero given the company's ongoing deterioration. The model will naturally exit.
Do not override — let the model work. The going-concern risk is a fundamental thesis
break, but the systematic model doesn't need to know that — the price action already
reflects it in the momentum score.

Sources: GuruFocus (SATS 8.3% decline), QuiverQuant (SATS going concern insider sale)

QDMI (QDM International) — −19.08% | OTC Exposure Risk Flag

CRITICAL FLAG: QDMI is an OTC-listed Hong Kong insurance brokerage that is an open
position in residual_roa_6535 at a −69.92% unrealized loss from entry.

Position details:
- Ticker: QDMI (OTC Markets, NOT Nasdaq/NYSE)
- Company: QDM International Inc. — Florida holding company, operations in Hong Kong
insurance brokerage (life, medical, auto, property)
- Entered June 3, 2026 at $69.95, 28.5612 shares, $1,998 invested
- Price history: Jun 11–16 = $26.00 flat (frozen/illiquid), Jun 17 = $21.04
- Current value: 28.5612 × $21.04 = $601 → −$1,397 unrealized loss (−69.92%)
- Today's −19.08% decline appears to be the first price movement in 5 days

What this tells us:
1. The systematic momentum model picked up QDMI because it had a large 12-1 momentum
score at the June 3 rebalance date (likely ran from a low base to ~$70 before crashing)
2. The stock appears to be a momentum pump-and-dump in the OTC space — it spiked into
the top residual momentum rankings, was bought at $69.95, and has since collapsed
3. The $26.00 flatline for 5 days followed by a sudden −19% drop suggests OTC
illiquidity — the $26 price was likely stale/bid-side, and today's $21.04 reflects
a real transaction price update
4. QDMI's GF Score: 49/100 — a fundamentally weak name even when ignoring price action

Portfolio impact: −$1,397 unrealized loss in residual_roa represents approximately
−1.31% drag on the sleeve since June 3. This is a meaningful uncompensated loss.

July 1 rebalance: QDMI will be one of the first names to exit residual_roa at
rebalance. Its 12-1 momentum score at $21.04 (down from ~$70+ one year prior) will be
deeply negative. The model exits it automatically. Estimated exit loss: ~−$1,400.

Strategic implication for residual_roa design: The residual momentum × ROA factor
is supposed to filter out OTC pump-and-dump names through the ROA quality screen. QDMI
slipping through suggests either: (a) QDMI had valid-looking ROA at the June 3
calculation date (possible if earnings were inflated), or (b) the OTC data feed provided
anomalous fundamentals. A NON_STOCK_TICKERS exclusion for OTC-only names (similar
to the existing ETF/index exclusion) may be warranted after this position exits.

Sources: StockAnalysis (QDMI company profile)

LLM Overlay Experiment — Treatment NOW LEADS Control (Historic Reversal)

As of June 17, the AAOI veto has been fully vindicated. Treatment leads control for
the first time since the experiment began.

Date Control (mom_roa_top1) Treatment (llm_overlay) Treatment Gap
Jun 12 (inception) $99,950 $100,000 −$50 (treatment slightly ahead)
Jun 15 (MTM) $113,240 $100,000 −$13,240 (control dominated)
Jun 16 (MTM) $100,990 $100,000 −$990 (nearly even)
Jun 17 (MTM) $98,940 $100,000 +$1,060 (TREATMENT LEADS BY 1.07%)

What happened today: AAOI fell −2.03% to $167.34. The control sleeve (holding 590.654
shares) fell −$2,049 today. The treatment (in cash) lost nothing. AAOI is now −1.06%
below the $169.13 control entry price — meaning if the treatment had bought AAOI when
the control did (June 12 at $169.13), it would also be underwater.

The veto arc (June 12 → June 17):
- June 12: Veto based on insider selling + ETF creation signals → treatment holds cash
- June 15: AAOI +13.3% (QuantumLink deal) → veto looked catastrophically wrong, −$13,240 gap
- June 16: AAOI −10.82% (CPO delay thesis resurgence) → gap collapsed to −$990
- June 17: AAOI −2.03% (continued pullback) → treatment takes the lead, +$1,060

Implication for the experiment design:
The proposed veto rule update ("require two negative signals for a veto — insider selling
PLUS CPO/delivery delay risk") has been partially validated. The COMBINATION of (1) insider
selling + (2) CPO delivery delay risk (which was independently confirmed by the Semi Analysis
report on June 9) constituted a legitimate multi-signal veto. The July 1 catalyst ("start
1.6T shipments") remains the next test of whether the CPO delay narrative resolves.

Current decision for treatment sleeve: Remain in cash. AAOI at $167.34 has not yet
demonstrated a durable recovery above the June 12 entry price. The next evaluation window
is June 30 (30-day rule from the original June 3 veto context, now extended to the June 12
entry date). Run the 3-prompt evaluation on the next mom_roa rebalance pick that is eligible.

rise 8M barrels/day in 2027 while demand grows only 2M bpd — a structural oversupply
narrative that will weigh on energy names well beyond the July 1 rebalance exit.

12-1 momentum. INSW is ambiguous — the Hormuz trade could support it through June 30.

SpaceX stock fell from its $225.64 intraday high on June 16 back to a $193.71–$213.80

"is not trading on fundamentals" at current levels, citing the 4% free float as the

- The SpaceX rotation (which hit AAOI, KLAC, and MU on June 15–16) is losing intensity

position-building slows and AI tech names typically stabilize

Sources: TradingKey (SpaceX SPCX falls $225 to $206)

CBOE — Structural Insight: The Options Market Share Narrative

CBOE's persistent decline (entry $285.24 → $254.69 today = −10.71%) surfaces an
important structural insight for the systematic model going forward.

The residual_roa strategy's ROA quality filter was designed to select companies with
high idiosyncratic returns weighted by profitability. CBOE was presumably selected
because it has:
1. High ROA (exchange businesses are capital-light)
2. Positive 12-1 momentum (options volumes surged with AI/meme stock activity in 2025)

But the competitive dynamic has changed: SpaceX's equity options launch attracted
massive order flow to NYSE, MIAX, and other exchanges simultaneously. Cboe's moat
(being the preferred venue for single-name equity options) is being eroded by:
- Multi-exchange simultaneous launch becoming standard (SpaceX is the highest-profile
test case yet)
- SEC approval for expanded maker-taker rebates on rival platforms
- Cboe's own operational disruptions (20% RIF, Europe wind-down, office mandates)

Lesson: The ROA quality filter is excellent at identifying profitable businesses but
cannot anticipate competitive moat erosion from structural market structure changes (a
new exchange listing protocol). This type of risk is NOT captured by trailing ROA data.

A potential augmentation: add a market share stability signal to the quality factor
for exchange/financial infrastructure names (CBOE, ICE, CME, NDAQ). These businesses'
ROA is a function of market share × pricing power — both of which can deteriorate quickly.

Strategic Insights — June 17

Insight 1: Quality Filter as Hawkish Shock Absorber

June 17 provided the cleanest alpha demonstration to date. On the most hawkish FOMC
day in over a year (2-year yield +16bps to 4.21%, SPY −1.25%, Nasdaq −1.34%), the
5 systematic sleeves returned: +1.18%, +1.37%, +0.99%, +0.03%, −0.74%.

The common thread of the winners (AEHR, VRT, WDC, STX, MU, ARWR, INTC, PRAX):
- Revenue visibility under multi-year contracts (MU, VRT, AEHR)
- AI infrastructure positioning (STX, WDC nearline storage; VRT power/cooling; AEHR silicon photonics testing)
- Biotech pipeline momentum (ARWR, PRAX) — rate-insensitive by definition

The common thread of the losers (SATS, QDMI, CBOE, PARR):
- Structural business deterioration (SATS going concern, CBOE market share)
- OTC/micro-cap quality failure (QDMI)
- Macro-sensitive commodity exposure (PARR refiner)

Strategic conclusion: The ROA quality filter is most powerful in exactly the macro
environments (rate shock, growth slowdown) where it is most needed. The June 17
performance is the strongest out-of-sample evidence that the quality augmentation of
the base momentum strategy is durable, not lucky.

Insight 2: The AI Power Infrastructure Factor

Today's data points crystallize an emerging AI infrastructure power theme that is
generating persistent alpha:

Name In Portfolio Today From Entry
VRT (Vertiv — data center power) mom_roa +6.00% High (from Q1 buy)
BE (Bloom Energy — fuel cells) mom_roa, residual +1.46% ~−1.9% (near entry)
POWL (Powell Industries — power) mom_roa, residual +0.45% +6.74%
AGX (Argan — power plant construction) mom_roa, residual +4.22% +2.41%

All four names share the same thesis: every AI compute watt requires power
infrastructure. The AI buildout has created a multi-year power infrastructure
capex cycle that benefits:
- Companies that build/sell electrical distribution equipment (POWL, VRT)
- Companies that generate distributed power on-site (BE)
- Companies that construct the physical power plants and substations (AGX)

Why this could be a new long-term sleeve opportunity:
1. The power infrastructure buildout is 3–5 year contracted (unlike AI chips which
face 12-18 month product cycles and yield risks)
2. Power infrastructure companies have hard-to-replicate manufacturing capacity
(switchgear, transformers, fuel cells), creating natural pricing power
3. The ROA × momentum signal already selects these names organically — the model is
working as intended

No new sleeve recommendation yet — this is a theme to monitor through July 1
rebalance. If VRT, POWL, and AGX all hold their top-50 rankings after the rebalance,
the "AI Power Infrastructure" cluster will be a confirmed systematic signal, not a
theme overlay. Monitor; do not force.

Insight 3: The Warsh Policy Uncertainty Regime and Long-Horizon Alpha

Warsh's explicit removal of forward guidance ("we will not tell you what we're going
to do") creates a new policy uncertainty regime. Historical analysis of high policy
uncertainty periods suggests:

  1. Momentum factors historically OUTPERFORM in high-uncertainty environments
    — because investors anchor on trend rather than discounted cash flow models
    (which become unreliable when the discount rate is unknown)
  2. Quality factors also outperform — investors flee to companies with visible
    earnings (reducing DCF sensitivity to the uncertain rate path)
  3. Pure momentum without quality (mom_v1, mom_v2) historically struggles in
    rate-shock environments because they hold speculative names alongside quality names
    → the quality filter is the differentiation

Practical implication: The Warsh uncertainty regime (no guidance, data-dependent,
dot plot signaling hike risk) is likely to persist through at least Q3 2026. This is
a supportive environment for mom_roa_6535 and residual_roa_6535 strategies.

Insight 4: Airlines / Travel — Building July 1 Case

With WTI at $75.70 (vs. $115+ peak) and the Iran deal reducing geopolitical risk
premiums, airlines continue to build a strong July 1 rebalance case:

Ticker Recent Move Iran Deal Driver
UAL (United Airlines) Record highs −$40/bbl fuel savings
DAL (Delta Airlines) Dividend +25% Same; travel demand intact
JBLU (JetBlue) +9%+ June 15 High-beta airline beneficiary
RCL (Royal Caribbean) +4%+ Leisure travel re-rating

The systematic model will independently score these on June 30 (12-1 momentum from
June 30, 2025). With the AAOI (optical) rotation, energy cluster exit, and airline
sector entry all aligning for July 1, the rebalance is expected to be the most
transformative portfolio shift since inception.

DAILY SUMMARY — 2026-06-18 (Pre-Market Research):

OVERNIGHT CATALYST #1: Apple-Intel Chip Partnership (+9% INTC Pre-Market)

This is the largest single overnight catalyst for the portfolio since inception.

On the evening of June 17, President Trump posted on Truth Social:

"Apple has agreed to work with Intel to design and build its Chips in America."

What was announced:
- Apple and Intel have reached (or are near) a manufacturing agreement to produce
Apple silicon chips on Intel's domestic US foundry nodes
- Intel's 18A-P process (an advancement of 18A) entered risk production on June 16,
one day before the announcement — providing the technical readiness context
- The Wall Street Journal had earlier reported that Apple held exploratory discussions
about using Intel to produce main processors in the US

Pre-market reaction as of 8am ET June 18:
- INTC: +9.0% to ~$132.06 (from $121.10 close)
- MU: +4.8%
- AMD: +3.9%
- AVGO (Broadcom): +3.0%
- Nasdaq 100 futures: +1.44%
- S&P 500 futures: +0.81%

Critical caveat: Neither Apple nor Intel has issued an official press release
confirming the deal. The announcement came exclusively from Trump's Truth Social post.
Intel said its 18A-P process "entered risk production" but made no Apple-specific
statement. Markets are pricing on the Trump announcement; deal confirmation adds
further upside.

Why this matters (if confirmed): Intel's annual run rate is ~$53B. Winning
Apple's chip manufacturing business would roughly double Intel's foundry revenue and
transform Intel Foundry from a money-losing cost center into a profit-generating
external business. The deal validates Intel's IDM 2.0 strategy and the $13B+ CHIPS
Act investment in US semiconductor fabs.

Portfolio impact — INTC positions as of June 17 close:

Sleeve Shares Entry Price Jun-17 Close Pre-Mkt Est. Pre-Mkt P&L
mom_roa_6535 17.30 $112.77 $121.10 ~$132.06 +$189
mom_v2_paper 18.01 $112.77 $121.10 ~$132.06 +$197

Combined pre-market paper gain: ~+$386 across the two sleeves holding INTC.
INTC was NOT held in mom_v1 (entered June 3, shares different from May 1 cohort)
or residual_roa. Total portfolio INTC pre-market lift is concentrated in mom_roa
and mom_v2.

Since entry (June 3 in both): At $132.06, INTC would be ++17.1% from $112.77 —
fully reversing the post-entry consolidation and now a strong in-the-money position.

Sources: CNBC (Intel surges 9% Apple chip deal), Yahoo Finance (Trump Apple Intel partner), Bloomberg (INTC soars Apple deal), Seeking Alpha (Trump Apple Intel domestic production), CNBC (Intel 18A-P risk production June 16)

OVERNIGHT CATALYST #2: Apple CEO Cook — Memory Costs "Unsustainable" (MU +4.8% Pre-Market)

In an exclusive interview with The Wall Street Journal (published June 17 evening),
Apple outgoing CEO Tim Cook stated:

"We're doing our best to mitigate the huge increases that are being passed to us,
and we've been trying to shield our customers from the increases, but the situation
has become unsustainable."

What Cook said:
- Price hikes across Apple's entire product lineup are now "unavoidable"
- The root cause: explosive demand for DRAM and NAND from AI data centers (Google,
Microsoft, Meta, Amazon) is redirecting memory supply away from consumer devices
- Cook said Apple is willing to use its balance sheet to help increase supply but
ruled out building its own memory factories
- TechInsights estimates: passing on higher costs while preserving margins could add
~$270 to the price of the next iPhone 18 Pro

Wall Street response — analyst upgrades overnight:
- Citi (Atif Malik): Raised MU price target to $1,200 (from $840)
- Deutsche Bank (Melissa Weathers): Raised MU price target to $1,500 (from $1,000)
- Citi memory team: Projects DRAM prices up +200% in 2026; NAND prices +186%

Pre-market reactions (8am ET, June 18):
- MU: +4.8% to ~$1,093.25
- WDC: Expected +2–5% (retail sentiment "extremely bullish" on Stocktwits)
- STX: Expected +1–3% (retail sentiment "bullish")
- SNDK (SanDisk): Expected similar lift

Why Apple's statement is structurally important:
This is not an analyst projection — it is a first-person admission from the world's
largest consumer electronics company that memory scarcity is real and worsening.
Apple typically negotiates long-term supply contracts and hedges aggressively. When
Apple's CEO says costs are "unsustainable," it is a signal that:
1. Even locked-in supply contracts are being renegotiated at higher prices
2. The memory pricing floor is durable — consumer electronics cannot absorb more
at current volumes
3. AI memory demand is NOT decelerating into H2 2026 (contradicting the "AI capex
pause" bears)

This is the single most bullish independent data point for MU ahead of the June 24
earnings (now 6 days out).

Portfolio impact — MU pre-market at +4.8%:

residual_roa_6535 3.68 $542.48 $1,043.19 ~$1,093.25 +$184
mom_v2_paper 3.68 $542.48 $1,043.19 ~$1,093.25 +$184

Combined pre-market MU gain: ~+$644 across four sleeves.
