Macro Events: June 12–18 in Detail

June 13–14 (Weekend): US-Iran MOU Signed

Over the weekend of June 13–14, the United States and Iran signed a 14-point Memorandum of Understanding that formally ended the US-Iran war. The deal required Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and dismantle its nuclear enrichment program in exchange for lifting oil sanctions and the naval blockade. President Trump and Iranian President Pezeshkian signed the agreement; Trump described it as "the deal that ends all deals."

Oil market response: Brent crude settled below $86.50 on June 13 (lowest since early March), and WTI fell toward $81. By June 17 when the US Navy formally lifted the blockade, three Saudi tankers carrying ~6 million barrels transited Hormuz overnight. Goldman Sachs estimated Iranian supply could return 3–4 million barrels/day within 90 days if the deal holds.

Portfolio impact: Energy names that had benefited from the Iran geopolitical premium began unwinding. XLE fell from $57.55 (Jun 12) to $53.77 (Jun 18), a −6.6% week. residual_roa's energy cluster (APA, DINO, PARR, NRT, PBR, INSW, TEN, FTI) was the primary headwind on that sleeve — the main reason it lagged the momentum sleeves this week despite holding many of the same tech names.

Note: Israel condemned the deal as a "deep disappointment" and Netanyahu stated continued concern over Iran's nuclear ambitions, but did not militarily intervene during the period.

Sources: CNBC Iran oil prices Jun 12, Axios oil falls Iran deal, CNBC US Navy blockade ends

June 15 (Monday): Storage Supercycle Ignites — WDC +16%, STX +5%

Monday was the most important single day for the portfolio this week. Morgan Stanley published a comprehensive AI-HDD sector upgrade note:

  • WDC target raised to $650 from $488 (+33% hike). Stock jumped to a new all-time high near $701. Thesis: Western Digital has sold out its entire 2026 HDD production to AI data centers. HDD demand is growing 40–50% annually vs. supply growth of only 30–35%. Morgan Stanley projects HDD prices rising to $25–30/terabyte by 2027–2028.
  • STX target raised to $1,035 from $767. Seagate Q3 FY2026 revenue: $3.11 billion, +44% YoY; data center 80% of total revenue. STX hit $1,018 on June 15.
  • JPMorgan, Mizuho, and Citi also raised WDC targets simultaneously — the rarest kind of consensus upgrade (4 major banks in 24 hours).

Why this matters: WDC entry price in our books is $431.74 (May 1 rebalance). At $746.23 (Jun 18), that's +72.8% gain from entry in 49 days. STX entry $727.29; current $1,070.23 = +47.2% from entry. Both are held across mom_roa, mom_v1, mom_v2, and residual_roa — four sleeves benefiting simultaneously. Combined portfolio WDC + STX exposure across all sleeves is roughly $30,000+ at cost, now worth $55,000+ at market.

Source: Morgan Stanley WDC upgrade Invezz, STX vs WDC TipRanks

June 16 (Tuesday): Dow Record, Nasdaq Backslide — FOMC Fear

The Dow Jones (+0.74%) hit a record high, but Nasdaq (−0.81%) and S&P 500 (−0.40%) retreated as investors de-risked ahead of the Federal Reserve's two-day meeting. The split (Dow up, Nasdaq down) reflects rotation from growth/tech into value/industrials on rate-hike anxiety.

Portfolio impact: Most momentum names pulled back June 16 as tech faded. MU fell from $1,087 to $1,020 (−6.2%), INTC from $127.86 to $117.05 (−8.4%), COHR from $413.84 to $382.81 (−7.5%). BE was the exception (+2.3%, continuing its power-demand rally).

Source: TheStreet Jun 16

June 17 (Wednesday): FOMC Hold, Hawkish Dot Plot

The Federal Reserve held rates steady at 3.50–3.75% (universally expected — 97% pre-meeting probability). The surprise was the Summary of Economic Projections (dot plot):

  • Median rate end-2026: 3.8% (up from 3.4% in March projections). This signals one additional rate hike expected before year-end.
  • 9 of 18 FOMC members now favor at least one additional hike in 2026 vs. 8 favoring no change and 1 favoring a cut.
  • New Fed Chair Warsh (appointed by Trump) struck a more hawkish tone than prior Fed communications — his first FOMC meeting carried significant symbolic weight as investors recalibrate to his style.

Market reaction: S&P +0.10%, Nasdaq +0.34% — markets mostly shrugged, because (a) the hold itself was expected and (b) the 9-member-hike consensus was within the range of prior dots. The real market test will come if/when a September 2026 hike is explicitly flagged.

Portfolio implications: A late-2026 rate hike (priced for October/November per OIS markets) is the single biggest structural risk to growth/momentum positions. Historical precedent: each 25bps hike compresses P/E multiples on high-multiple growth names by ~1–2x turns. With many holdings at 30–50x forward earnings (INTC at 133x!), the sensitivity is elevated. The key unknown is whether earnings momentum (MU, WDC, BE) grows faster than the rate-compression headwind.

Also on June 17: the US Navy formally lifted the Iran naval blockade, and three Saudi tankers transited Hormuz overnight — the first commercial oil traffic through the strait in months.

Sources: CNBC Fed hold Jun 17, StockTitan dot plot flip

June 18 (Thursday): The Big Day — Nasdaq +1.91%, S&P +1.08%

Thursday was the week's climax, driven by three simultaneous catalysts:

  1. FOMC Relief Rally: With the hike priced in, markets re-risk-on. Nasdaq +1.91%, S&P +1.08%, Russell 2000 +2.12%. The "one hike expected but not imminent" clarity gave growth stocks room to breathe.

  2. Bloom Energy (BE) +15.4%: BE closed at $328.91, a new 52-week high. Two drivers:
    - FERC regulatory win: A new FERC policy makes it materially easier and faster for customers to deploy BE fuel cells without grid-connection backlog delays. This removes the biggest operational friction point for AI data center customers choosing onsite power.
    - Mid-year Data Center Power Report: Bloom's own analysis (well-received by investors) documenting fast AI data center growth through 2030, with grid constraints pushing developers toward onsite power and carbon capture.
    - Daiwa and UBS raised targets to $324 and $322 respectively with Overweight ratings.
    - Source: StocksToTrade BE Jun 18, TipRanks BE 52-wk high

  3. Intel (INTC) +10.64%: INTC closed at $133.99 after President Trump posted on social media claiming a "domestic manufacturing partnership" between Apple and Intel. While unconfirmed by either company, the market interpreted this as validation of Intel's foundry roadmap. INTC named Seok-Hee Lee to lead foundry packaging operations — a specific organizational signal that the foundry business is being operationalized, not just promised.
    - Context: BofA's double-upgrade (Jun 11) to Buy with $135 target cited server CPU demand and foundry upside. INTC is now +250% YTD (from $36.90 Jan 1 to $133.99).
    - Caution: Forward P/E of 133x is extremely speculative. Foundry breakeven not until 2027. The Apple rumor needs official confirmation to sustain. Source: Intel stock surge Jun 18 TradingKey

  4. Micron (MU) +8.7% to $1,133.99: Pre-earnings momentum accelerating. Wall Street scrambling to raise targets ahead of the June 24 print. TD Cowen raised to $1,500, RBC Capital Markets to $1,200, Aletheia Capital to $1,600. The stock crossed $1 trillion market cap this week — only the 10th US company ever to do so.

Holdings Deep-Dive: Key Winners This Week

WDC (Western Digital) — $746.23, +72.8% from May 1 entry ($431.74) — 4 Sleeves

WDC is now the single largest winner by absolute dollar gain across the portfolio. At entry, four sleeves each had ~$1,998–$2,031 invested in WDC. Combined: ~$8k at entry, now ~$14k at market (+$6k cumulative gain).

The structural story: WDC has crossed 50% gross margin for the first time in company history, driven by higher-capacity nearline drives (the kind used in AI training clusters), UltraSMR technology adoption, and improved pricing. The company has sold out its entire 2026 HDD production — a level of pre-commitment that effectively guarantees strong Q4 FY2026 (report ~late July) and FY2027 guidance.

The competitive moat: Morgan Stanley's June 15 note explicitly stated that WDC and STX have a combined duopoly in high-capacity nearline HDD. There is no third entrant — HGST was absorbed into WDC, and Toshiba's HDD segment serves different markets. The supply constraint isn't a near-term production scheduling issue; it's a 3-year lead-time issue for new clean-room capacity. This moat is genuinely defensible through at least 2027.

What to monitor: Any hyperscaler announcement that they are pivoting from HDD to NVMe/NAND for AI storage would be the key bear catalyst. Amazon/Meta/Google have all confirmed HDD roadmaps through 2028, but any defection would reprice rapidly. For now, the bull case is extraordinarily well-supported.

Source: WDC sold out Yahoo Finance, TIKR WDC gross margin

BE (Bloom Energy) — $328.91, +13.1% from May 1 entry ($290.67) — mom_roa, mom_v2, mom_v1

BE's +26.4% week pushed it from underwater to firmly profitable: entry $290.67, current $328.91 = +13.1% gain. The June 18 FERC win is the most structurally significant catalyst for Bloom since the Crusoe Energy partnership was announced.

FERC clarity matters: Prior to the new FERC policy, deploying a Bloom fuel cell system at a large AI data center required grid-connection approval — a process that could take 18–36 months due to utility backlogs. The new policy creates a faster track for "behind-the-meter" onsite generation that doesn't touch the transmission grid. This expands Bloom's addressable market dramatically for urgent AI buildout sites where utilities can't commit to power delivery timelines.

The Crusoe overhang resolved?: Prior reports flagged the 1.8 GW Cheyenne, Wyoming project pause as a risk. The June 18 rally suggests the market is now treating the FERC win and new contract pipeline as more important than the Crusoe delay. If Crusoe confirms the project proceeds on its revised timeline (expected confirmation by July 2026), this removes the last major bear point.

Revenue visibility: Bloom's fuel cell contracts are typically 5–20 year power purchase agreements (PPAs), providing long-duration revenue visibility. Unlike the semi names where each quarter requires a new beat, Bloom's backlog converts to predictable cash flow. This is structurally different from the binary-risk profile of pure-momentum plays.

Is BE a new sleeve candidate?: The AI power infrastructure theme is real and durable. BE, FCEL (FuelCell Energy), and any SMR developer (TerraPower, X-energy — both private) represent a 5–10 year secular theme driven by grid constraint. BE is the only large-cap, publicly-traded pure play. A dedicated "AI Power Infrastructure" sleeve with 3–5 names (BE, VRT (Vertiv), POWL, a utility or SMR developer) could capture this theme independently of the memory supercycle.

Sources: StocksToTrade BE Jun 18, Barchart BE CEO magic words

MU (Micron Technology) — $1,133.99, +108.9% from May 1 entry ($542.48) — 3 Systematic Sleeves + Residual

MU is now a 10-bagger from its pre-2026 lows and has crossed the $1 trillion market cap threshold. The June 24 earnings report is 5 days away and is the most important single catalyst in the portfolio's history.

Pre-earnings consensus:
- Revenue guidance: ~$33.5B (guidance) vs $34.8B (consensus estimate)
- Non-GAAP EPS: ~$19.15 (guidance) vs $19.82 (consensus)
- Gross margin: ~81% — if sustained, this is the highest in MU's history by 15+ percentage points

Analyst target range: TD Cowen $1,500, RBC $1,200, Aletheia $1,600, Daiwa $1,600. Average target of the bullish camp far exceeds current price. The "bear" target from the most cautious analyst: $249. The spread from $249 to $1,750 represents genuine uncertainty about whether the DRAM supercycle is structural or cyclical.

The key question for June 24: Will gross margin guidance for Q4 and beyond show further expansion (proving the structural HBM thesis) or slight compression (suggesting supply is beginning to catch up with demand)? The 81% margin at this scale is unprecedented in commodity memory — it signals that HBM pricing power is extreme. Even a guided 75–78% margin for Q4 would represent a high-quality beat.

Earnings proximity rule: The June rebalance is expected ~June 27–28, 3 days after MU's June 24 report. This means all sleeves will be rebalancing AFTER MU earnings, which eliminates the earnings-proximity risk that hit CIEN (bought 1 day pre-earnings, fell −29%). If MU beats and guides strongly, sleeves will retain MU at the rebalance; if MU disappoints, the signal may naturally rotate it out.

Source: TechTimes MU HBM earnings preview, Yahoo Finance MU earnings key number, Investing.com MU HBM bull case

Holdings Deep-Dive: Key Losers

VIAV (Viavi Solutions) — $47.17, −11.8% from Jun 12 entry ($53.46 Jun 12 close) — mom_roa, mom_v2, mom_v1

VIAV was one of the week's hardest-hit optical names. Entry from the June 3 rebalance was $52.45 for mom_roa ($53.46 was the Jun 12 close). VIAV provides optical components and network test equipment. Its optical hardware products compete in the same market as FN (Fabrinet) and CIEN — all three are suffering from the same phenomenon: the sector rotation from optical-interconnect-component into storage/memory and AI-semi-equipment.

The structural issue: investors see VIAV's optical test division as stable but slow-growth, while its optical component division directly competes with cheaper Asian manufacturers. The AI-driven optical market that VIAV participates in (network test, optical amplifiers) is real, but the market is rewarding companies with direct AI hardware supply chain exposure — which means LRCX, AMAT, MU — not test equipment.

Signal check: VIAV at $47.17 vs entry $52.45 = −10.1% loss on mom_roa position. The Jun 3 rebalance bought it when it was showing strong recent momentum. The post-June 3 optical sector selloff (triggered by CIEN's convertible offering on June 4) has continued to weigh on all optical names. At the June rebalance, VIAV's 12-1 momentum score will likely have degraded enough to exit.

VSAT (ViaSat) — $64.13, −1.6% from entry ($65.15 May 1) — mom_roa, residual, mom_v1, mom_v2

VSAT was +11.6% from entry as recently as June 11 ($72.71). The week of Jun 12–18 reversed that: falling from $70.17 to $64.13 = −8.6%. VSAT now shows only a small −1.6% loss from May entry.

The SpaceX dynamic: SpaceX's IPO debut June 12 (at $175+/share, raising $75B) highlighted the Starlink-ViaSat competitive tension. While we noted June 12 that VSAT's $437M US Space Force PTS-G contract insulates the defense/aviation segment, the market is now pricing in incremental Starlink market share risk in commercial broadband.

Satellite market dynamics: Starlink has ~6 million subscribers vs. ViaSat-3's 2 million. ViaSat's moat is:
(a) Government/defense segment (~40% revenue) where Starlink is not certified for classified traffic
(b) Aviation in-flight connectivity (Inmarsat merger gives VSAT the most comprehensive aviation network globally)
(c) ViaSat-3 Americas and ViaSat-3 EMEA have 2× the capacity of any prior Ka-band satellite

VSAT at $64.13 is modestly below entry. The momentum signal will evaluate it at rebalance — if the 12-1 score is still positive (driven by the Jan–Apr run-up), it stays; if the recent selloff has dragged the signal negative, it exits naturally.

FN (Fabrinet) — $573.76, −20.9% from entry ($725.36 Jun 3) — mom_roa, mom_v1, mom_v2

FN's continued underperformance (−6.1% this week, −20.9% from entry) raises the question of whether to exit manually. The systematic answer is: hold until the rebalance. The signal integrity of the systematic sleeves depends on not making discretionary exceptions during drawdowns.

What changed this week on FN: JPMorgan analyst Samik Chatterjee downgraded FN to Neutral from Overweight with a $700 price target. The downgrade cites "softening auto/LiDAR demand" as a near-term headwind and increased customer concentration risk (a small number of hyperscale and networking customers). Despite this, the analyst's $700 target is still +22% above current $574.

The case for patience: Q3 FY2026 guidance called for $1.15–$1.20B revenue with sequential growth across all three segments (Telecom, Datacom, HPC). Fabrinet is ramping Data Center Interconnect (DCI) capacity — the optical backbone linking distributed data centers. This is a structural AI-demand product, not cyclical. The downgrade is on relative attractiveness (vs. other names), not on absolute fundamentals.

At the June rebalance: FN's 12-1 month momentum score will reflect the −20.9% drawdown from the June 3 entry. Depending on where its prior 12 months look relative to peers, it may exit. If it exits, all three sleeves realize a ~−20.9% loss on the position and rotate into the next-ranked name.

CIEN (Ciena Corporation) — $428.22, −20.0% from entry ($535.56 May 1 for most sleeves) — mom_roa, residual, mom_v1, mom_v2

CIEN continues to languish. The convertible notes overhang ($2.875B, announced June 4) is still anchoring the stock. CIEN fundamental case: Q2 FY2026 +40% revenue YoY, +290% EPS YoY, raised FY2026 guidance to $6.3B revenue (+32%). The stock is at $428 vs. analyst average target $564.92 — 32% implied upside.

The dilution math: $2.875B convertible at a conversion premium means dilution of ~5–8% of share count if converted at current prices. This is a temporary anchor; the market will fully reprice once the conversion period window closes (typically 12–24 months). Patient hold to the June rebalance, then decide based on the signal.

LLM Overlay Status — Updated June 19

mom_roa_top1_paper (Control) vs llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper (Treatment)

What happened on June 12 (re-inception): The mom_roa_6535 signal rotated its top-1 name from FN to AAOI (Applied Optoelectronics) at the June rebalance. The control sleeve (mom_roa_top1) purchased 590.65 shares of AAOI at $169.13, for a position value of $99,900.

The LLM overlay VETOED AAOI on June 12. The treatment remains 100% cash at $100,000.

Why the veto was justified (flags available as of June 12 scoring):
1. Q1 2026 earnings miss: Revenue $151.1M (+51% YoY, positive) but EPS loss of $0.07 vs. $0.05 expected — bottom-line miss
2. Q2 guidance below consensus: AAOI guided Q2 revenue $185M vs. street estimate $197M; EPS breakeven ($0.00) vs. $0.07 estimated — both misses
3. SVP insider selling on Day 1: Fred Chang (SVP) sold 34,000 shares on June 12 at $163.78–$171.80 per share ($5.6M total), filed with the SEC. Insider selling on the same day as the systematic buy is a high-quality negative signal.
4. $600M ATM equity offering: AAOI entered an "at-the-market" offering with Raymond James and Needham — allows dilutive issuance of new shares at-the-market. Dilution overhang on a stock already priced at 917% one-year return.
5. InvestingPro "overvalued" flag: At $169, AAOI trades above its estimated Fair Value despite strong revenue growth.

Week 1 result (Jun 12→18):
- Control (AAOI): $99,950 → $95,697.37 = −4.25%
- Treatment (cash): $100,000 → $100,000 = 0.00%
- Treatment advantage: +4.25pp in 4 trading days

This is early data (n=1 week), but the veto logic was textbook: insider selling + guidance miss + dilution all present simultaneously. The AAOI situation mirrors the original FN entry thesis — strong momentum bringing the signal to a buy, but on-the-ground intelligence suggesting the momentum is not supported by improving fundamentals.

Next steps for the overlay:
- If AAOI is still the top-1 mom_roa name at the June 27–28 rebalance, the overlay needs to run the 3-prompt evaluation again: is the veto re-confirmed or should cash be deployed?
- The AAOI position for the control will be evaluated on whether 12-1 momentum still has it ranked #1 after a −4.25% week.

Source: Investing.com AAOI SVP sale

Strategic Insights & New Sleeve Ideas

1. Memory/Storage Supercycle Concentrated Sleeve — NOW HIGH CONVICTION

The June 15 analyst wave (Morgan Stanley WDC $650, STX $1,035; TD Cowen MU $1,500, RBC MU $1,200) is the clearest research convergence this portfolio has seen. WDC +72.8% from entry, STX +47.2% from entry, MU +109% from entry — all in 49 days.

Proposed sleeve structure (research-only until after MU June 24 earnings):

Name Thesis Weight
MU HBM4 sold out, DRAM oligopoly, $1T cap 30%
WDC AI-HDD duopoly, 50%+ GM, all 2026 production sold 25%
STX HDD nearline 44% YoY growth, 80% data center mix 25%
LRCX Etch/deposition steps in HBM manufacturing chain 10%
TER Semiconductor test equipment, HBM yield testing 10%

Signal: 12-month trailing revenue momentum + gross margin trend (not price momentum). Quarterly rebalance. Hold period: 2–3 years minimum.

When to build: After MU June 24 earnings. If MU beats on both revenue AND gross margin guidance, this sleeve becomes a conviction build for July. If MU disappoints on gross margin, wait for clarity on whether the HBM supercycle is already cresting.

Risk: This sleeve is highly correlated — all five names will fall together if the AI capex cycle turns. The hedge is that the supply shortage math (3-year fab lead time) makes the cycle structurally longer than historical DRAM cycles. Still a single-factor bet.

2. AI Power Infrastructure — Emerging Theme Worth Monitoring

BE's +26.4% week is not isolated. The broader trend: AI data centers require 150–200 MW of power per facility, and US grid capacity cannot support the AI buildout pipeline at the planned pace. Solutions companies include:

  • BE (Bloom Energy): Fuel cells, now benefiting from FERC regulatory clarity. Currently held.
  • VRT (Vertiv): Data center power and cooling infrastructure. Currently held by residual_roa ($328.47 entry, check current price).
  • POWL (Powell Industries): Electrical equipment for power distribution. Currently held by multiple sleeves.
  • GEV (GE Vernova): Grid and power infrastructure, strong order book. Not currently held.
  • SMR developers: TerraPower, X-energy — both private, not available until IPO (possible 2026–2027)

A dedicated 4–5 name "AI Power Infrastructure" sleeve would diversify away from the semiconductor concentration risk while remaining AI-adjacent. Primary difference from semiconductor names: revenue is driven by grid constraint and power demand (structural, multi-decade trend), not by DRAM pricing cycles (shorter cyclical). This could be a genuine risk-diversifier that still participates in the AI theme.

Decision: Research first. Build target: September 2026 if the grid-constraint narrative remains intact.

3. Hawkish Fed Regime Adaptation

The June 17 dot-plot shift (median rate 3.8% end-2026, implying one hike) creates a new headwind that wasn't in the model when the sleeves were designed. Historical momentum factor research (AQR, Asness et al.) shows that:
- Momentum strategies tend to underperform during rate-hike initiation (the first 1–3 hikes after a prolonged hold)
- Momentum strategies recover once the market reprices to the new rate regime

This is not a reason to exit the sleeves — the paper trade is long-horizon. But it suggests:
(a) Position sizing consideration: If building new capital in this sleeve setup, consider a smaller initial allocation until rate clarity emerges (October FOMC)
(b) Defensive rotation at June rebalance: If any top-ranked name is extremely high-multiple (INTC at 133x is the obvious one), the rate hike risk argues for the signal to naturally rotate it out if its momentum score degrades

Practical action: At the June rebalance, flag any holding with forward P/E >100x as "rate-sensitive" and apply extra scrutiny (but still let the signal decide).

4. AAOI as a Pattern Lesson for LLM Overlay Signal Design

The AAOI veto is an excellent case study. The three flags that matter most in retrospect:

  1. Insider selling on the day of the trade: SVP Fred Chang sold $5.6M the exact day the signal triggered a buy. Management insiders have access to information momentum signals don't: forward order pipelines, customer conversations, product yields. SVP-level selling at momentum-triggered price levels is one of the strongest near-term mean-reversion signals in empirical research (Seyhun, "Insiders' Profits, Costs of Trading, and Market Efficiency", 1986 and subsequent literature).

  2. Guidance below consensus for BOTH revenue and EPS: When a company guides both dimensions below street consensus in the same quarter, the probability of further estimate cuts is historically ~65% (based on earnings revision literature). This is the "negative estimate revision cycle" entering its first stage.

  3. ATM equity offering on a momentum-driven stock: An ATM offering is a management signal that they believe the stock is FAIRLY TO RICHLY PRICED (otherwise they wouldn't dilute at current prices). This is the opposite of what momentum bulls need — management conviction that the price is right or high.

Proposal for LLM overlay scoring rubric update: Add a formal "insider activity" check as one of the 3 required prompts. The current 3-prompt structure (macro context, company fundamentals, technical/momentum validity) should incorporate insider transaction data from Form 4 filings as an explicit input into the fundamentals prompt.

5. Sector Portfolio June Rebalance — XLE Exit Likely

XLE (Energy, $53.77, −8.7% from $58.88 May 1 entry) has been the sector_top4's drag all month. With the Iran MOU signed and Hormuz reopening (near-50% prewar flow levels within 30 days per Goldman Sachs), oil will face sustained supply-side pressure through at least Q3 2026.

Sector momentum scores to watch at June rebalance:
- XLK: +3.6% this week, +18.2% from entry → likely stays #1
- XLI: +2.7% this week, +4.6% from entry → likely stays #2
- XLB: −0.7% this week, +0.8% from entry → borderline #3/#4
- XLE: −6.6% this week, −8.7% from entry → likely exits
- Replacement candidates for XLE slot:
- XLY (Consumer Discretionary): Benefits from lower gas prices (Iran deal → oil decline = lower pump prices = more consumer spending). This is the most direct macro-rotation beneficiary from the Iran deal.
- XLF (Financials): Rate hike expectations often benefit bank margins (steeper yield curve from hike). If October hike becomes high-confidence, XLF momentum could accelerate.
- XLC (Communication Services): SpaceX IPO buzz, AI-driven ad spend recovery, Alphabet/Meta still executing.

The sector overlay's treatment should also rotate the XLE cash into whatever the signal selects at rebalance.

High priority (before June 24):

- 🟡 AAOI overlay decision: Run 3-prompt LLM evaluation before June 27–28 rebalance. Key inputs: Q2 results (if out), any change to ATM equity offering status, insider activity update. If AAOI is still top-1 mom_roa name, overlay must score.

Active monitoring:
- 🔵 BE Crusoe/Cheyenne update: Watch for any announcement on the 1.8 GW project timeline. BE at $328.91 — further upside depends on this project proceeding.
- 🔵 XLE sector rotation: Track momentum score through June 27. Candidates to replace: XLY, XLF, XLC.
- 🔵 FN (Fabrinet): At $573.76, −20.9% from entry. Will likely exit at June rebalance if momentum score has degraded past top-N threshold. If so, control (mom_roa_top1) changes underlying pick.
- 🔵 VIAV and VSAT: Both show weakening momentum. Likely exit at June rebalance.
- 🔵 INTC at 133x P/E: Trump Apple-Intel foundry rumor drove Jun 18 surge. Watch for official confirmation or denial — either will reprice INTC sharply. The rumor is currently holding the stock.
- 🔵 Iran MOU implementation: Deal requires Iranian compliance milestones (nuclear dismantlement schedule). Any Israeli military action or Iranian non-compliance could reverse the oil price trajectory and revive energy names. Keep watching geopolitical headline risk.
- 🔵 Fed October hike probability: Monitor OIS/CME FedWatch weekly. If probability crosses 50%, begin monitoring growth stock drawdown risk for late September/October.
- 🔵 Memory storage sleeve (new): Research build for July post-MU earnings. 5-name concentrated: MU/WDC/STX/LRCX/TER. Begin building backtesting spec.
- 🔵 AI Power sleeve (new): Research build for September. 4–5 names: BE/VRT/POWL/GEV + pending SMR developer. Spec out signal definition (revenue momentum + power contract backlog).
- 🔵 Earnings proximity filter: Before June 27–28 rebalance, pull earnings calendar for all names in the top-N momentum ranking. For any name with report within ±5 trading days, apply half-sizing rule. This prevents another CIEN-style pre-earnings buy.
- 🔵 Post-IPO anti-lock basket research (SpaceX): RKLB, RDW, SPCE behavior Jun 12 → Jul 12 (first 30 days post-SpaceX IPO). Measure if the anti-lock pattern (competitor bounce) is emerging. T+14 is June 26 — first check date.

June 19 Summary

Markets closed today (Juneteenth). All sleeve NAVs are as of June 18, the last trading day.

The week of June 12–18, 2026 was one of the strongest in the portfolio's history. mom_roa_6535 is the new clear leader at +11.15% inception-to-date (up from +6.42% at the June 11 report six days ago). After correcting the KLAC split artifact, residual_roa's true total return is approximately +10.55%, nearly tied with mom_roa and still well ahead of SPY (+3.62%).

Five themes drove the week: (1) the storage supercycle ignition (WDC +32.6%, STX +14.9%, Morgan Stanley's 33% WDC price target hike) driven by AI data center HDD shortages through 2028; (2) Bloom Energy's +26.4% breakout on FERC regulatory clarity for onsite AI power deployment; (3) MU's accelerating pre-earnings rally to $1,134 as the June 24 earnings print approaches with $1,200–1,600 analyst targets; (4) the US-Iran MOU and naval blockade lifting, which definitively punctured the energy premium (XLE −6.6% this week, and the sector overlay's XLE veto was validated); and (5) the FOMC's hawkish dot-plot pivot (9 of 18 members now see a rate hike in 2026), which is the most significant new structural risk introduced this week.

The two LLM overlays are producing their first signal-quality data: the AAOI veto (+4.25pp treatment advantage in week 1) and the XLE sector veto (+1.46pp treatment advantage this week) are both positive. This is n=1 data but the signal logic was sound in both cases (insider selling + guidance miss for AAOI; Iran deal macro read for XLE).

The single most important event of the next 30 days: Micron earnings June 24. The HBM supercycle thesis — which underlies most of this portfolio's alpha — gets either validated or stress-tested at that print. Hold all MU positions pre-earnings. The June 27–28 rebalance follows 3 days later, allowing post-earnings signal to inform the new portfolio.

CRITICAL UPDATE: SATS — Interest Payment Made June 18, Default Averted

On June 18, 2026 — the final day of the 30-day grace period — EchoStar's DISH DBS
subsidiary made the previously deferred $183 million interest payment covering three
series of notes (5.25% Secured 2026, 5.75% Secured 2028, 5.125% Unsecured 2029).
An Event of Default on $24.56B of total debt was averted.

What remains unresolved: The 2026 principal maturity wall totals $6.13 billion
(including $2.0B in 7 3/4% Notes and $2.75B in 5.25% Secured Notes). The Restructuring
Support Agreement (RSA, signed March 19 with 82% of noteholders) is designed to
address this through debt-for-equity conversion, but execution requires AT&T deal
proceeds ($20.25B, not arriving until November 2027 final close). SATS traded
$104–$112 over the Juneteenth weekend (weekend secondary market pricing), settling
near $107.63.

Portfolio action: The model exits SATS at July 1 regardless (12-1 momentum
deeply negative from peak $180+ to $107). The interest payment is near-term relief,
not a thesis change. Audit note: If SATS is halted or suspended before July 1,
the paper position cannot be closed — document as uncloseable if that occurs.

Sources: SEC 8-K EchoStar June 18 2026 payment,
EchoStar RSA announcement,
StockTitan SATS 10-Q going concern

US-Iran Geneva Talks POSTPONED June 19 — Oil Volatile

On June 15, the US and Iran signed a framework to end hostilities and reopen the
Strait of Hormuz — oil fell ~5% on the announcement (~$80/bbl Brent). But on
June 19 (Juneteenth), the next round of Geneva peace talks were abruptly postponed,
causing Brent to partially recover. WTI is now in the $78–82/bbl range as June 22
opens — volatile but still well below pre-Iran-conflict peaks of $95–100+.

Energy positions in residual_roa_6535 (all targeted for July 1 exit):

Ticker Entry Est. Jun-22 Price P&L % Note
APA (Apache) $38.35 ~$33 ~−14% Iran deal structurally bearish
DINO (HF Sinclair) $73.38 ~$64 ~−13% Refining margin squeeze
PARR (Par Pacific) $65.38 ~$50 ~−24% Worst portfolio performer
NRT (Natural Resource) $8.17 ~$7.00 ~−14% WTI-correlated royalty trust
PBR (Petrobras) $18.20 ~$16.50 ~−9% Brazilian upstream
INSW (Int'l Seaways) $78.01 ~$82 ~+5% Tanker voyages UP with Hormuz open

The Geneva postponement provides a short-term reprieve (oil bounced) but the
structural case for exit is unchanged: IEA forecasts 6M bpd surplus by 2027, and
even a failed Iran deal does not restore the $95+ peak pricing thesis. The 60-day
framework runs through mid-August — July exits remove portfolio from this uncertainty.

INSW exception: Tanker operator benefits from MORE Hormuz traffic, not less.
The model may score INSW with positive 12-1 momentum June 30. Worth noting.

Al Jazeera (US Iran framework oil falls stocks rally),
NBC News (oil prices Iran deal may not go lower)

INTC — 4 Days Without Official Apple Confirmation

Intel closed June 18 at $133.99 (+10.64%). As of June 22 pre-market, INTC remains
near $134 — the market has priced in the deal without getting confirmation from
either company. Timeline:

Date Event INTC
Jun 17 AH Trump: "Apple agreed to work with Intel" (Truth Social) +9% pre-mkt
Jun 18 Markets close at $133.99 (+10.64%) $133.99
Jun 19 Juneteenth — no statement from Apple or Intel Market closed
Jun 22 4th business day; still no press release ~$134

Intel is up +262% YTD from $37 at start of 2026. At $134, Mizuho's PT of $135
(Neutral rating) suggests limited upside without formal deal confirmation. The
stock's current pricing reflects ~70% probability of the deal being real and
eventually contracted. Formal confirmation = another 5–10% leg; explicit denial
= potential 15–20% reversal to $110–115 range.

For the July 1 model hold: The 12-1 momentum score from June 3 entry ($112.77)
will be strongly positive at June 30 regardless of Apple deal status. Model holds.

Sources: TIKR (Intel up 260% 2026 Apple deal what's left),
Blockonomi (INTC surges Apple partnership CEO vision)

Strategic Insights — June 22

Insight 1: MU + PCE Sequencing — The One-Two Punch Risk

MU earnings Wednesday night and PCE data Thursday create a 14-hour window where
the portfolio could move sharply in two opposite directions:

Scenario A (bull-bull): MU beats big, PCE benign → tech rally extends across
Thursday and into July 1. All five systematic sleeves benefit. Best case for the period.

Scenario B (bull-bear): MU beats, but PCE hot → rate spike reverses tech gains.
After-hours MU surge gives back 50% or more during Thursday's session.
Model action: Hold through the noise. A strong MU business print is a 12–24
month thesis, not a one-day trade.

Scenario C (bear): MU misses on gross margin → MU −15–20%, drags tech, coincides
with hot PCE. Worst case but lowest probability given Apple's "unsustainable" supply
admission (first-person confirmation of pricing power from MU's largest consumer customer).

The key watch number: MU's next-quarter gross margin guidance. If guided above
80%, the supercycle is real. If guided below 78%, HBM yield issues are emerging.

Insight 2: AI Power Infrastructure — Self-Reinforcing Cluster

The AGX (+5%), FIX (+5%), BE (+13%), VRT (+1%), POWL (+8%) cluster in the portfolio
represents a structural theme that reinforces itself:

  1. AI training/inference demand grows → data centers need more power
  2. Utility grid timelines are 3–5 years → developers bypass the grid
  3. On-site power (BE fuel cells, AGX gas plants) creates demand for electrical
    construction (FIX, POWL) and thermal management (VRT)
  4. The cycle repeats at each new campus

The ROA × momentum model captured this cluster organically because all five companies
have high ROA (profitable backlog execution) and strong 12-1 momentum. No manual
override was needed — the factor model found the theme.

Key risk: Any major hyperscaler CapEx pause in Q3 2026 guidance would compress
multiples across this cluster simultaneously. No evidence of CapEx deceleration;
the opposite — every major hyperscaler (MSFT, AMZN, GOOG, META) has raised 2026
CapEx guidance in 2026.

Insight 3: OTC Filter — QDMI → Implementation Before August 1

The QDMI collapse (~−70%) confirms the need for an exchange-quality filter in the
universe construction. Proposed implementation for July 1 development session:

  • Add exchange column to fundamentals_cache; populate from yfinance info['exchange']
  • Universe filter: exchange IN ('NYSE', 'NasdaqGS', 'NasdaqGM', 'NasdaqCM', 'AMEX')
  • Exclude: OTC, Pink Sheets, OTCQB, OTCQX
  • Estimated impact: Eliminates ~200–300 OTC names from the ~5,000 name universe;
    removes ~90% of pump-and-dump risk with minimal loss of legitimate small-cap names
  • Must be in place before August 1 rebalance (July 1 rebalance may still see OTC names
    in new picks; implement filter during July 1 development session)

Insight 4: Iran Oil Deal — Why the July 1 Exit Timing Is Correct

The Geneva postponement on June 19 is negotiating friction, not deal collapse. The
60-day framework runs until ~August 14, 2026. For energy positions:

  • Exiting APA/DINO/PARR/NRT/PBR at July 1 removes portfolio exposure during the
    most uncertain 6 weeks of the Iran negotiation process
  • Even if talks fail: the structural IEA supply glut (+6M bpd surplus by 2027) is
    independent of Iran and is bearish for upstream producers long-term
  • Even if deals succeed: refiner margins (DINO, PARR) compress as cheap Iranian oil
    floods the market, hurting crack spreads even as raw crude prices fall

The exit is correct in either scenario. The July 1 timing minimizes further drag
from names that are already substantially underwater.

Questions to Monitor This Week

  1. MU Wednesday June 24: Revenue beat and gross margin guidance above 80%?
    This is the week's dominant binary event for the entire portfolio.
  2. SATS July 1 principal maturity: Any additional 8-K filings detailing
    principal payment deferral or formal restructuring close? The RSA must execute
    by/around July 1.
  3. INTC: Official Apple/Intel press release? Day 5 without confirmation as of
    June 23 open. Watch for any WSJ/Bloomberg updates citing negotiations.
  4. PCE Thursday June 26: Core MoM >0.3% = hike scare; <0.2% = relief rally.
    Impacts rate-sensitive names (DOCN, VRT, growth names in all sleeves).
  5. ARWR SHASTA: Q3 topline window opens July. Any early readout announcement
    is a major binary event upward for ARWR.
  6. US-Iran talks: Geneva rescheduled? New meeting date announcement = oil resumes
    decline = additional pressure on exit candidates.
  7. Overlay prep: Run overlay_prep.bat by June 30. LLM decisions required BEFORE
    July 1 execution.

Deep Dives: June 22 Key Catalysts

COHR (Coherent Corp) +12.31% — AI Optical Infrastructure Re-Rating

Coherent closed at $425.48 (+$46.63, +12.31%), the largest single-day absolute dollar gain
in the portfolio today. No single press release triggered the move; instead, several
reinforcing factors converged on June 22:

  1. Sector rotation from software to hardware: GOOGL's −6.5% decline (see below) drove
    institutional rotation INTO AI-agnostic hardware plays. Coherent is a direct beneficiary —
    it doesn't matter whether OpenAI or Google wins the model race; optical transceivers are
    needed by every AI data center regardless.

  2. Nasdaq-100 rebalance effect: With AI hardware names (ALAB, CRWV, NBIS, TER) added
    to QQQ effective June 22, attention focused on AI infrastructure plays broadly. COHR is
    not in the Nasdaq-100 but benefited from the AI optics narrative.

  3. Ongoing fundamentals: Coherent's AI optical transceivers are booked through 2028
    (NVIDIA's $2B strategic investment confirms this); Q4 FY2026 adj EPS guided $1.52–$1.72
    with datacenter/comms now ~75% of sales; CHIPS Act LoI for up to $50M in direct funding.

Portfolio impact:

Sleeve Shares Entry (Jun 3) Jun-22 Close Unrealized
mom_roa_6535 6.06 $329.66 $425.48 +$580 (+29.1%)
mom_v1_paper 3.29 $329.66 $425.48 +$315 (+29.1%)

In 19 days since the June 3 entry, COHR is up +29.1%. At July 1, the 12-1 momentum
score will be strongly positive (COHR is up ~200% YTD from its pre-2026 levels). Near-certain
hold at July 1 rebalance.

Sources: Yahoo Finance (COHR NVIDIA optics deal),
Seeking Alpha (COHR this party is here to stay)

CRDO (Credo Technology) +11.29% — Evercore ISI Initiation + DustPhotonics Silicon Photonics

Credo Technology closed at $302.52 (+$30.69, +11.29%), extending the post-Q4 earnings
rally with two new catalysts:

  1. Evercore ISI initiated with Outperform — joining Needham, Roth, Jefferies, BofA,
    Mizuho, Susquehanna, and JPMorgan. Stifel raised PT to $350 from $250.
    Consensus now: 18 analysts Buy/Strong Buy, avg PT rising.

  2. DustPhotonics acquisition gives CRDO a vertically integrated silicon photonics stack
    for the 800G → 3.2T optical transceiver roadmap. This makes CRDO not just an AEC (Active
    Electrical Cable) company but a full optical interconnect stack player — expanding the TAM
    and extending the runway.

Q4 FY2026 fundamentals underpinning the rally:
- Revenue: $437M (+157% YoY)
- GAAP gross margin: ~68%
- GAAP net income: $169M (net margin: ~38.7%)
- Q1 FY27 guide: $465–$475M with high-60s gross margins
- Full-year FY2026 revenue: $1.34B; net income $472M

Portfolio impact:

mom_v1_paper 4.98 $214.71 $302.52 +$437 (+40.9%)

In 19 days from the June 3 entry, CRDO is up +40.9% — the highest % gain of any
June-3 position. At $302.52, Stifel's $350 PT implies another +15.7% upside from here.

analyst PT of ~$377 implies +5.3% additional upside. Street high: $500.

HOLD at July 1 despite being underwater from our May 1 entry. The model correctly
captures the stock's 12-1 trend even if we bought at the interim peak.

Position recovery math: If CIEN reaches Barclays' $607 target, the −14% loss

New information emerged today:

Google/Nvidia foundry expansion:
Search results confirm that Alphabet/Google and Nvidia are designating Intel as a backup
chip manufacturer for their most advanced AI processors because TSMC capacity is stretched.
This is SEPARATE from the Apple deal and represents a multi-customer Intel foundry thesis:

  • Customer 1: Apple (Trump announcement June 17 — still officially unconfirmed but priced in)
  • Customer 2: Google — AI processor backup manufacturing
  • Customer 3: Nvidia — AI chip overflow capacity at Intel 18A-P

If all three relationships are confirmed, Intel's IDM 2.0 strategy has reached critical mass.
Intel Foundry would become THE domestic US alternative to TSMC — with the entire US AI
semiconductor ecosystem as potential customers.

TER (Teradyne) +4.36% to $457.00 — Nasdaq-100 Inclusion Effective Today

Teradyne closed at $457.00 (+$19.08, +4.36%) on the effective date of its Nasdaq-100

five AI/tech names: ALAB (Astera Labs), CRWV (CoreWeave), NBIS (Nebius), RKLB (Rocket Lab),
and TER (Teradyne). Five names were removed: CHTR, CTSH, INSM, VRSK, ZS.

The mechanical tailwind: Passive ETFs tracking QQQ (the Nasdaq-100 ETF, ~$350B AUM)
must buy TER to match the new index composition. This creates systematic demand beyond
fundamental buyers.

Additional catalyst: Teradyne secured a $139.9M Air Force multi-year contract for
diagnostic test station kits, adding US defense revenue alongside its semiconductor test
core business.

for manufacturing scale, not confirmation of shipment. The veto criteria has not been met.
The treatment remains in cash at $100,000.

1. June 15 QuantumLink deal (+13.3% one day) — veto looked catastrophically wrong; stock reversed
2. June 22 China credit line (+5.79%) — veto behind again; thesis still intact but weakened

Key question for the experiment: The credit line for fixed assets ($300M RMB = ~$41M USD
equivalent) signals that AAOI is investing in new optical component manufacturing equipment.
If this equipment is for 1.6T CPO pumps/transceivers, it IS the preparation for the trigger
event. The runbook should be REVIEWED: does "preparing to ramp" count as "confirmed shipment"?
For now, the answer is NO — the strict reading of the runbook requires actual shipment confirmation.

GOOGL −6.5%: AI Talent Exodus and What It Means for the Portfolio

Alphabet fell −6.5% on June 22 on two consecutive AI talent departures:

  1. Noam Shazeer → OpenAI: A leading AI engineering figure, Shazeer returned to Alphabet
    in August 2024 via a $2.7B reverse acquihire of Character.AI. His departure to OpenAI is
    the most high-profile talent loss since that acquihire.

  2. John Jumper → Anthropic: Key DeepMind researcher, notable for AlphaFold protein-folding
    work; his departure to Anthropic signals that well-resourced AI labs can compete with
    Google's talent packages.

Market impact: Alphabet lost ~$250 billion in market cap on June 22. Amazon −4.8%,
Microsoft −3%, Meta −2.3% were sympathy casualties as megacap AI software sold off.

Portfolio implication: Our sleeves hold NONE of these names. The quality-momentum
framework organically avoided high-multiple software megacaps (they don't score high on
ROA × momentum in a way that passes the universe filter). The result: our portfolio
GAINED on a day where the S&P's largest components by weight fell sharply.

Strategic insight: The GOOGL talent exodus is actually BULLISH for AI infrastructure.
More competition in frontier models = more training runs = more GPU compute = more HBM
memory = more optical transceivers = more power. Our holdings (MU, WDC, COHR, CRDO, VRT,
AEHR) benefit from a MORE competitive AI model landscape, not less.

This is the core resilience argument for the ROA × momentum factor approach:
it owns the "picks and shovels" that benefit regardless of who wins the model race.

Sources: Bloomberg (Alphabet shares drop second AI star departs),
InteractiveCrypto (Alphabet plunges 6.5% AI talent exodus),
TradingKey (GOOGL moved down 3.80% Jun 22)

SpaceX SPCX −16.4% — Post-IPO Correction and $20B Bond Offering

SpaceX fell −16.4% to ~$154.60, the largest single-day decline since its IPO debut
on June 12, 2026 (10 days ago). The causes:

  1. $20B inaugural bond issuance: SpaceX confirmed its first bond offering as a public
    company. The $20B in new debt raised concerns about cash burn rate, dilution, and the
    company's near-term profitability discipline.

  2. MSCI "lowest possible ESG rating": Added an additional institutional seller cohort
    (ESG-constrained funds that cannot hold the name).

  3. Post-IPO correction mechanics: SpaceX briefly surpassed Amazon and Microsoft in
    market cap in its first two trading days before declining. The three-day losing streak
    (−3.6% Wed, −5% Thu, −16.4% Mon) reflects the typical post-IPO normalization.

Portfolio impact: None. SpaceX is not held in any sleeve. The portfolio avoided the
post-IPO euphoria-to-correction cycle entirely.

What this signals for the model: High-momentum names at IPO peak can score highly on
short-term price momentum but are dangerous entries due to (a) IPO lockup overhangs,
(b) lack of established ROA track record, and (c) index-inclusion mechanics that reverse
quickly. The ROA filter would likely exclude SpaceX at IPO (no multi-year ROA data). This
is a case where the dual-signal (ROA × momentum) protected the portfolio from a tempting but
dangerous entry.

Sources: CNBC (SpaceX stock tanks 16% IPO rally selloff),
Yahoo Finance (SpaceX dropping again June 22),
TipRanks (SpaceX SPCX sinking June 22 2026)

Strategic Insights — June 22

Insight 1: Hardware/Software Bifurcation as a Persistent Structural Theme

June 22 crystallized a theme that has been building all year:
AI hardware wins; AI software loses in a talent-departure/competition-escalation event.

  • COHR +12.31%, CRDO +11.29%, VRT +7.48%, MU +6.82% — all hardware/infrastructure
  • GOOGL −6.5%, DOCN −4.06%, MSFT −3%, META −2.3% — all software/platform

This is not a coincidence. It reflects the structural position of each company:
- Hardware companies get paid per unit of AI compute consumed; their revenue scales with
ANY model running on ANY inference stack
- Software/platform companies compete for AI user mindshare; talent departures directly
reduce their competitive moat

Implication for sleeve design: The ROA × momentum model NATURALLY tilts toward hardware
because hardware companies have hard assets (ROA) and visible demand cycles (momentum).
Software companies often have high gross margins but lower ROA (less hard-asset intensity).
This structural tilt is a feature, not a bug — and it shows up in days like today.

Insight 2: AAOI Veto Runbook Refinement Needed

The second scare event (June 15 QuantumLink +13.3%, June 22 China credit line +5.79%)
raises a question about the runbook's trigger criteria:

Current trigger: "confirmed first-customer 1.6T CPO shipment"

Is the China credit line a partial trigger? The RMB 300M allocation for fixed assets
implies equipment purchases for manufacturing capacity. If the equipment is for 1.6T CPO
production, it is preparation for the trigger — typically 3–6 months ahead of first shipment.

Proposed clarification for the runbook:
- Trigger 1 (hard): First confirmed 1.6T CPO customer shipment from a hyperscaler
- Trigger 2 (soft, conditional): AAOI confirms (on earnings or IR call) that 1.6T CPO
production equipment is installed and sampling has begun
- Trigger 3 (invalidation of veto): If AAOI reports Q2 2026 earnings above analyst
consensus AND guidance includes 1.6T CPO revenue line → re-enter

The doubled credit line is a precursor event but does not meet any of the three trigger
criteria. Maintain cash position.

Insight 3: CRDO DustPhotonics Acquisition — Silicon Photonics vs. AEC Long-Term

CRDO's DustPhotonics acquisition gives it silicon photonics capability. This is important
for the long-term durability of the CRDO position:

AEC (Active Electrical Cable) limitation: The AI connectivity market will eventually
transition from AEC (copper with active components) to silicon photonics as data rates
exceed 3.2T and distances require optical solutions. CRDO's existing AEC business could be
disrupted by optical-native solutions.

Silicon photonics acquisition = hedge: By acquiring DustPhotonics, CRDO is building
the capability to compete in the optical era BEFORE it arrives. This is a smart execution
of the product roadmap. Combined with its 35%+ net margins and $1.34B FY2026 revenue base,
CRDO has the capital to fund this transition internally.

Implication for sleeve: CRDO should score well on 12-1 momentum at July 1 AND at the
August 1 and future rebalances. The DustPhotonics acquisition extends the long-duration
thesis from 1-2 years to 3-5 years.

Insight 4: OTC Filter + New Universe Quality Checks (Pre-August 1 Development)

Three developments reinforce the case for the planned exchange-quality filter:
1. QDMI: −70% collapse from OTC pump-and-dump; no material news
2. SATS: Credit risk that appeared as "positive ROA" (spectrum assets masked operational losses)
3. SpaceX lesson: IPO-peak entries on momentum alone (without ROA track record) are dangerous

Proposed additions to the filter before August 1 rebalance:
1. Exchange filter: exchange IN ('NYSE', 'NasdaqGS', 'NasdaqGM', 'NasdaqCM', 'AMEX')
→ Excludes QDMI-type OTC names
2. Going-concern flag: Screen for 10-Q/10-K filings that contain "going concern" language
→ Would have excluded SATS at May 1 if implemented
3. IPO age filter: Require minimum 12 months of price history for valid 12-1 momentum
→ Prevents buying at IPO peak (SpaceX would be excluded until June 2027)

These three filters together would have prevented all three categories of failure observed
since inception.

Questions to Monitor This Week

  1. MU Wednesday June 24 (CRITICAL): Revenue beat + gross margin guidance >80%?
    This is the single most important event for the entire portfolio this week.
    - Bull case: Revenue $34.8B+ AND GM guidance 81%+ → MU likely $1,300–$1,500
    - Bear case: GM miss below 78% → MU −15–20%, dragging all 4 sleeves
  2. PCE Thursday June 26: Core MoM >0.3% = rate hike panic; <0.2% = relief rally.
    Directly impacts FOMC 9-8 split narrative and October hike probability.
  3. VRT dividend June 25: $0.38 to mom_roa paper account.
  4. SATS grace period: Any EchoStar 8-K by June 30 on payment/waiver/extension?
    Each day without resolution increases the probability of Event of Default at July 1.
  5. AAOI experiment: Does AAOI hold above $169.13 (original veto price)?
    If control ends June 30 above $169.13, the experiment is currently negative overall.
    If below $169.13, the cumulative veto thesis is intact.
  6. ARWR SHASTA: Q3 2026 topline data window opens July. Watch for any PR.
  7. Overlay prep: Run overlay_prep.bat by June 29.
    LLM decisions needed BEFORE July 1 execution:
    - Next systematic #1 name for mom_roa_top1 sleeve
    - XLE slot reassessment for sector_top4 macro veto
    - Four-prompt macro set for sector_top4

- The momentum sleeves amplify moves both directions: Jun 15 +3.87% vs SPY +1.76%, but
Jun 16 âˆ’3.65% vs SPY âˆ’0.60%. Beta to the semi complex is running ~2â€“2.5Ã— the market.
- Residual_roa consistently damps down days (Jun 16: âˆ’0.99% vs mom_roa âˆ’3.65%) while
still participating meaningfully in up days (Jun 22: +2.33%). The low-beta property is
structural, not coincidental. The energy cluster headwind (Iran deal) partially offset
the semi tailwind â€” net result is lower beta in both directions.
- June 22 is the most remarkable day: SPY fell slightly (âˆ’0.31%) while momentum sleeves
added +2.3-2.7%. This is pure MU driving. At $1,211 ATH on earnings eve, MU alone
contributed approximately +0.8-1.0% to each of the four holding sleeves.

Macro Theme 1: Iran Peace Deal â€” Signed June 14-15, Now Structural

Status as of June 22: The Strait of Hormuz reopened under a 60-day negotiation MOU
framework. Pakistan PM Sharif brokered the agreement; Trump confirmed the framework June
14-15. Full mine-clearing and vessel normalization will take weeks, but the geopolitical
risk premium has been stripped from oil.

Oil price collapse:
- Brent crude: ~$87 (June 12 pre-market) â†’ $78.96 June 15 â€” first sub-$80 since
March. WTI settled at $76.05.
- Experts caution full price normalization takes months; consumers may not see pre-war
gas prices until 2027, but the supply-risk premium has deflated.
- XLE ETF erased its entire 2026 Iran War rally. Entry price for sector_top4: $58.88;
June 22 level: ~$54.67 (âˆ’7.2%). Benzinga reported XLE gave back the entire year's
war-era outperformance.
- APA Corp shed >22% from peak. Entry $38.35 in residual_roa; now estimated ~$29-30.

Energy cluster damage in residual_roa (~8 names):

Ticker Entry Sector Est. Status Iran Sensitivity

DINO $73.38 Refiner ~âˆ’24% High

Estimated total energy cluster drag: âˆ’$3,200 to âˆ’$3,800 since June 3 rebalance, or
roughly âˆ’3.5% on residual_roa's $109k base. The fact that residual_roa is still +3.93%
over the same window means the other 42 names generated +7-8% to overcome this drag.
This is the clearest evidence of the idiosyncratic signal's quality: it can absorb a
brutal sector headwind and still generate alpha.

July 1 rebalance action: All 5-8 energy names will rank near the bottom of the
residual universe after this crash. The signal will exit them systematically. Accept the
realized losses â€” honor the process, no manual override before rebalance.

Macro Theme 2: New Fed Chair Warsh â€” "One Hike" Dot (June 16-17)

Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting left the target range unchanged at 3.5â€“3.75%, but the
new median dot is 3.8% year-end â€” implying one 25bp hike is the base case for H2 2026.
Warsh is a known hawk; the market had priced in a "persistent pause" under Powell's
legacy framework. This repricing created the sharp Jun 16 reversal (SPY âˆ’0.60%, momentum
sleeves âˆ’3.1 to âˆ’3.65%).

Portfolio implication: A rate hike in H2 2026 would modestly compress growth-stock
multiples. However, the portfolios hold companies benefiting from structural AI demand
(MU, INTC, STX) where earnings growth can absorb multiple compression. The bigger risk
is a surprise second hike. Monitor incoming CPI data carefully.

Source:
- CNBC: Stock market updates June 17, 2026
- Kavout: Semiconductor sell-off context

Context: The June 5 Semiconductor Crash (Pre-Window, Explains the Hole)

The June 9-10 NAV weakness (all momentum sleeves âˆ’3 to âˆ’4% vs the June 9 start) traces
back to a broader semiconductor crash that occurred on June 5, 2026 â€” before the current
reporting window. Cause: Broadcom (AVGO) fiscal Q2 2026 earnings miss.

  • AVGO AI networking revenue: $4.1B actual vs $4.8B expected (âˆ’14% miss)
  • CEO Hock Tan maintained 2027 AI semiconductor outlook rather than raising it
  • PHLX chip index fell 10% â€” worst single day since March 2020
  • $1.3 trillion in sector market cap wiped out
  • AMD âˆ’10.86%, Intel âˆ’11.28% on June 5

This is why June 9-10 showed all momentum sleeves down: they were still working through
the hangover from that washout. The June 11-12-15 rally was the genuine recovery/
continuation. Understanding this context clarifies that the "V-bottom" in the NAV data
was not random â€” it was a washout followed by a clean confirmation.

Sources:
- TheStreet: Nasdaq falls 4%, semiconductor slide wipes $1T, June 5
- Intellectia: Semiconductor selloff $1.3T analysis
- Intellectia: Semi stocks rebound after trillion selloff

June 24 earnings setup (after-close release):

  • Revenue consensus: $34.66Bâ€“$35.59B (company guided $32.75Bâ€“$34.25B; consensus
    tracking above the top of guidance range â€” a strong signal)
  • EPS consensus: $19.72â€“$19.95
  • Gross margin: ~81.6% â€” would be HIGHEST in company history if achieved. Management
    guided 81% at the midpoint.
  • HBM4 focus: Jensen Huang confirmed June 5 in Seoul that all three HBM suppliers
    (SK Hynix, Samsung, Micron) are qualified and shipping for Nvidia's Vera Rubin platform.
    Micron's entire 2026 HBM supply is fully contracted.
  • Implied move: 17% (options-derived). At $1,211, that's roughly $1,004â€“$1,418 range.
  • Analyst consensus: 27 analysts, all Strong Buy. Zero sell ratings. Deutsche Bank
    PT $1,500, Wolfe Research $1,250, Daiwa $1,600.
  • Forward demand: DRAM demand growing +27% YoY in 2026, +24% in 2027. Supply gap
    persists into 2028+ given HBM4 conversion and fab lead times (2-3 year greenfield).

Scenario analysis:

Scenario Probability MU Move Portfolio Impact (4 sleeves)
Beat + strong HBM4 guidance ~65% +12 to +18% +2.5 to +3.5% per sleeve
Beat + in-line guidance ~20% +3 to +8% +0.5 to +1.5% per sleeve
Meet + guidance disappointment ~10% âˆ’10 to âˆ’15% âˆ’2 to âˆ’3% per sleeve
Miss or negative surprise ~5% âˆ’20%+ âˆ’4%+ per sleeve

Expected value is clearly positive. Hold all four positions through earnings.

Action policy: No preemptive exit. At July 1 rebalance, MU will rank top regardless
of a modest post-earnings dip (the 12-1 momentum from $542 is too large to erase with a
15% single-day drop). The only scenario to pre-exit would be a catastrophic announcement
(fraud, export ban, plant disaster) â€” none of which are in the consensus risk set.

Post-earnings monitoring (June 25):
- Did gross margin hit or beat 81%?
- Did management raise FY2026 revenue guidance?
- Did HBM4 Vera Rubin Ultra 2027 allocation commentary surprise positively?
- Did the stock hold above $1,100 (i.e., no "beat and crash" like CIEN)?

Intel (INTC) â€” The Sleeper Winner: +24.9% From Entry

INTC was purchased at the June 3 rebalance at entry $112.77 by mom_roa, mom_v1, and
mom_v2. As of June 22, INTC closed at $140.94 â€” +24.9% from entry in 19 days.
This is the second-largest single-name alpha contributor after MU.

The INTC driver sequence since June 11:

  1. June 11 â€” BofA double upgrade: Underperform â†’ Buy, PT $96 â†’ $135. Thesis: server
    CPU opportunity ($40B TAM by 2030), Intel Foundry wins (Alphabet 3M-unit TPU order
    for 2028, Apple M-series wafers in pipeline). Stock surged 10%+ that session.

  2. June 17-22 â€” Apple/AI foundry deals: Multiple new reports. Apple plans to work
    with Intel to design and manufacture chips in the US. Alphabet/Google and NVIDIA
    designated Intel as backup manufacturer for advanced AI processors (TSMC capacity
    stretched). Stock ripped another 10-11%.

  3. June 22 close $140.94 (+5.19%): Apple deal explicitly endorsed by Trump, adding
    political tailwind to the thesis. INTC now 47% above its early-June sub-$100 trough.

  4. 18A-P node on schedule: Enhanced node entered risk production. Full design-rule
    compatibility with 18A. This de-risks the #1 bear case (foundry timeline slippage).

BofA foundry projections: Revenue from $1.1B (2026) â†’ $47.1B (2030). Even at 30%
probability of execution, this is a transformational option embedded at current prices.

Key risk: INTC has run +47% from its trough in 6 weeks. Execution risk on 18A-P
volume production remains real. A single fab yield problem could erase multiple points.
Watch the September quarter for first signs of volume foundry revenue.

cycle â€” STX faces no Samsung/SK Hynix competition equivalent; WDC is the only peer.

Ciena (CIEN) â€” Convertible Overhang Anatomy

CIEN entered at $535.56 (June 3 rebalance) across mom_roa, mom_v1, mom_v2, and
residual_roa. The convertible notes deal structure is now fully known:

  • $2.875B convertible senior notes, 0% coupon, due 2031
  • Conversion price: $746.66/share (60% premium over June 8 price of $466.67)
  • Use: $100M note hedge + $140M share repurchases + $1.14B term loan repayment
  • Net: deleveraged balance sheet, but dealer hedging creates mechanical selling pressure

The conversion premium at $746 means there is ZERO dilution risk at current prices (~$445
range). The headwind is the dealer delta-hedge: dealers who sold the converts to investors
short-sell stock to hedge their long-convert position. This mechanical selling typically
resolves over 3-6 months as the position seasons and delta-hedgers get longer.

Analyst range: Stifel Buy PT $615 (+38%), UBS Neutral PT $508 (+14%). Business
remains strong ($6.3B FY2026 guidance, +32% YoY).

Strategy: Hold through July 1 rebalance; let the signal decide on exit. If CIEN
exits the top-50 due to price decline, accept the loss. If it stays ranked, hold and
wait for the dealer hedging to resolve over Q3 2026.

Sources:
- Quiverquant: Ciena convertible overhang
- Investing.com: Ciena closes $2.875B offering

Strategic Research: New Long-Term Sleeve Candidate

Photonics/Optical Interconnect Concentrated Sleeve

The current portfolio has scattered exposure to optical interconnect: FN in mom sleeves,
AAOI in the single-name experiment, CIEN and COHR across multiple sleeves, VIAV in
several. But no sleeve is deliberately harvesting the optical ramp as a concentrated thesis.

The multi-year tailwind:
- Data center AI inference clusters require 10-100Ã— more optical ports per rack vs
traditional IT infrastructure
- The 400Gâ†’800Gâ†’1.6T transceiver migration is a multi-year procurement wave (2026-2030)
- AAOI confirmed $324M in 800G/1.6T orders from hyperscalers; FN manufacturing
components for the same buyers; CIEN selling coherent systems; COHR making transceivers
- Hyperscalers (Amazon, Google, Microsoft, Meta) have committed $100B+ in AI capex for
2026, most of which requires optical connectivity upgrades

Proposed sleeve structure:
- Universe: 20-30 photonics/optical names (AAOI, FN, CIEN, COHR, VIAV, LRCX [optical
deposition], MXL, IIVI, LITE, CMLS, AAON, LITE)
- Signal: 12-1 momentum + ROA filter (same as mom_roa_6535 structure)
- Size: Top-5 equal-weight positions
- Rebalance: Monthly

Long-term viability (3-5 year horizon): The optical demand curve is driven by AI
model sizes growing +4-10Ã— annually, which translates directly into interconnect upgrades.
Unlike the DRAM cycle (which has historical boom-bust), optical is a replacement-rate
cycle (once installed, these transceivers depreciate and require upgrade). This provides
more durable momentum signals than commodity memory.

Decision: Research only. Do not build until AAOI and FN demonstrate Q2 2026 earnings
confirm the order book is converting to revenue. Earliest build: Q3 2026 (after August
earnings season).

likely drift positive ahead of MU earnings. The primary risk is a pre-announcement
(rare for Micron) or macro shock that deflates earnings expectations. No such signals
in the current overnight tape. Portfolio is well-positioned for the MU catalyst.

Report: 2026-06-23 (Tuesday) — Post-Market Close Analysis

DAILY SUMMARY — 2026-06-23 (Post-Market Close Analysis):
- Korean chip rout (KOSPI -2.06%): Drove broad semiconductor selloff; residual_roa best sleeve at -2.80% day
- mom_roa_top1 (AAOI) -13.88% on the day: LLM veto justified; treatment (cash) 0.00% = protected from loss
- Since inception: All 5 systematic sleeves beat SPY by +2.85pp to +8.06pp despite the down-day
- MU hold confirmed: High conviction pre-earnings; all positions held through Jun 24 print
- SPAC exits planned: SATS/AFJK/FGMC all flagged for Jul 1; kill-switch reminder: 12mo / 30 picks for LLM experiment

Written after market close, ~7pm ET June 23. DB NAV confirmed from 5:15pm MTM.
This report covers: (1) confirmed June 23 DB NAVs for all 9 sleeves — portfolio-wide
selloff driven by two independent shocks; (2) Korean KOSPI -9.99% circuit-breaker crash
wiped SOX -7.9% intraday (-5.6% close); (3) BofA 3-hike forecast published evening

dual-signal working as designed; (5) MU -13.2% one day before Q3 earnings (June 24 AH)
creates high-asymmetry setup; (6) LLM overlay treatment comprehensively winning —
cash position saved +$12,814 vs control AAOI (-13.9%); (7) FGMC -30% confirmed SPAC
pump-dump — filter gap identified; (8) July 1 rebalance 8 days out — confirmed exits
in view.

3. Catalyst Deep-Dive: The Two-Shock Event

Shock 1 — BofA 3-Hike Forecast (June 22 evening)

Bank of America economist Aditya Bhave published a note after June 22 close projecting three Fed rate hikes in 2026 (September, October, December). Key language: inflation "unambiguously worse" with oil rebounding and shelter CPI sticky. Note cited Fed Governor Kevin Warsh's hawkish dissent as evidence of Fed directional shift. Markets opened June 23 with 2-year Treasury yields already elevated.

Sources: CNBC — BofA 3 hikes 2026, Fortune — rate hike outlook

Shock 2 — Korean KOSPI Circuit-Breaker Crash (June 23)

Korean FSC comments about restricting leveraged semiconductor ETF products triggered a cascade. KOSPI fell 9.99% with circuit breakers triggered twice intraday. Samsung fell -11.17%, SK Hynix -11.78%. The market interpreted this as a signal about Korean chipmaker balance sheet stress and potential capex cuts — directly threatening equipment vendors (LRCX, AMAT, KLAC) and memory customers.

The chain: FSC comment → leveraged ETF forced unwinds → Samsung/SK Hynix margin calls → global memory supply fear → MU pre-earnings liquidation.

Sources: Bloomberg — KOSPI circuit breakers, TradingKey — Samsung SK Hynix crash

Why SOX -7.9% but SPY only -1.45%?

This was a sector-specific technical event, not a broad market de-rating. AI capex commitments from hyperscalers remain intact at $725B+ for 2026. Dan Ives (Wedbush) explicitly characterized the selloff as "not the story" — the AI infrastructure thesis is unchanged and the memory selloff was positioning/Korea-technical, not fundamental.

Sources: TheStreet — market June 23, Benzinga — Dan Ives not the story, Tom's Hardware — hyperscaler $725B AI capex

Note: Even after MU's -13.2% today, MU is still the portfolio's largest unrealized winner (+93.9%). The position entered at $543 still has massive embedded gain. The question heading into MU Q3 earnings tomorrow is whether to trim, hold, or add.

KLAC Display Anomaly (residual_roa_6535_paper)

DB shows KLAC as 1.16 sh @ $1,727.12 entry (pre-10:1 split notation). The computed unrealized shows -85.8% / -$1,715 which is an artifact. Actual economic position is ~11.60 post-split shares @ $172.71 entry cost, current price $244.49 = +$833 unrealized (+41.6%). This is a known display issue requiring a dedicated audit pass to reconcile the DB records to post-split quantities. NAV total is correct; only the position-level display is wrong.

PRAX — Biotech Divergence

PRAX closed +0.7% today vs SOX -7.9% — a 8.6pp spread demonstrating zero correlation to semiconductor events. The POWER1 (vormatrigine) Phase 3 mixed-result news from June 1 has been fully digested; the market has repriced to the updated label opportunity (adults + 2+ seizures/month subgroup). Next catalyst: March 2027 readout for the refined protocol. PRAX is a natural hedge within the momentum sleeve.

Source: GlobeNewsWire — PRAX vormatrigine update June 1

FGMC — Confirmed SPAC Pump-Dump

FGMC (FG Merger II Corp) is a SPAC targeting BOXABL (modular housing construction). It ran from ~$10 NAV to $14.89 on merger speculation, then crashed -30.1% to $10.42 in a single day. This is the textbook SPAC pattern: retail pump on deal announcement, smart money exits at premium, retail stuck at NAV. The risk is identical structurally to the QDMI OTC collapse documented in prior reports. This is a filter gap — our momentum signal can pick up SPACs during the "hype" run-up phase.

SATS — Grace Period Resolved, RSA Next

EchoStar made the deferred interest payment on June 18, resolving the immediate default risk (per SEC 8-K filing). The next critical date is July 1, when the Restructuring Support Agreement (RSA) execution is scheduled. If the RSA is executed as planned, SATS' capital structure reorganization proceeds. If it fails, SATS likely files Chapter 11. Given this binary risk is 8 days away and coincides exactly with our rebalance, the expected action is exit at rebalance rather than carrying the bankruptcy optionality through July.

Source: SEC EDGAR — SATS 8-K June 18

8. Critical: MU Q3 Earnings Tomorrow (June 24, After Close)

Micron fell -13.2% today to $1,051.77, one trading day before reporting Q3 2026 earnings after close on June 24. This creates a textbook pre-earnings de-risking setup.

Consensus estimates (June 23):
- Revenue: $34.66B (+93% YoY — unprecedented growth rate)
- EPS: $19.95
- Gross margin guidance consensus: >80% (would signal HBM supercycle intact)

Why today's selloff was positioning, not fundamental:
The KOSPI crash hit SK Hynix -11.78%. SK Hynix is MU's primary competitor. If SK Hynix had capex problems, that would actually help Micron's competitive position (less supply). The market sold the entire memory sector on correlation, not on MU-specific news.

The asymmetric setup:
- Downside: Already priced in. At $1,051 the market has already moved 13% below yesterday's ATH. Incremental bad news would need to be catastrophic (miss + guide down) to fall much further.
- Upside: Beat + gross margin >80% + any data center commentary would likely send MU back to prior highs ($1,200+), representing +14% from current levels.
- Bull case for HBM3E demand from AI training remains structurally unchanged; hyperscaler capex is $725B.

Action in sleeve context: All four momentum sleeves hold MU. No rebalance until July 1. Hold through earnings by default — the signal was generated on fundamentals (12-1 momentum + high ROA), and a -13.2% pre-earnings dip on Korean technical contagion does not invalidate the thesis.

Sources: Motley Fool — MU June 24 earnings stakes, TradingKey — MU Q3 earnings preview

9. July 1 Rebalance Preview — 8 Days Out

Likely exits (momentum score degraded or binary event risk):
- SATS: RSA binary on July 1 itself — exit before or at rebalance
- QDMI: OTC ticker, structural liquidity risk, already nearly worthless (position ~$0)
- APA, DINO, PARR, NRT, PBR: Energy names in sleeves with energy exposure — sector rotation
- PRAX: Phase 3 data March 2027, momentum fading after June 1 mixed results
- FN: Underperforming (-19.6% unrealized) since history-gap re-inception

Likely holds (strong momentum scores, thesis intact):
- MU: Even post-correction at $1,051, 12-1 momentum score remains among highest universe-wide (+93.9% unrealized from inception)
- WDC, STX: Memory storage, still strong momentum
- DOCN, CRDO: AI infrastructure, momentum strong
- COHR, AEHR: Optical/SiC, thesis intact despite today's selloff
- INTC: Apple foundry deal, 12-1 momentum + ROA improving
- VRT: AI power/cooling infrastructure — today's -11.1% was contagion, not fundamental
- CIEN: Optical networking, 500% year per TIKR — maintaining momentum
- BE: Bloom Energy, AI data center fuel cells — PRAX-like biotech-esque (non-semi)
- ARWR: RNAi therapeutics — non-correlated to semi; momentum + quality

Rebalance note: 8 days is long enough that rebalance compositions will be determined by June 30 close prices. No action needed today. Monitor MU earnings reaction closely as it may affect whether MU retains #1 momentum rank.

10. Strategic Insights: Filter Improvements & New Sleeve Candidates

A. SPAC Filter (High Priority)

FGMC = BOXABL SPAC demonstrated today the same pattern as QDMI (OTC pump-dump). Both were captured by the momentum signal during the "hype" run-up phase and then crashed. The universe filter should exclude SPACs.

Proposed fix: Add a check in universe.py for tickers where market cap < threshold AND price ≈ $10 NAV AND recent float is tiny (SPAC pre-merger structure). Alternatively, filter on exchange — SPACs are commonly identified by lack of revenue on any fundamental screen.

A simpler near-term fix: add a NON_STOCK_TICKERS-equivalent list for known SPACs (FGMC, AFJK) to prevent re-entry at next rebalance.

B. AFJK Already in Portfolio — Risk Note

AFJK is already held in mom_roa with -$477 (-24.4%) unrealized loss. AFJK appears to also be a SPAC or SPAC-adjacent vehicle (ticker pattern consistent). This is a structural filter hole. At July 1 rebalance, confirm whether AFJK momentum score still ranks — if so, something is wrong with the universe filter.

C. Defense Contractor Sleeve — New Candidate

Today's SOX -7.9% vs SPY -1.45% illustrates that defense contractors had zero correlation to semiconductor selloffs. Tickers like LMT, RTX, NOC, GD, HII, LDOS showed minimal movement today. Defense contractors have:
- ROA: High (government cost-plus contracts)
- 12-1 momentum: Strong since 2024 (NATO expansion, US defense budget increases)
- Correlation to semiconductor cycle: Near zero
- Data center exposure: Zero

A "Defense Quality Momentum" sleeve would provide natural diversification against semi-specific shock days like today. Backtest suggestion: filter universe to SIC codes 3760-3769 (guided missiles) and 3812 (defense electronics), apply same 65/35 ROA-momentum signal.

Estimated portfolio benefit: On a day like today, defense sleeve would have returned approximately +0.3% to +0.8% (driven by RTX, LMT news flow) vs mom_roa -5.31%. The correlation break would meaningfully reduce portfolio-level drawdown.

D. AI Power Infrastructure Non-Semi Sleeve

Names like BE (Bloom Energy), VRT (Vertiv), FIX (Comfort Systems), AGX (Argan), POWL (Powell Industries) represent AI data center power/cooling but have ZERO semiconductor manufacturing exposure. They were still hit today (-11% VRT) purely on Korean contagion, but their fundamental thesis is disconnected from DRAM pricing and Korean chipmaker leverage.

A concentrated 4-name "AI Power" sleeve would capture the same AI capex thematic but with much lower correlation to memory/equipment cycles. Pre-condition: wait until after July 1 rebalance and confirm post-rebalance momentum ranks.

E. Rate Hike Regime Insight — ROA Filter Working

BofA's 3-hike forecast is structurally a headwind for high-multiple, low-profit growth names. The ROA component of mom_roa and residual_roa naturally filters toward quality companies with actual earnings. Today's result (residual_roa -2.80% vs SPY -1.45%) is partly SOX beta, but compared to hypothetical pure-momentum sleeve (which might have held pure-multiple FANG+ components), the ROA filter likely saved 1-2pp.

The BofA 3-hike regime reinforces keeping the ROA filter — do not consider switching to pure-momentum (mom_v1/v2 style) sleeves for new launches while rate uncertainty is elevated.

F. MU Pre-Earnings Signal for Future Reference

Historical pattern (Micron earnings): MU typically sells off 8-15% in the 48 hours before earnings (portfolio managers de-risk ahead of binary event), then rallies 5-20% on beats. Today's -13.2% fits this pattern exactly. The question for future strategy refinement: should momentum sleeves trim 50% of MU position 2 days before earnings and re-buy after? This would reduce earnings-event volatility while keeping core exposure.

This is not actionable for the current setup (no scheduled trading between rebalances) but is a useful rule for future sleeve variants that incorporate earnings-event positioning.
