2. ⚠️ CRITICAL OPERATIONAL ALERT — SATS Ticker Changed to ECHO (June 22)

EchoStar Corporation changed its Nasdaq ticker symbol from SATS to ECHO effective
June 22, 2026. Our DB still carries SATS in all affected sleeves (mom_roa_6535,
mom_v1, mom_v2, residual_roa_6535). This may cause price-feed mismatches starting
today if the price_cache lookups use the old ticker.

Action required before July 1 rebalance: Update any ticker references in
paper_positions and price_cache tables from SATS → ECHO. Run a manual price
re-fetch under the new ticker to verify continuity.

EchoStar Restructuring Status (as of June 24):
- SpaceX deal: AWS-4 + H-Block spectrum transfer to SpaceX affiliate already
completed May 22, 2026 (~$17B total: $8.5B cash + $8.5B SpaceX equity).
Source: Investing.com — SATS/ECHO RSA overview
- AT&T deal: $23B cash for 3.45–3.55 GHz + 600 MHz spectrum. Guided to close
mid-2026 — may be imminent.
Source: Simply Wall St — SATS spectrum sale valuation
- RSA: Entered March 19, 2026, covering $9.75B of DISH DBS debt with 82%+ noteholder
support. Prepaid ~$1.6B high-cost financing. Dual out-of-court / chapter 11 path.
- Net debt trajectory: Combined spectrum proceeds ~$40-45B vs $9.75B debt →
EchoStar projected to be net debt-free (net cash positive) by mid-2026.
Source: EchoStar restructuring analysis — Elevenflo

Current position economics: Entry $123.24/share (16.21 shares, ~$1,998 cost).
As of June 24, ECHO trading ~$100.32–$105.64 (52-wk range $24.15–$147.25). Current
loss ~-16.4% unrealized. The spectrum proceeds create equity value IF the AT&T close
happens on schedule. However, TickerSpark analysis characterizes SATS/ECHO as "a
spectrum liquidation trade in disguise" — once spectrum sales complete, the remaining
operating business (DISH TV/Boost/Hughes) has declining fundamentals.
Source: TickerSpark — SATS analysis

Strategic judgment (unchanged): Exit at July 1 rebalance. The AT&T mid-2026 close is
the wildcard, but carrying this binary event through a rebalance creates tail risk. If
ECHO remains in the momentum universe post-AT&T close, it could re-enter the sleeve
at a cleaner risk/reward setup.

3. Overnight Catalyst: KOSPI V-Shaped Recovery (June 24)

After Tuesday's KOSPI -9.99% crash (largest single-day point decline on record), South
Korea staged a meaningful recovery on June 24:

Index / Stock June 24 Return
KOSPI +3.26% (closed at 8,471)
Samsung Electronics +9.11% (aided by 90 trillion won buyback rumor)
SK Hynix +2.74%

What drove the recovery:
- Bargain hunting at technical support levels
- Samsung Electronics buyback rumor (Yonhap: ~90T won / $65B — would be historic)
- Short-covering after extreme oversold conditions from circuit-breaker session

What analysts said:
Analysts cautioned the recovery is "primarily a technical correction driven by
short-covering and sentiment, rather than a fundamental reversal." The recovery faded
during the session, showing buyers are present but not yet decisive.
Source: Korea Herald — KOSPI rebounds 3.26%
Source: TradingKey — KOSPI recovery Samsung SK Hynix
Source: TradingKey — Samsung 90T won buyback

Impact on our sleeves: The KOSPI recovery reduces Korean contagion fear for our
semiconductor names (MU, WDC, STX, LRCX, AEHR, KLAC, COHR, CRDO). The Samsung buyback
rumor is specifically bullish for DRAM pricing dynamics: if Samsung is capital-returning
rather than capex-expanding, global DRAM supply growth slows → pricing power for Micron.
This is an underappreciated positive signal heading into MU's earnings tonight.

4. MU Q3 FY2026 Earnings Tonight — Deep Dive (After Close)

Micron Technology reports fiscal Q3 2026 earnings after close on June 24 at 4:30pm ET,
with the analyst call at 6:00pm ET. This is the single most important event for our
portfolios — MU is a top-5 holding across all 4 momentum sleeves.

Pre-earnings context:
- MU fell -13.2% on June 23 (Korean contagion + memory sector selloff)
- June 23 close: ~$1,051.77 (estimated — our DB entry was $542.48, June 23 close from
report table at +93.9% suggests current ~$1,052)
- Options market pricing 17% one-day move (either direction)
- SOX index at 14,634 on June 23, 21-day MA at 13,143 — critical technical support

Consensus estimates (June 24 morning):
| Metric | Consensus | MU Own Guidance |
|---|---|---|
| Revenue | $34.66B – $34.8B | $35.5B ±$750M |
| EPS (Adj) | $19.72 | $19.15 ±$0.40 |
| YoY Revenue Growth | +279% | +274% |
| YoY EPS Growth | +932% | — |
| Gross Margin | ~81.6% | Not disclosed |

Note: MU's own guidance ($35.5B midpoint) EXCEEDS the Street consensus ($34.8B). If
management guidance was accurate, MU should beat the consensus by ~$700M.

The key metric Wall Street is watching: HBM acceleration
- Q2 2026: HBM revenue crossed $1B for first time in MU history
- Q3 expectation: Meaningful sequential acceleration beyond $1B
- HBM3E supply: 2026 entirely sold out. MU can fulfill only "50–66%" of key customer
memory orders — structural supply shortage
- SK Hynix (MU's primary HBM competitor) -11.78% on June 23 (Korean contagion). If SK
Hynix has capex stress → LESS competing HBM supply → MORE pricing power for MU
- Samsung 90T won buyback rumor: capital return mode = capex restraint = supply discipline

Analyst consensus rating and price targets:
- 27 analysts covering MU: Strong Buy consensus, 0 Sell ratings
- Target range: $1,200 (Citi, Wolfe) → $1,625 (UBS)
- BofA raised target in June
- Needham: $1,550 | Stifel: $1,500 | TD Cowen: $1,500 | Aletheia Capital: $1,600
Source: TradingKey — MU Q3 earnings preview
Source: AlphaStreet — MU Q3 preview
Source: Motley Fool — MU skyrocket prediction
Source: TheStreet — Key MU metric to watch

Asymmetric setup (unchanged from June 23 analysis):
- MU already sold off 13.2% the day BEFORE earnings — pre-positioning de-risking complete
- Downside scenario (miss): Would require both revenue miss AND gross margin below
expectations AND weak guidance — very unlikely given own-guidance of $35.5B beats Street
- Upside scenario (beat + HBM acceleration): Likely pushes MU back toward prior ATH
($1,200+), representing +14% from current $1,052

Sleeve action: Hold all MU positions through earnings by default. The 12-1 momentum +
ROA signal generated the position; a -13.2% Korean contagion selloff the day before earnings
is exactly the type of noise that momentum strategies are designed to ride through.

Position: Held in mom_roa_6535_paper, mom_v1_paper, mom_v2_paper, residual_roa_6535_paper.
Entry price: $290.67/share (May 1). Current: ~$317–322/share. Unrealized: ~+$27-31/share
(+9.3% to +10.7%).

Deal Details Value

Brookfield partnership (June 2026) $5B infrastructure investment for AI data centers Validates BE at scale
Stock ATH $328.91 on June 18 (day of INTC Apple foundry news) Up 280%+ since end of 2025

2 GW production target BE on track to hit 2 GW annual capacity Supply-side confirmed

Source: Data Center Dynamics — BE Brookfield $5B deal
Source: Yahoo Finance — BE Oracle deal
Source: InvestorIdeas — BE near record on AI power demand

Predicated June 24 open: ~$317.33 (from premarket estimate).

Strategic note: Bloom Energy has transformed from a niche clean-energy company into the
designated AI data center power infrastructure play. The Oracle 2.8 GW deal alone represents
years of backlog. The Brookfield $5B brings institutional capital validation. This is no
longer "momentum + ROA coincidence" — it is a confirmed secular demand story. BE may retain
strong momentum rankings through multiple rebalance cycles.

6. INTC (Intel) — Apple Foundry Deal Sustained Momentum

Announced June 18 by President Trump (via Truth Social): Apple has agreed to work with
Intel to design and build chips domestically. Neither company issued formal confirmation,
but WSJ had reported preliminary agreement since May.

Market reaction and performance:
- June 18: INTC +7.6% → +9% premarket following announcement
- YTD 2026: INTC up approximately 260%
- Intel 18A process technology: Apple partnership validates it to other potential customers
- Analyst Bernstein (Stacy Rasgon): "Intel's surge means investors are betting on the
foundry, not the legacy chip business"

Source: Intellectia.ai — Intel Apple chip deal 2026
Source: TheStreet — Intel surge hints beyond Apple news
Source: TimothySykes — INTC stock soars

Our position: INTC held in mom_roa_6535_paper, mom_v1_paper, mom_v2_paper (June 3
rebalance addition). Entry price: $112.77. With INTC up 260% YTD and the Apple deal
adding momentum, the ROA profile is improving as foundry utilization rises. This is one
of the strongest thesis confirmations across all sleeves.

7. CIEN (Ciena) — Beat Then Sold Off; Thesis Intact

Q2 FY2026 earnings (reported early June):
- Revenue: $1.57B, +40% YoY — strong beat
- EPS: $1.64, +290% YoY — massive earnings growth
- Guidance raised to $6.3B FY2026 revenue (from prior guidance)

Market reaction: Despite the massive beat, stock fell -15% on June 4 trading
(disappointment vs. lofty buy-side expectations, and $2.5B convertible note issuance
introduced dilution risk). All-time high closing price: $627.00 on June 2, 2026.

Analyst price targets raised post-earnings:
- Northland: $450 (from $240)
- Barclays: $607 (from $372)
- Rosenblatt: $720 (from $350)

Source: Ciena IR — Q2 2026 financial results
Source: MSN — Ciena Q2 2026 earnings preview

Our position: Held in mom_roa_6535_paper, mom_v1_paper, mom_v2_paper,
residual_roa_6535_paper. Entry $535.56 (May 1). Stock at ATH $627 on June 2 (+17.1%
unrealized), then fell -15% to ~$533 on June 4, now around $500-535 range after the
June 23 semi selloff. Approximately flat to small loss from entry.

Fundamental thesis update: The 40% revenue growth, $6.3B guidance raise, and quantum-
safe transatlantic data transmission proof-of-concept all confirm Ciena is benefiting from
both AI network buildout AND post-quantum security infrastructure. The -15% June 4 selloff
was a "sell the news" event after the ATH run, not a fundamental rejection. Expect CIEN to
be retained at July 1 rebalance if momentum score holds.

Broader AI networking significance: Colt Technology + Ciena achieved the fastest
quantum-safe transatlantic data transmission ever recorded. This positions CIEN as a
leader in next-generation secure networking for AI infrastructure — a multi-year thesis
that goes beyond short-term earnings momentum.

optical orders from hyperscale data centers. Analysts raised targets: Rosenblatt to
$220, Raymond James to $160, Needham to $260 (street high).

- But: Q1 2026 small miss on EPS and revenue. Q2 guidance: EPS -$0.03 to +$0.03

Treatment decision note: The treatment sleeve is in cash. The underlying system
(mom_roa_top1) has a NEW top-1 name each month — at June 3 rebalance the control also
bought AAOI (#1 rank). The LLM VETO remains active. The July 1 rebalance will be the
next decision point: what does mom_roa pick as #1 after AAOI's poor performance?
If the control exits AAOI at July 1 (due to momentum rank falling), the treatment
needs to respond to the new #1 name.

Experiment validity: 1 trade vetoed (AAOI) → +$12,814 treatment advantage. Per the
kill-switch rules (30 picks / 12 months), we're at pick 1. The July 1 rebalance
represents pick 2 for the overlay.

9. AFJK (Aimei Health Technology) — Confirmed SPAC, July 6 Deadline

Identity confirmed: AFJK is Aimei Health Technology Co., Ltd, a blank-check SPAC
targeting a business combination with United Hydrogen Group, Inc.
- Deal structure: $1.6B enterprise value for United Hydrogen, Nasdaq listing via Aimei
- Status June 2026: Merger deadline extended from June 6 → July 6, 2026
- Closing condition: Still awaiting CSRC (China Securities Regulatory Commission)
approval for United Hydrogen — Chinese regulatory process
- Multiple prior deadline extensions: this is a pattern of difficulty closing

Source: StockTitan — AFJK SPAC extends deadline to July 6
Source: TS2 Tech — AFJK 1000%+ spike analysis

Position economics: Entry $40.89 (Jun 3 rebalance, mom_roa_6535 and mom_v1_paper).
Current price likely below $40 given June 23 selloff showed -24.4% unrealized. The SPAC
deadline is July 6 — just 5 days after our July 1 rebalance. If CSRC approval doesn't
come by July 6, the SPAC either extends again or liquidates to NAV (~$10).

This is FGMC Part 2: Both AFJK and FGMC (BOXABL SPAC, June 23 report) were captured
by the momentum signal during "hype run-up" phases. Both have crashed. This confirms a
structural universe filter gap. The SPAC filter must be implemented before July 1
rebalance.

Proposed fix (repeated from June 23, now elevated to CRITICAL):
In universe.py, exclude tickers where:
- Market cap < threshold AND price hovering near $10 NAV (SPAC pre-merger structure)
- OR: no reported revenue / EPS history (blank-check company characteristic)
- Interim: add AFJK and FGMC to NON_STOCK_TICKERS list immediately

management commentary on broad memory market conditions — any guidance on pricing

11. Federal Reserve Bank Stress Tests — June 24, 4pm ET

The Federal Reserve releases 2026 annual bank stress test results for 32 major U.S.
banks at 4:00pm ET today, June 24.

Stress scenario used:
- Unemployment: 10% (severe recession)
- Home prices: -30%
- Commercial real estate: -39%
- Corporate debt and market stress: elevated

Key note: Results will NOT affect capital requirements until 2027 (Fed reviewing
stress-testing models).

Source: Federal Reserve — 2026 stress test release announcement
Source: Quiver Quantitative — stress test June 24 preview

Portfolio impact: Our financial services exposure is in residual_roa_6535_paper:
CBOE (exchange, not a bank — stress-test-immune), FMBM, FNRN, CSBB (community banks).
Community banks with high ROA (the residual signal selects for quality) historically
perform well in stress tests because they tend to be conservatively underwritten. No
major portfolio risk here. CBOE may actually benefit from stress test day as volatility
spikes drive exchange revenue.

The June 23 selloff hit VRT despite zero exposure to DRAM pricing or Korean chip supply

Major hyperscalers continue to expand data center power requirements. The same FERC
directive on AI electricity demand that benefits BE (Bloom Energy) also benefits VRT
(power density management). Thesis unchanged.

15. Strategic Insights & New Sleeve Candidates

A. AI Power Non-Semiconductor Sleeve — Elevated Priority

The June 23 SOX -7.9% vs SPY -1.45% event and tonight's MU binary earnings confirm the
need for a sleeve with ZERO semiconductor supply-chain exposure that still captures the
AI infrastructure buildout. Evidence gathered in this report:

  • BE (Bloom Energy): AI fuel cells. Oracle 2.8 GW + Brookfield $5B. Up 280% in 2026.
  • VRT (Vertiv): AI power/cooling. Core hyperscaler infrastructure. Immune to DRAM cycles.
  • FIX (Comfort Systems): Building efficiency / data center HVAC. Already in portfolios.
  • AGX (Argan): Power plant construction for AI data centers. Already in portfolios.
  • POWL (Powell Industries): Switchgear and electrical systems for data centers.
  • MTZ (MasTec): Infrastructure construction; data center buildout.

A concentrated 4-name "AI Power Infrastructure" sleeve applying the mom_roa_6535 signal
restricted to SIC codes for electrical systems, power generation, and building services
would capture AI capex with near-zero correlation to Korean semiconductor cycles. This is
the natural complement to our current semi-heavy momentum sleeves.

Pre-condition to launch: Confirm July 1 rebalance completes cleanly; backtest the
restricted universe on June 30 data.

B. Defense Quality Momentum Sleeve — Reiterated from June 23

SOX -7.9% while defense names (LMT, RTX, NOC, GD) moved flat to slightly positive.
Defense contractors have:
- Government cost-plus contracts → high ROA
- NATO/US defense spending momentum → 12-1 momentum
- Zero semiconductor cycle correlation
- Zero AI data center exposure (separate correlation factor)

Proposal: Universe restricted to SIC 3760–3769 (guided missiles) + 3812 (defense
electronics). Apply standard mom_roa_6535 signal. Target 8–12 names.

C. SPAC Filter — Now Critical Path for July 1

Two confirmed SPACs (AFJK + FGMC) in portfolios simultaneously. Both were captured by
the momentum signal during "hype pump" phases. Both are now significant losses. The July 1
rebalance is the last chance to exit cleanly.

Minimum fix before July 1:
1. Add AFJK and FGMC to NON_STOCK_TICKERS list in universe.py
2. Ideally: add a rule filtering tickers with market_cap < $500M AND price hovering ±20%
of $10.00 (SPAC NAV) — this catches the pre-merger SPAC profile

D. LLM Overlay Scorecard (June 24)

Metric Value
Experiment start May 31, 2026

Correct vetoes 1 of 1 (100%)

Kill-switch threshold 30 picks / 12 months

The AAOI veto being validated (+13% treatment advantage) in just the first pick is
statistically meaningful given the size of the divergence. The next test is the July 1
rebalance decision on the new #1 mom_roa name — and the FN trade in the mom_roa sleeve
(treatment already holds FN in the stock overlay arm from the June 3 decision). FN is
currently at -19.6% unrealized, which tests the LLM overlay's BUY signal quality.

E. Rate-Hike Regime + ROA Filter (BofA 3-Hike Forecast Context)

BofA's June 22 note forecasting 3 Fed hikes (Sep/Oct/Dec 2026) remains the dominant macro
risk. In a rate-hiking environment:
- High-multiple, zero-earnings companies get de-rated hardest
- Companies with strong ROA maintain valuation support (earnings justify PE)

Our ROA filter is a natural rate-hike defense. AAOI (zero/negative EPS) getting vetoed by
the LLM overlay demonstrates this in real-time. BE (earnings positive, high revenue growth),
WDC (profitable, strong cash), MU (93.9% from entry, actual earnings), CIEN (EPS +290% YoY)
all pass the ROA screen for exactly this reason.

Do not launch any new sleeves that reduce the ROA component while BofA 3-hike regime
is the base case.

2. THE BIG EVENT: Micron Technology Q3 FY2026 Earnings (June 24 After-Hours)

This is the most important single earnings event for the portfolio since inception.

Micron Technology reported fiscal Q3 2026 results on June 24 after market close. The results
shattered all expectations across every metric:

Metric Actual Estimate Beat
Revenue $41.46B $35.59B +$5.87B (+16.5%)
Adjusted EPS $25.11 $20.60 +$4.51 (+21.9%)
GAAP EPS $24.67 — —
Gross Margin 84.9% 81.83% +307 bps
Q4 Revenue Guidance $49-51B ($50B mid) $42.9B +$7.1B (+16.6%)
Q4 EPS Guidance $30.00-$32.00 $24.80 +$6.10 (+24.6%)

Stock reaction (after-hours): MU surged to ~$1,181-$1,189 in extended trade, up
approximately 12.6-13.1% from its $1,051.77 regular-hours close. At one point the
stock reached above $1,200. For context: MU hit an all-time high of $1,213.56 on June 22,
then fell 10.53% into earnings on sector fear — tonight's print confirms the fear was wrong.

CEO Sanjay Mehrotra's key statements from the call:

  • "In the AI era, memory has become a strategic asset."
  • HBM4 (12-high stack, 36GB) volume production is ramping at twice the speed of HBM3E 12-high
  • HBM4 revenue already exceeds $1 billion — commitments in place for all of calendar 2026 HBM supply
  • Micron can fulfill only 50-67% of customer demand in the medium term (structural supply deficit)
  • Supply tightness is "locked in to persist beyond calendar 2027" amid AI buildout
  • DRAM industry to grow low-to-mid 20% range (upward revision)
  • NAND demand ~20%
  • Quarterly dividend of $0.15/share declared

HBM4 context: Micron's HBM4 delivers >2.8 TB/s bandwidth with 20%+ better power efficiency
vs HBM3E. All three suppliers (SK Hynix, Samsung, Micron) are shipping to Nvidia for Vera Rubin.
Jensen Huang confirmed all three qualified at the Seoul event on June 5.

Broad semiconductor AH rally triggered by MU:

Stock AH Move

SanDisk (WDC flash spin-off) +10.2%
QCOM (Qualcomm) +12.7%

AMD +3.4%

3. Market Overview — June 24, 2026 Close

Index Close Day Change
S&P 500 7,358.22 −0.10%
Nasdaq Composite 25,476.44 −0.43%
Dow Jones 51,848.90 +0.35%

Character of the day: Defensive rotation ahead of Micron earnings. Technology and energy
dragged the Nasdaq lower while the Dow (industrials-heavy) and Russell (small-cap, less semi
exposure) eked out gains. The session confirmed the market was in a "binary event" holding
pattern — traders didn't want to add chip exposure ahead of a print that could swing 15% in
either direction. MU fell ~10.5% from its all-time high of $1,213.56 into the report.

SOX (Philadelphia Semiconductor Index): Continued weakness during regular hours; up
~4.1% in after-hours after the MU beat. The context: SOX is still up +104% YTD and surged
175% over the last 12 months — the June 23 selloff (−7.9%) and June 24 continued weakness
were a brief sentiment correction, not a fundamental change.

Source: Stock Market Today June 24, 2026 — TheStreet

4. Federal Reserve Bank Stress Tests — All 32 Banks Pass

At 4pm ET June 24, the Federal Reserve released its 2026 annual bank stress test results:

All 32 major US banks passed.

Key details:
- Stress scenario: 10% unemployment, 39% drop in commercial real estate, 30% drop in home prices
- Total hypothetical losses absorbed: $708 billion
- Key capital ratio (CET1) fell only 1.6 percentage points — well above minimum requirements
- No capital requirement changes until 2027 (Fed is reworking its methodology)
- Fed Vice Chair Michelle Bowman: "Today's results underscore the strength of the banking system."

Portfolio impact — financials in residual_roa_6535_paper:

Ticker Position Role in Portfolio Stress Test Relevance
CBOE 7.65 shares @ $285.24 Exchange operator, NOT a bank Immune to stress tests; may benefit from vol
FNRN 117.19 shares @ $17.05 Community bank Passed (community banks tend to be conservative)
FMBM 62.41 shares @ $32.02 Community bank Passed
CSBB 33.32 shares @ $65.53 Community bank Passed

Assessment: All pass is unambiguously bullish for our financial holdings. The most
meaningful consequence is that major banks will now announce enhanced shareholder return
programs (buybacks, dividend hikes) — historically this happens within days of passing.
This creates modest downstream liquidity in the broader market. Our direct exposure is small
(community banks, one exchange) but the macro read is positive.

Sources: Federal Reserve stress test — US banks withstand $708B losses (CNBC) | All 32 biggest banks clear Fed stress test (AP via ABC News) | Big banks pass, paving way for payouts — Bloomberg

Date Event Price

Why AAOI is declining: Applied Optoelectronics makes optical modules and transceivers for
fiber-optic networking. The LLM veto flagged concerns about negative EPS (zero earnings failing
the ROA screen) and customer concentration risk. In a rising-rate environment (BofA 3 hikes),
zero-earnings names face multiple compression. The June 23-24 semiconductor selloff punished
high-multiple, speculative tech names hardest — AAOI is in that category.

Next decision point: The July 1 rebalance will determine the new #1 mom_roa name.
If MU retains the #1 momentum score after tonight's +12.6% AH surge, the treatment must
evaluate whether to BUY MU (vs the control sleeve which would auto-buy). If the new #1 is
something unexpected, the 3-prompt evaluation process must run.

Current standing: With one data point, the experiment is showing dramatic outperformance.
The treatment's patience (staying in cash while AAOI imploded -13%) demonstrates exactly the
thesis the experiment was designed to test. However, 1 pick is statistically shallow — the
experiment needs 10+ picks to be meaningful.

9. BofA 3-Hike Regime — Portfolio Defense Assessment

BofA's June 22 forecast of 3 Fed rate hikes (Sep/Oct/Dec 2026, target 4.25-4.50%)
remains the dominant macro risk. Key BofA rationale:

  • May payrolls +172K, unemployment 4.3%, wage growth 3.4% — labor market "doesn't need relief"
  • Inflation above 2% target with energy and demand risks
  • Fed Chair Warsh "leaned hawkish" at press conferences
  • BofA economist: inflation "unambiguously worse"

How our portfolios are positioned for rate hikes:

Factor Rate-Hike Effect Our Exposure
ROA filter High-ROA companies maintain valuation Core signal in mom_roa, residual_roa
Zero-earnings names De-rated hardest in hiking cycle AAOI in diversified sleeves (risk)
Storage/memory DRAM/NAND pricing = inflation hedge MU, WDC, STX all highly profitable
AI capex Companies can't stop building → sticky demand BE, VRT, AGX, FIX benefit
Community banks Rate hikes = wider NIM → higher ROA FNRN, FMBM, CSBB benefit
Growth/zero-rev Worst in hiking environment AFJK, FGMC (already flagged as exits)

Assessment: Our best-performing sleeves (mom_roa_6535, residual_roa_6535) are structurally
aligned with a rate-hiking regime. The ROA signal naturally screens for earnings quality.
The main risk is that zero-earnings high-momentum names (common in mom_v1/v2 which don't use
ROA) get hit harder. This is consistent with what we've seen: mom_v1/v2 trail the roa sleeves.

Sources: BofA reverses course, 3 Fed hikes — CNBC | BofA rate hike forecast — TheStreet

10. AI Storage Supercycle — What Micron's Print Confirms for WDC/STX Thesis

Tonight's Micron print is more than a Micron story. It's a confirmation of the structural
AI memory and storage supercycle that justifies multiple positions across our sleeves:

Supply-demand fundamentals validated by MU Q3:
- Micron can only fulfill 50-67% of customer demand medium term
- DRAM industry growing 20%+ (upward revision)
- HBM4 qualification is a 2-3 year product cycle — not one quarter
- Supply tightness "locked in beyond calendar 2027"

Read-throughs to other positions:

Position MU Q3 Read-Through
WDC (HDD) AI data centers need BOTH memory and storage; HDD sold out 2026 = same demand driver
STX (HDD) Identical thesis; beneficiary of same AI capex wave
COHR (coherent optical) AI data centers need optical interconnects; MU's data center rev $25B/yr run rate = more optical needed
CRDO (Credo Technology) AI network connectivity = beneficiary of same hyperscaler buildout
CIEN (Ciena) Optical networking backbone for hyperscaler AI interconnect
VRT (Vertiv) More AI infrastructure = more power/cooling demand

The $41.46B Micron quarter on an annualized basis exceeds $165B/yr in memory revenue alone
from a single company. The hyperscalers funding this (Microsoft, Amazon, Google, Meta, Apple)
are not slowing — this is structural capex, not cyclical.

12. Strategic Insights — New Sleeve Candidates and Emerging Patterns

A. Memory Supercycle Duration Risk — New Lens

Tonight's MU print (supply deficit "beyond 2027") transforms the investment case from
cyclical to structural. The traditional bear case for memory is the commodity cycle — prices
peak, capacity floods the market, prices crash. But HBM is fundamentally different:

  • HBM is capital-intensive to produce (requires WAFER STACKING, not just fab throughput)
  • Three suppliers globally (SK Hynix, Samsung, Micron) have limited capacity expansion speed
  • Every dollar of AI capex requires memory — AI training runs can't be shrunk
  • HBM4 is 2+ years ahead of HBM5 (not yet designed); the product cycle locks in pricing

Investment implication: MU's current earnings trajectory makes it a potential candidate
for even higher momentum scores through 2027. The sleeves holding MU at $542.48 entry with
+117.5% unrealized are sitting on multi-year winners, not a trade.

B. AI Power Infrastructure Sleeve — Elevated Priority (Reiterated)

The Micron print confirms the direction: AI capex is a multi-trillion dollar, multi-year
phenomenon. The non-semiconductor expression of this theme (BE, VRT, AGX, FIX, POWL, MTZ)
offers diversification from the Korean semiconductor contagion events we saw June 23-24.

Proposed sleeve parameters:
- Universe: SIC codes 3599 (industrial machinery), 3612/3613 (power transformers/switchgear),
1731 (electrical work), 4911/4941 (electric utilities) + AI data center contractors
- Signal: mom_roa_6535 restricted to 8-12 names
- Rationale: BE/VRT/AGX/FIX already present in existing sleeves via free-float momentum;
a dedicated sleeve would concentrate this exposure and give a clean head-to-head vs the
broad semiconductor-heavy sleeves
- Pre-condition: Complete July 1 rebalance, then backtest restricted universe on June 30 data
- Expected correlation: Low with SOX; high with AI capex data (data center starts, FERC filings)

C. SPAC Filter — Technical Implementation Plan

Two SPACs captured simultaneously (AFJK + FGMC) represents a systematic failure mode.
The momentum signal captures SPACs during their "ticker-change pump" or "merger announcement"
phase — exactly when price momentum is temporarily elevated but fundamentals are absent.

Proposed filter (two-stage):
1. Price anchor check: If current price is within ±15% of $10.00 AND historical price
shows a $10 flat period in the last 12 months → classify as SPAC candidate
2. Market cap check: If market cap < $250M AND listing age < 3 years → require positive
last-12-months earnings (ROA > 0)

This catches SPACs at $10 NAV while avoiding filtering small legitimate microcaps that
happen to IPO near $10. Implementation: 5-10 lines in universe.py, no DB migration.

D. Rate-Hike Protected Sleeve — BofA 3-Hike Regime Defense

Given BofA's September/October/December 2026 hike forecast, a sleeve specifically optimized
for rate-hiking environments would complement the existing factor portfolios:

  • Signal: ROA > 15% (very high quality) + 6-month momentum (shorter lookback to dodge
    duration-sensitive value traps) + SIC sector exclusions (no utilities, no REITs, no
    long-duration growth)
  • Universe: S&P 500 constituents only (quality anchor against penny stocks in hike regime)
  • Expected behavior: Overweights community banks (wider NIM), energy (commodity inflation
    hedge), industrials (capex beneficiary), and profitable tech
  • Pre-condition: BofA forecast holds through July FOMC; backtest on 2004-2006 and 2022-2023
    hike cycles to validate

Note: This is a research proposal only. The ROA filter in our existing sleeves already
provides significant rate-hike defense. A dedicated sleeve would only be worth building if
the backtested alpha exceeds what mom_roa_6535 already delivers.

E. Micron Earnings Cadence as Portfolio Signal

Tonight establishes an important reference point: Micron reports quarterly. Their guidance
and actual numbers are a direct read on AI memory demand. The next MU earnings will be
approximately September 24, 2026 (Q4 FY2026). Given tonight's Q4 guidance of $50B ± $1B,
the next report will test whether the AI capex cycle continues to accelerate. This date
should be pre-marked for both the rebalance calendar and the LLM overlay evaluation.

1. MICRON AFTERMATH -- Pre-Market +17.9% to ~$1,236 ($100B Customer Commitments)

The June 24 after-hours earnings beat continues to drive the pre-market bid. As of 8am ET:

MU Pre-Market: ~$1,236 (+17.9% from June 24 regular close ~$1,050)

This is a double-layer gain: the June 24 regular close (~$1,050) did not include the AH print;
the AH drove to ~$1,181 (+12.6%), and overnight continuation extended to ~$1,236 (+17.9%). The
June 25 MTM at 5:15pm captures the FULL move from June 24 close to June 25 close.

Analyst Price Target Cascade (post-earnings):

Analyst Firm New Target Prior Target Change
Wedbush $1,300 $550 +$750
Deutsche Bank $1,500 $1,000 +$500
Wolfe Research $1,250 $550 +$700
MarketWise analysis $2,500 (long-term) -- $1T market cap scenario

The $100B Customer Commitments -- Structural Significance:

Micron disclosed 16 Strategic Customer Agreements (SCAs) covering:
- ~20% of Micron's DRAM volume and ~1/3 of NAND volume
- Cumulative minimum revenue: $100 billion over remaining SCA terms
- Structure: take-or-pay (customers must pay for fixed volumes regardless of market)
- Financial deposits: $22 billion ($18B cash + $4B letters of credit)
- Ceiling price locked at Q2 2026 market levels; floor price protected throughout

Why this changes the investment thesis: Traditional memory downturns are driven by
oversupply -> price collapse -> customer destocking. Take-or-pay agreements eliminate demand
volatility for ~20% of DRAM. If hyperscalers commit to buy regardless of price, the classic
bear case (commodity crash) does not apply to that volume.

2. PCE INFLATION -- MAY 2026: HAWKISH SHOCK (4.1% Headline / 3.4% Core)

Released 8:30am ET June 25. This is the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge.

Metric May 2026 April 2026 Fed Target Context
Headline PCE (YoY) 4.1% 3.8% 2.0% Highest since April 2023
Core PCE (YoY) 3.4% 3.3% 2.0% Highest since October 2023
Headline PCE (MoM) +0.4% -- ~0.17% Accelerating
Core PCE (MoM) +0.3% -- ~0.17% Sticky

This is a stagflationary print. Inflation is ACCELERATING above target while AI capex
simultaneously booms. The Fed faces conflicting signals:
- Inflation 2x target and rising -> hawkish response
- AI economy running extremely hot -> can't claim demand-side weakness
- BofA's Sep/Oct/Dec 2026 3-hike forecast is now more likely, not less

Why markets are up anyway (pre-open): The MU earnings beat is being interpreted as proof
that AI capex is structural and rate-insensitive. This view has merit -- hyperscalers build
AI infrastructure because competitors do too (a competitive arms race), not because the Fed
funds rate is low. But the PCE data creates a compression risk on high-multiple growth stocks.

WDC behavior confirms this tension: Pre-market $725.70 -> intraday $612-$665 -> close
~$643. The stock gave back ~$80 (11%) from pre-market highs after the PCE print. This
illustrates the dynamic: AI rally + PCE sell-off partially offsetting each other.

Rate-hiking regime winners/losers in our portfolio:

Exposure PCE Hawkish Effect Our Sleeves
MU, WDC, STX (memory) POSITIVE (pricing power = inflation hedge) All 4 systematic
VRT, BE, AGX, FIX (AI power) NEUTRAL (capex-driven, not rate-sensitive) mom_roa, residual
FNRN, FMBM, CSBB (community banks) VERY POSITIVE (NIM expansion) residual_roa
APA, DINO, PARR (energy) POSITIVE (commodity = inflation hedge) residual_roa
AAOI, PRAX, GSAT, VSAT (zero earnings) NEGATIVE (multiple compression) mom_v1/v2
AFJK, FGMC (SPACs) NEGATIVE (risk-off + no earnings) various -- exit

The ROA filter in our best two sleeves (mom_roa, residual_roa) is functioning as designed:
it naturally screens for earnings quality, which provides structural resilience in rate-hiking
environments.

Sources: CNBC PCE May 2026 | Fox Business PCE rising | Yahoo Finance market June 25 live

3. CRITICAL INFRASTRUCTURE: SATS TICKER CHANGED TO ECHO (June 24, 2026)

This is the most urgent operational issue in today's report -- requires action before July 1.

EchoStar Corporation changed its Nasdaq ticker from SATS to ECHO on June 24, 2026, effective
immediately. The new ticker ECHO now represents the same shares.

Why this breaks the DB:
- paper_positions still holds ticker = 'SATS'
- paper_mtm.py joins positions to price_cache by ticker to get today's close
- daily_price_refresh.py fetches closes for held tickers -- 'SATS' returns no data going
forward; 'ECHO' is the live ticker
- Result: from June 25 onward, SATS positions will show $0 current price or NULL in MTM

Current ECHO (was SATS) position details:

Sleeve Shares Entry Price Entry Value Unrealized (at ~$99)
mom_roa_6535 16.21 $123.24 $1,998 ~-$394 (-19.7%)
mom_v1_paper 8.11 $123.24 $999 ~-$197 (-19.7%)
mom_v2_paper 16.21 $123.24 $1,998 ~-$394 (-19.7%)

Price context: On June 24 (ticker change day), ECHO dropped 4.8% to $98.92 from $103.92.
The AT&T $23B spectrum deal (3.45GHz + 600MHz) is closed. SpaceX's $17B payment closes
approximately November 30, 2027 -- 18 months away.

Required fix (run before July 1 rebalance):
1. Update ticker in positions table: UPDATE paper_positions SET ticker = 'ECHO' WHERE ticker = 'SATS'
2. Warm ECHO into price_cache: run warm_close.py or equivalent for 'ECHO'
3. Add 'ECHO' to NON_STOCK_TICKERS so it cannot re-enter after exit
4. Plan exit at July 1 rebalance: ECHO is in a 18-month cash-flow limbo pre-SpaceX payment

Sources: EchoStar SATS->ECHO ticker announcement -- IR | Simply Wall St ticker change | EchoStar spectrum deal FCC -- AT&T

NVIDIA invested $2 billion in Coherent Corp. (NYSE: COHR) via private placement on March 2, 2026,
acquiring 7,788,161 shares. The deal includes:
- A multi-billion-dollar NVIDIA purchase commitment for AI optics
- Future access and capacity rights for advanced laser/optical networking products
- COHR using proceeds to expand US-based manufacturing for next-gen data center optics

Held at $329.66 entry in mom_roa_6535 and mom_v2_paper. The deal was announced before our
May 1 inception, but represents a permanent fundamental upgrade -- COHR went from "optical
component supplier" to "strategic NVIDIA partner for AI data center optics." The MU
earnings confirm the thesis: AI spending is structural, and optics are a required layer.

Quantum-safe networking is the next 5-year driver. Held at $535.56; fundamental case intact.

Held in mom_roa and mom_v1. MU's print validates continued hyperscaler infrastructure spending.

6. PRAX (Praxis Precision Medicines) -- Phase 3 Failure, Exit Candidate

Status: Vormatrigine program in jeopardy. Exit at July 1 rebalance.

  • POWER1 Phase 3: MISSED primary endpoint (focal onset seizures, refractory population)
  • POWER2 Phase 3: Enrollment PAUSED pending program reassessment
  • Secondary measure hit (50% responder rate) and higher-dose signal observed -- management
    evaluating dose/study modifications before deciding on POWER2 continuation
  • Timeline for POWER2 restart: uncertain; originally expected H2 2026 topline

Decision: A Phase 3 primary endpoint miss almost always collapses the momentum score that
put PRAX in the portfolio. The enrollment pause further delays any recovery. Exit at July 1
if not in top-momentum cohort (it almost certainly will not be). Do not speculate on rescue.

Sources: PRAX POWER1 miss POWER2 pause -- Simply Wall St | StockTitan PRAX vormatrigine

11. Strategic Insights

A. SPAC Filter -- Technical Implementation Plan (Post-July 1 Priority)

Two SPACs entered simultaneously at June 3 rebalance (AFJK + FGMC). SPAC volume in 2026 is
3x higher than 2024. The failure mode will recur without an explicit filter.

Proposed minimum implementation (add to universe.py, ~10 lines):

# Exclude probable SPACs: price near $10 NAV + small market cap + new listing
SPAC_PRICE_ANCHOR = 10.0
SPAC_PRICE_TOLERANCE = 0.20  # within 20% of $10 (covers $8-$12 range)
SPAC_MAX_MARKET_CAP = 250_000_000  # $250M
SPAC_MAX_AGE_DAYS = 3 * 365  # listed < 3 years

def filter_probable_spacs(df):
    near_nav = (df['price'] - SPAC_PRICE_ANCHOR).abs() / SPAC_PRICE_ANCHOR < SPAC_PRICE_TOLERANCE
    small_cap = df['market_cap'] < SPAC_MAX_MARKET_CAP
    new_listing = df['listing_age_days'] < SPAC_MAX_AGE_DAYS
    return df[~(near_nav & small_cap & new_listing)]

This would have excluded both AFJK (trading ~$40 but had ~$10 SPAC history) and FGMC
(entered at $10.38). The price history check (flat $10 period in last 12 months) adds
robustness for SPACs that have already moved from NAV.

B. AI Power Infrastructure Sleeve -- Backtest After July 1

Two dedicated AI power ETFs launched in 2026 validates institutional demand for this theme.
The $527B hyperscaler AI capex creates a multi-year buildout for VRT, AGX, FIX, POWL, MTZ, BE.
These names already rank highly in our systematic sleeves via free-float momentum.

A dedicated sleeve would test whether concentration in this sub-theme outperforms the broader
50-name systematic portfolio. Pre-condition: complete July 1 rebalance first. Then backtest
the restricted universe (SIC 3599/3612/1731 + manual inclusions) on 2023-2026 data.

If the backtest shows >5pp held-out alpha vs mom_roa_6535, deploy as a 9th systematic sleeve.
Reference universe: AIPO ETF holdings as a starting point.

C. PCE Stagflation as a Durable Regime -- Portfolio Positioning

May 2026 PCE at 4.1% confirms BofA's 3-hike forecast. This regime favors:
- High-ROA businesses (can defend margins) -> already our primary signal
- Memory/storage (DRAM/NAND pricing power = inflation pass-through)
- Community banks (NIM expansion on rate hikes) -> residual_roa's FNRN/FMBM/CSBB
- Energy refiners (crack spread widening in stagflation) -> residual_roa's DINO/PARR
- AI infrastructure (capex-driven, rate-insensitive) -> VRT/BE/AGX/FIX/POWL

The sleeves already positioned to benefit most: mom_roa_6535 and residual_roa_6535.

D. MU September 24, 2026 -- Next Binary Event

MU reports Q4 FY2026 approximately September 24, 2026. The Q4 guidance of $50B +/-$1B vs
Q3 actual $41.46B implies ~+21% sequential growth. If the $100B SCAs provide demand stability,
MU could report another beat. Pre-mark this date on the rebalance calendar.

Three-Day Recap: June 23-25 â€” The Most Volatile Week Since Inception

The June 23-25 period contained three distinct macro events compressed into three sessions.
No prior three-day window has tested the portfolio across such diverse stressors.

Day 1 â€” June 23 (Tuesday): Global AI Chip Rout

Sleeve Jun-22 NAV Jun-23 NAV Day %
mom_roa_6535 $114,081 $108,016 -5.31%
mom_v2 $108,403 $103,900 -4.15%
mom_v1 $108,168 $103,994 -3.86%
residual_roa $110,306 $107,218 -2.80%
sector_top4 $103,995 $102,046 -1.88%
SPY benchmark $103,294 $101,794 -1.45%
mom_roa_top1 (AAOI) $101,238 $87,186 -13.88%

What happened June 23: South Korea's KOSPI index fell nearly 10%, triggering a circuit
breaker halt. The cascade:
- SK Hynix -12%, Samsung -12% (chip-heavy Korean names sparked the sell)
- MU -13.4%, TSM -5.2%, NVDA -3.2%, MRVL -9%
- Nasdaq -2.21%, S&P -1.44%
- Japan's Nikkei -3.6%, SoftBank -15%

What triggered it: The Bank of America circulated a research note raising the probability
of the FOMC hiking in October 2026, on top of residual hawkish shock from the June 17 FOMC
dot-plot. Asian AI trade was crowded and de-risked hard ahead of MU earnings that night --
"sell the news before the news."

Portfolio context: mom_roa's -5.31% was the worst single-day loss since inception.
All four semiconductor-weighted sleeves underperformed SPY materially. This is the expected
behavior: concentrated AI hardware exposure amplifies both gains and losses vs. the index.
The June 18 single-day best (+2.95% for mom_roa) had already established this dynamic;
June 23 confirmed it in the downside direction.

The AAOI (control) catastrophe: The mom_roa_top1 control sleeve lost -13.88% in one
session. AAOI fell roughly -14% as part of the AI optical/semiconductor-adjacent selloff.
This is the single worst day for the control sleeve and represents the highest-magnitude
argument for the LLM veto to date.

Sources: https://www.cnn.com/2026/06/23/business/stock-market-kospi-dow-nasdaq-ai (CNN Wall Street AI selloff Korea circuit breaker),
https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/23/tech-stocks-sell-off-mag7-samsung-sk-hynix.html (CNBC Tech rout intensifies global stocks),
https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-06-23/korean-stocks-fall-more-than-4-from-record-high-on-tech-selloff (Bloomberg Kospi slides chip concerns),
https://www.thestreet.com/stock-market-today/stock-market-today-dow-jones-sp-500-nasdaq-updates-june-23-2026 (TheStreet June 23 market)

Day 2 â€” June 24 (Wednesday): Pre-Earnings Consolidation -> MU Blowout After Close

Day 3 â€” June 25 (Thursday, Today): MU Surge + Apple/MSFT Price Shock = Mixed

The day opened with MU up ~19% from the June 24 close, but two simultaneous negative
catalysts (Apple MacBook/iPad price hikes -6.12% AAPL; Microsoft Xbox hikes -3.23% MSFT)
dragged the Mag7 and XLK. The portfolio, holding neither Apple nor Microsoft, benefited
from MU's surge while avoiding the consumer hardware pain. Final result: all five
systematic sleeves +1.32% to +2.65% vs SPY +0.14%.

DEEP DIVE: MU (Micron) Q3 FY2026 Earnings -- THE Defining Portfolio Event

Micron reported fiscal Q3 2026 (quarter ending May 28, 2026) on June 24, 2026 after market close.

This is the single most important event in the portfolio's 56-day trading history.

Results vs. Consensus:

Metric Micron Report Street Consensus Beat Magnitude
Revenue $41.46B $35.59B +16.4% upside
Non-GAAP EPS $25.11 $20.20-$20.60 +21.9-24.3% upside
Gross Margin ~82% ~81% In-line / slight beat

Q4 FY2026 Guidance (blowout):
- Revenue: $50B +/- $1B -> Street was expecting ~$42.9B -> +16.6% above consensus
- Adjusted EPS: $30-$32
- Gross Margin guidance: ~86% (would be highest quarterly GM in Micron history)
- Analyst Susquehanna raised price target to $2,000 (from $1,750)
- Analyst Barclays raised price target to $2,000 (from $1,175)
- Multiple others raised into $1,200-$1,550 band

The Most Important Disclosure -- $100 Billion in Contracted Revenue:
Micron disclosed it has signed 16 strategic customer agreements representing approximately
$100 billion in minimum contracted revenue, with $22 billion in firm financial commitments,
locking in supply across 3-5 years with data center operators, hyperscalers, and automakers.

This single disclosure structurally changes how the stock should be valued. Cyclical
semiconductor companies are typically valued on forward P/E with significant discount for
cycle risk. A company with $100B in minimum contracted backlog is not a cyclical -- it is
a contracted industrial. The comparison is no longer "semiconductor cycle peak P/E" but rather
"backlog-backed industrial with ~86% gross margins."

MU stock performance June 24-25:
- June 24 AH: +13.3-14.6% immediately after the print
- June 25 open: surged ~19% from June 24 close
- June 25 intraday range: $1,136.31 - $1,255.00
- June 25 close (DB): $1,213.56

Portfolio unrealized P&L (MU at June 25 close, $1,213.56):

Sleeve Shares Entry (May 1) Jun-25 Price Unrealized
mom_roa_6535 3.683 $542.48 $1,213.56 +$2,477 (+123.7%)
residual_roa 3.683 $542.48 $1,213.56 +$2,477 (+123.7%)
mom_v2_paper 3.683 $542.48 $1,213.56 +$2,477 (+123.7%)
mom_v1_paper 1.842 $542.48 $1,213.56 +$1,236 (+123.7%)

Total MU unrealized across all sleeves: ~+$8,667
From the May 1 entry at $542.48, MU is +123.7%. This is the portfolio's best position by
total return, and the Q4 guidance ($50B + 86% GM) suggests the supercycle has years, not
quarters, to run.

Why the beat was "structural" not "cyclical":
1. $100B contracted revenue -- the supply is pre-sold. Revenue visibility is a new
paradigm for memory semiconductors.
2. HBM4 transition -- The mix shift to HBM (High Bandwidth Memory) dramatically elevates
ASP and margins. DRAM that used to sell at $5-8/GB now sells at $40-60/GB in HBM4 form.
3. Apple CEO admission (June 17) now converted to ACTUAL price increases (June 25):
Apple raised MacBook/iPad prices by 20%/$200. This is not a projection -- it happened.
4. Q4 GM guidance 86% -- Historically, memory companies run 30-50% GM in mid-cycle.
86% is software-company margin territory, achieved through mix shift to HBM and pricing power.

Sources: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0000723125/000072312526000013/a2026q3ex991-pressrelease.htm (SEC MU FY2026 8-K press release),
https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/24/micron-mu-earnings-report-q3-2026.html (CNBC Micron Q3 2026 earnings),
https://www.thestreet.com/latest-news/micron-mu-q3-2026-earnings-call-updates (TheStreet MU Q3 earnings live recap),
https://www.tradingkey.com/analysis/stocks/us-stocks/261990080-micron-mu-q3-2026-earnings-record-revenue-hbm4-analysis-tradingkey (TradingKey MU Q3 HBM4),
https://www.investing.com/analysis/microns-100-billion-backlog-could-reset-how-the-market-values-memory-200682816 (Investing.com $100B backlog),
https://www.benzinga.com/markets/earnings/26/06/60091960/is-micron-still-cheap-post-earnings-how-100-billion-in-contracts-is-making-cyclical-skeptics-offsides (Benzinga $100B contracts),
https://stockstotrade.com/news/micron-technology-inc-mu-news-2026_06_25-2/ (Susquehanna/Barclays PT $2,000)

Apple and Microsoft Announce Hardware Price Hikes -- Confirm Memory Supercycle

On June 25, 2026, Apple and Microsoft took the actions that Apple CEO Tim Cook had warned
about in his June 17 WSJ interview ("the situation has become unsustainable"):

Apple price increases (effective June 25, 2026):
- MacBook Neo (entry-level): $599 -> $699 (+$100, +16.7%)
- MacBook Air 13" 512GB: $1,099 -> $1,299 (+$200, +18.2%)
- MacBook Pro 1TB M5: equivalent to +$300 (~+17%)
- iPad (base model): $349 -> $449 (+$100, +28.6%)
- All iPad Pro models: +$200
- iPhone, Apple Watch, AirPods: NOT raised (storage-lighter products)

Microsoft Xbox price increases:
- Standard model: +$100
- Higher-memory-capacity model: +$150
- Microsoft statement: "Console storage and memory prices have increased by more than 2.5x
and we expect another doubling by the fall of 2027"

Market reaction:
- AAPL: -6.12% to close (~$272B market cap wiped out in one session)
- MSFT: -3.23%
- Broad Mag7 drag: Nasdaq -0.46% despite MU's massive surge

Why this matters for the portfolio's memory thesis:
Tim Cook's June 17 warning was a verbal signal. June 25 is the execution -- Apple ACTUALLY
raised prices because memory costs cannot be absorbed at current purchase volumes. Microsoft
explicitly stated they expect memory prices to DOUBLE AGAIN by fall 2027. These are not
analyst projections. These are the two largest consumer electronics companies in the world
(by revenue) raising prices and explaining that memory scarcity is the cause.

For MU/WDC/STX: This is structural confirmation. Consumer electronics has moved from
being the demand driver to being a price-taker secondary to AI datacenter contracts. The
pricing floor is now validated by the two largest hardware companies in the world.

Portfolio impact: No direct AAPL or MSFT exposure in any sleeve. The portfolio benefited
from MU's earnings surge (+2.59% for mom_roa today) while avoiding the consumer tech pain
(-6% AAPL, -3.2% MSFT). This asymmetric positioning -- long infrastructure, not consumer
-- continued to prove itself in a third distinct market stress test.

Sources: https://www.cbsnews.com/news/apple-price-hikes-macbook-ipad-2026/ (CBS News Apple Microsoft price hikes June 25),
https://www.nbcnews.com/business/consumer/apple-stock-ipad-macbook-price-hikes-rcna351786 (NBC News Apple shares plunge),
https://ca.finance.yahoo.com/news/following-apple-microsoft-dramatically-hikes-174523579.html (Yahoo Finance Microsoft Xbox hike),
https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/apple-price-hike-ipad-macbook-ai-memory-chip-2026-9.7248577 (CBC News Apple Microsoft AI memory chip supply),
https://www.thestreet.com/stock-market-today/stock-market-today-dow-jones-sp-500-nasdaq-updates-june-25-2026 (TheStreet June 25 Mag7)

INTC (Intel) â€” BofA Double-Upgrade to Buy, PT $160 (+20% from Current)

Bank of America executed a double-upgrade on Intel (INTC) on June 25, 2026:
- Previous rating: Underperform (bearish)
- New rating: Buy
- Price target: $160 (from prior Underperform target of $90)
- BofA's foundry forecast: Foundry revenue rising from $1.1B in 2026 to $47.1B by 2030,
driven by wafers, advanced packaging, Apple-related production, MediaTek TPU work, and
Intel Terafab engagements

Intel stock on June 25: +6.31% (INTC closed at $132.87 per DB price_cache)

Why BofA's upgrade is strategically significant:
BofA was previously one of Intel's most vocal bears. A double-upgrade (skipping Neutral to
go directly to Buy) from a major bear signals that the institutional consensus on Intel's
foundry story has crossed a tipping point. BofA's $47.1B foundry revenue forecast by 2030
implies Intel Foundry becomes the #2 or #3 global foundry by revenue within 4 years.

The multi-customer Intel foundry case (updated):
1. Apple: 18A-P manufacturing (Trump announced June 17; lower-end chips only, TSMC keeps >90%)
2. Google/Alphabet: Backup AI chip manufacturing (confirmed June 22)
3. NVIDIA: Overflow AI chip capacity at 18A-P (confirmed June 22)
4. MediaTek: TPU production (BofA reference June 25)

LLM Overlay Experiment â€” Historic Divergence Record: $18,071 Lead

CONTROL SLEEVE IN FREEFALL -- Treatment (cash) leads by largest margin ever

Date AAOI Price Control NAV Treatment (Cash) Lead
Jun 12 (inception) $169.13 $100,000 $100,000 $0
Jun 15 (QuantumLink surge) $191.55 ~$103,245 $100,000 -$3,245
Jun 16 $170.81 ~$100,005 $100,000 ~$0
Jun 17 $167.34 $98,940 $100,000 +$1,060
Jun 18 $161.85 $95,697 $100,000 +$4,303
Jun 22 (China credit reversal) $171.23 $101,238 $100,000 -$1,238 (scare #2)
Jun 23 (chip rout) ~$147 $87,186 $100,000 +$12,814
Jun 24 $146.97 $86,908 $100,000 +$13,092
Jun 25 (close) $138.54 $81,929 $100,000 +$18,071

The June 23 global AI chip rout was the decisive event. AAOI fell approximately -14% in
a single session, consistent with the sector-wide panic. The control sleeve lost $14,052
in one day (-13.88%). The treatment (in cash since June 12) is untouched.

The experiment's current state:
- Control (AAOI): -18.1% from $169.13 inception price / NAV $81,929
- Treatment (cash): +0.00% / NAV $100,000
- Treatment outperformance: +22.1 percentage points (13 trading days)
- AAOI current price: $138.54 (close June 25 per DB)
- For the control to BREAK EVEN from here, AAOI must rise from $138.54 to $169.13 = +22.1%

The veto's thesis remains intact:
The LLM veto on June 12 cited: (1) insider selling, (2) Semi Analysis report showing 1.6T
CPO deployments pushed "later in 2026," (3) fundamental uncertainty about CPO timing vs
QuantumLink/datasheet hype. The chip rout that hammered AAOI is precisely the "uncertain,
hype-driven" environment the veto was designed to avoid. No confirmed 1.6T CPO shipment
announcement has been made as of June 25.

However, acknowledge the June 22 reversal risk: AAOI nearly reversed the veto advantage
on June 22 (control briefly led treatment by +$1,238 when AAOI surged +5.79% on China credit
news). The experiment's validity depends on AAOI either being fundamentally right (CPO starts
-> treatment re-enters at higher price) or fundamentally wrong (CPO delayed -> treatment wins
by staying out). June 23's -14% is the market agreeing with the veto for now.

Re-entry criteria (unchanged):
- Trigger 1 (hard): First confirmed 1.6T CPO customer shipment from a hyperscaler
- Trigger 2 (soft): AAOI Q2 earnings confirm 1.6T CPO production equipment installed and sampling
- Trigger 3: Q2 results above consensus with CPO revenue guidance line

None of these conditions are met as of June 25.

Sector overlay (llm_overlay_sector_top4 vs. sector_top4):
- Control ($103,280.53) still leads treatment ($100,894.73) by $2,385.80
- The XLE cash veto continues to narrow the gap but has not closed it
- June 25: XLK dragged by AAPL price hike news; treatment's cash vs XLK position
worked against treatment today -- XLI and XLB (both held by treatment) performed better
than XLK
- The treatment's XLE cash slot is the right structural call; the gap is from the
early seeding disadvantage (treatment was seeded after control had accumulated XLK gains)

SATS (EchoStar) â€” July 1 Principal Maturity Now 6 Days Away

SATS most recent DB price: $100.28 (June 24 close -- most recent data available).

From May 1 entry ($123.24):
- 16.21 sh x ($100.28 - $123.24) = -$372 unrealized (-18.6%)

Timeline status:
- June 18: $183M DISH DBS interest payment made (default averted at wire deadline)
- June 25: SATS trading near $100 -- down from $180+ at inception
- July 1: $6.13B principal maturity wall -- 7 3/4% Senior Notes ($2.0B), 5.25% Secured
Notes ($2.75B), and others due in 2026 total
- The RSA (Restructuring Support Agreement with 82% of noteholders, signed March 2026)
converts most debt to equity via the AT&T proceeds chain
- AT&T cash ($20.25B) arrives November 2027 -- not available to service July 1 principal

Expectation: The RSA should handle the July 1 principal via debt-for-equity conversion
or extension. The paper model will exit SATS at July 1 rebalance regardless -- its 12-1
momentum is deeply negative ($100 vs $180+ a year ago). No action needed for paper trade.

Case study note: SATS is a confirmed example of ROA x momentum failing on going-concern
credit risk. The spectrum asset values gave SATS a positive-ROA appearance at May 1 scoring
despite being operationally insolvent. Going-concern screen (proposed filter) would have
excluded it if the 10-Q going-concern language was checked.

Sources: https://ir.echostar.com/news-releases/news-release-details/echostar-announces-suite-transformative-transactions-delever-its (EchoStar RSA announcement),
https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/SATS/10-q-echo-star-corp-quarterly-earnings-report-e8bdf47b1303.html (SEC EchoStar 10-Q going concern)

ARWR (Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals) â€” SHASTA Data Window Now Open

Position: 26.69 sh @ $74.85 entry | Jun-25 close $79.27 | +$118 (+5.9%)

The SHASTA Phase 3 topline data window officially opened in Q3 2026 (which began July 1
per the standard calendar). Arrowhead's CMO James Hamilton stated at the June 2026 RBC
Healthcare Conference that the company is "cautiously optimistic" SHASTA will show
statistical significance on acute pancreatitis and is confident the studies are
"sufficiently powered based on blinded event rates."

SHASTA key details:
- SHASTA-3 and SHASTA-4: Phase 3 trials of plozasiran in Severe Hypertriglyceridemia (SHTG)
- FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation for SHTG
- SHASTA-5: Additional study for acute pancreatitis primary endpoint (enrollment ongoing)
- REDEMPLO approvals accumulating: US (approved), China, Australia, Canada, EU positive CHMP
opinion, European Commission approval expected shortly

Timeline: Data expected "toward end of June 2026" or early Q3 per company statements.
As of June 25, no press release has been issued. The window is now open; any press release
this week or early July would be the portfolio's second major binary biotech event (after PRAX).

Sentiment: "Cautiously optimistic" from the CMO combined with Breakthrough Therapy
Designation and an enrolled trial means the statistical bar is defined and the study is
powered. Unlike PRAX's POWER1 (which missed the primary endpoint), SHASTA has biological
validation from the FCS indication (plozasiran already approved for FCS -- SHTG is the same
mechanism in a larger patient population).

Sources: https://www.theglobeandmail.com/investing/markets/stocks/ARWR-Q/pressreleases/2065571/arrowhead-pharmaceuticals-eyes-q3-shasta-data-as-redemplo-approvals-build-momentum/ (Globe and Mail ARWR Q3 SHASTA),
https://finance.yahoo.com/sectors/healthcare/articles/arrowhead-pharmaceuticals-eyes-q3-shasta-140758490.html (Yahoo Finance ARWR Q3 SHASTA data wave),
https://ir.arrowheadpharma.com/news-releases/news-release-details/arrowhead-pharmaceuticals-completes-enrollment-shasta-3-shasta-4 (Arrowhead SHASTA enrollment complete)

- January 29, 2027: ulixacaltamide PDUFA (Essential Tremor, ~7M US patients)

July 1 hold/exit decision: PRAX's June 30, 2025 price was well below today's $327.86.
The 12-1 momentum score will be positive (stock has risen strongly over the past year,
despite the POWER1 miss). The model will likely HOLD PRAX at July 1. If PRAX is in
the top-50 momentum names AND has positive ROA score, it stays.

Caterpillar (CAT) â€” AI Power Demand Drives 5th Straight Session Gain; Not Held

On June 25, CAT gained +5.28% to near $966, its 5th consecutive up session, driven by
AI data center demand for large-scale power generation equipment. CAT's Power and Energy
segment is benefiting from the Microsoft Project Kilby (West Texas AI data center with
Chevron), an example of hyperscalers needing on-site gas turbines to bypass grid queues.
CAT's record $63B backlog (+79% YoY) gives it multi-year revenue visibility.

Not held by any sleeve. CAT's stock price and revenue profile (capital goods, heavy
machinery) may not meet the ROA filter criteria the model uses. However, the AI power
demand dynamic (identical to what is driving BE, AGX, VRT, FIX in our portfolio) suggests
CAT should appear in future momentum + quality scans if its ROA remains strong.

Monitoring note: If CAT appears in the top-50 or top-100 momentum scoring on June 30,
it would be a July 1 new entry candidate. Worth watching.

Sources: https://www.tradingkey.com/news/market-movers/261991591-market-movers-cat-20260625 (TradingKey CAT +5.28% June 25),
https://www.tikr.com/blog/caterpillar-stock-is-up-over-60-in-2026-heres-where-cat-could-go (TIKR Caterpillar up 60% 2026 AI)

Core PCE â€” Tomorrow, June 26 (The Final Macro Catalyst Before July 1)

The Bureau of Economic Analysis releases May 2026 PCE data tomorrow, June 26 (Friday):

Current inflation backdrop:
- Core PCE (excluding food and energy): 3.40% YoY in May 2026 (up from 3.30% in April)
- Headline PCE: 4.10% YoY in May (up from 3.80% in April)

What the FOMC is watching:
- The June 17 dot plot showed 9 of 18 members projecting a 2026 rate hike
- Warsh is "data-dependent" -- elevated Core PCE above 3.0% gives the hawkish bloc ammunition
- A hot print (Core MoM >+0.3%) could accelerate October hike probability from ~35% to ~50%
- A cool print (<+0.2%) would reduce that probability and provide relief

Portfolio sensitivity:
- Rate-sensitive growth names (DOCN, CRDO) face multiple compression risk if hike probability
rises materially
- Hardware names (MU, STX, WDC) are less rate-sensitive given contracted revenue
- Energy-adjacent names (BE, AGX) are inflation-insensitive (long-term contracts)

The key risk: If PCE comes in hot AND the market's mood is already fragile from the
Apple/Microsoft price hike announcements (which signal ongoing consumer inflation), the
combination could trigger a Friday selloff. The portfolio's semiconductor-heavy weighting
means it would take a harder hit than SPY in that scenario.

Sources: https://tradingeconomics.com/united-states/core-pce-price-index-annual-change (Trading Economics US Core PCE)

2. XLE slot reassessment for sector_top4 macro veto
3. Four-prompt macro set for sector_top4 (rates/valuation/breadth/bear-case)

Strategic Insights â€” June 25

Insight 1: The Chip Rout to MU Blowout Sequence -- What It Teaches About Market Structure

The June 23 Korean circuit-breaker selloff hit our portfolio harder than SPY (mom_roa -5.3%
vs SPY -1.4%). But the pattern that FOLLOWED is what matters:

Event Date Market Action Portfolio
FOMC hawkish shock Jun 17 SPY -1.21% Portfolio beats by 2+ pp
Global chip rout Jun 23 SPY -1.44% Portfolio falls 4pp MORE than SPY
MU blowout earnings Jun 24 AH -> Jun 25 SPY +0.14% Portfolio beats by 2.5pp

The rout-then-beat pattern is classic: crowded AI trades de-risked ahead of a binary event
(MU earnings), then the event was so massively bullish that it validated the fundamental thesis
and re-attracted buyers. The key question for the strategy: does concentration in AI
hardware amplify losses during routs enough to undermine the long-run alpha?

Current answer: Over 56 trading days, mom_roa is +9.51% vs SPY +1.89%. The chip rout
cost ~2.5pp of relative performance in one day, but the prior days of outperformance had
already banked substantially more. The net alpha through June 25 remains strong.

Strategy implication: The portfolio SHOULD underperform SPY on days like June 23.
That is the cost of concentrated, high-ROA momentum exposure. The payoff comes on days
like June 25 (MU +19% open, INTC +6%). This is the risk-reward trade the sleeve design
intended. The key discipline: do not react to single-day drawdowns by de-risking into the
wrong names.

Insight 2: MU $100B Contracted Revenue -- This Is Not a Semiconductor Stock Anymore

The most important element of MU's earnings was not the Q3 beat (which was extraordinary).
It was the $100B in minimum contracted revenue across 3-5 years from 16 hyperscalers.

Implication for valuation:
- Pre-contracts: MU priced as cyclical semicon -> peak P/E ~10-15x, trough -40-60% from peak
- Post-contracts: $100B/3-5yr = $20-33B/yr guaranteed floor. At current $41.5B quarterly
run-rate ($166B/yr), the guaranteed floor is $20-33B/yr = 12-20% of revenue floor-locked
- MU now has more revenue contracted than Apple has ENTIRE iPhone business contracted

For the model's July 1 hold decision: MU's 12-1 momentum score will be one of the
highest in the entire universe (stock up from $542 entry date price to $1,213, and up even
more from June 30, 2025 baseline). The July 1 model hold is mathematically certain. The
interesting question is whether MU retains its relative ranking at August 1, when a higher
price base makes the 12-1 score from the prior year more favorable.

Insight 3: AAOI Veto -- Post-Mortem If Experiment Were To Close Today

If the LLM overlay experiment closed as of June 25:
- Treatment wins by $18,071 (+22.1pp over control)
- The June 15 scare (+13.3% one day) was a false positive -- thesis reversed two days later
- The June 22 scare (+5.79%, China credit) was a false positive -- reversed by June 23 chip rout
- The June 23 chip rout (-14%) was the decisive validation event

What the experiment demonstrates so far:
The veto's value is NOT in timing a specific catalyst (CPO shipments). It is in
recognizing that AAOI has elevated binary event risk (CPO deployment timing unknown) and
that the best position in a crowded AI trade ahead of uncertain catalysts is to wait for
confirmation before committing capital. The cash position preserved optionality through
two scare events and survived the chip rout with zero loss.

The experiment is not over. If AAOI receives a confirmed 1.6T CPO shipment announcement
before August 1, the treatment would re-enter. The question is whether that re-entry price
would be low enough for the treatment to still win over the full experiment window.

Insight 4: WDC Specific Risk -- Fox Advisors Downgrade

Western Digital was downgraded by Fox Advisors from Outperform to Equalweight on June 22
(the downgrade was circulated June 23 and added company-specific headwind during the chip rout).
WDC fell ~8.5% on June 23 (vs MU -13%, STX broadly in line).

The Fox Advisors concern: The overhang from the Western Digital -> SanDisk share swap
(WDC spun off its flash business into SanDisk separately) creates near-term technical
selling pressure as holders receive new SanDisk shares they may not want.

WDC at June 25 close: $675.39 (entry $431.74 = +56.4% unrealized). Despite the
downgrade and chip rout, the fundamental position is strongly in the money. However,
the SanDisk overhang is a real technical risk: if SNDK (SanDisk) holders sell, it creates
supply. Monitor WDC/SNDK divergence at the July 1 rebalance scoring date.

Sources: https://www.quiverquant.com/news/Western+Digital+Slides+as+Chip+Rout+and+Share-Swap+Overhang+Weigh+on+Stock (Quiverquant WDC Fox downgrade)

Insight 5: Apple/Microsoft Price Hikes -- A Permanent Memory Scarcity Signal

Cook's verbal "unsustainable" warning (June 17) becoming ACTUAL price increases (June 25)
represents a key moment in the memory supercycle narrative:

  1. Verbal warning (June 17) -> analysts upgrade MU, WDC, STX
  2. Actual price increases (June 25) -> narrative confirmed; consumers absorb or defer
  3. What comes next: If Apple iPhone 18 Pro also includes a memory price pass-through
    (Cook said iPhone was NOT raised yet), that will be the final validation that the entire
    consumer technology supply chain has re-priced around memory scarcity

Microsoft's explicit statement ("storage and memory prices have increased more than 2.5x;
expect another doubling by fall 2027") is the most forward-looking bullish datapoint for
memory stocks. No analyst has published a report saying "I expect memory prices to 5x from
2025 to 2027." Microsoft just said it in a press release.

For the model: MU, WDC, STX are near-certain holds at every rebalance until either
(a) the $100B contracted revenue expires, (b) GM guidance turns down, or (c) Microsoft
reverses this pricing signal. None of these conditions are anywhere close to being met.

Questions to Monitor Into Tomorrow and Next Week

  1. PCE data June 26 (Friday): Core MoM vs. +0.2% (neutral) or +0.3%+ (hike concern)?
    This is the final macro catalyst before July 1 rebalance.

  2. ARWR SHASTA data: Any press release before June 30? Company said "toward end of June."
    A positive press release before July 1 scoring would be a major catalyst.

  3. SATS July 1 maturity: Any 8-K from EchoStar on RSA execution or waiver? The July 1
    paper exit is certain regardless; the question is whether SATS faces a formal
    restructuring event that disrupts trade execution.

  4. WDC SanDisk share-swap technical overhang: Does WDC/SNDK diverge further into July 1?
    The Fox Advisors downgrade may cause institutional de-allocation that weighs on WDC.

  5. AAOI Q2 earnings timing: AAOI typically reports late July/early August. No impact
    on July 1 rebalance, but sets up the next LLM overlay decision cycle.

  6. Overlay prep (CRITICAL -- by June 29): Run overlay_prep.bat to generate:
    - LLM evaluation of new #1 candidate for mom_roa_top1
    - XLE macro reassessment for sector_top4 veto
    - Full four-prompt sector macro set

What's driving it — four simultaneous headwinds:

The market's character: Thursday's session was "sell the hardware OEM (Apple, Microsoft),

reversion: even the infrastructure names are giving back some of the Thursday gains.

2. Micron (MU) — Premarket -5% to ~$1,173: The Morningstar Bear Thesis

June 25 close: $1,213.56 | June 26 premarket: ~$1,173 (-3.3% to -5.0%)

MU's +15.7% session on June 25 (after its post-earnings surge) was the single largest
single-day dollar gain in the portfolio's history. Friday's -5% premarket is pure
profit-taking behavior — institutions booking gains before the weekend after a two-day
+17.9% run from the June 24 close (~$1,050) to Thursday's close ($1,213.56).

However, there is a credible bear thesis that deserves honest treatment:

Morningstar's counter-argument (published June 26 premarket): Morningstar analyst
William Kerwin expects Micron to benefit from an "unprecedented cyclical upswing" through
2028 — but then anticipates revenue falling more than 50% across 2029-2030 as pricing
normalizes and new capacity floods the market. The scenario:
- SK Hynix and Samsung both commit to HBM capacity expansions in 2026/2027
- TSMC's N2 process (expected 2027) lowers the barrier to competing with Micron's process advantages
- Hyperscaler AI capex moderates after the initial buildout phase
- Supply glut begins 2028-2029 → prices revert → Micron revenues from $165B annualized to $80B

The bull counter to Morningstar:
1. The $100B take-or-pay contracts eliminate 12-20% of revenue from the supply glut cycle risk
(customers MUST pay regardless of spot prices)
2. HBM4 is 2+ years ahead of HBM5 in design; Samsung has not yet qualified HBM4 for Nvidia
Vera Rubin (Micron and SK Hynix hold the qualification); Samsung catching up takes 12-18 months
3. Q4 guidance of $50B + 86% gross margin is already built on current production — Morningstar's
$80B scenario requires a simultaneous revenue AND margin compression that hasn't materialized
in the contracts already signed

Portfolio position at June 26 premarket prices (~$1,173):

Sleeve Shares Entry Pre-Mkt ~$1,173 Unrealized
mom_roa_6535 3.683 $542.48 ~$1,173 +$2,330 (+116.2%)
residual_roa 3.683 $542.48 ~$1,173 +$2,330 (+116.2%)
mom_v2 3.683 $542.48 ~$1,173 +$2,330 (+116.2%)
mom_v1 1.842 $542.48 ~$1,173 +$1,165 (+116.2%)

Even at the premarket -5% level, MU remains one of the largest P&L contributors in the
portfolio (+116%) and the $100B contracted revenue base makes the Morningstar 2030 scenario
the tail risk, not the base case.

- Susquehanna: $2,000 (raised June 25)
- Deutsche Bank: $1,500 (raised June 25)
