3. Catalyst and Macro Review

A. Today: Q2/H1 End — Signal Lock Day for July 1 Rebalance

June 30, 2026 is the last trading day of Q2 2026 and H1 2026. Today's 4pm closes are the inputs to the momentum scoring that determines the July 1 rebalance. All five systematic sleeves (mom_v1, mom_v2, mom_roa, residual_roa, sector_top4) plus the LLM overlay controls/treatments will rebalance based on today's close prices.

Implication: window dressing creates a final bid for the biggest winners (VSAT +24% June 29, KLAC +7%, UCTT all-time high, VICR guidance raised) — these names likely get one more lift today before institutions rotate out post-rebalance tomorrow.

B. Nike NKE Q4 FY2026 Earnings After Bell (Not Portfolio Name — Market Mover)

Nike releases Q4 FY2026 results at ~1:15pm PT. Analyst consensus: revenue $10.85B (-2% YoY), EPS $0.13 (-7% YoY). Focus: CEO Elliott Hill turnaround (inventory clearance, wholesale restoration), Greater China outlook (-20% revenue decline risk). NKE results will influence consumer/macro sentiment for the final hours of Q2 trading.
- Source: StockTitan — Nike Q4 FY2026 Earnings Date

C. Jobs Report Thursday July 2 — Holiday-Shortened Week

The June Employment Situation releases Thursday July 2 at 8:30am (bond market closed Friday July 4 for Independence Day). May 2026 baseline: +172K nonfarm payrolls, unemployment 4.3%. The jobs report will directly feed July/September FOMC rate expectations.

FOMC context: Paused at 3.50–3.75% (June 16–17 meeting). Core PCE revised upward to 3.3% for Q4 2026 (well above 2% target), keeping the pause posture in place. Markets are NOT pricing a cut until late 2026 or 2027 at earliest under current trajectory.
- Source: BLS — May 2026 Employment Situation | IC Markets — June 30 Fundamental Forecast | FRED Blog — FOMC June 2026 Projections

D. AAOI — 1.6T Transceiver Shipments START July 1

The most significant overnight catalyst: Applied Optoelectronics' milestone calendar shows "Start shipments for 1.6T order" = July 1, 2026 (tomorrow). The $200M+ first volume order from a major hyperscaler begins delivering. This is the first tangible revenue delivery of the 1.6T product line.

Additional AAOI context:
- Q1 2026 revenue +51% to $151.1M; fifth straight quarter of record sales
- Q2 2026 guidance: $180–$198M revenue, 29–30% non-GAAP gross margin
- Manufacturing capacity expansion: 388,000 sq ft in Pearland TX, Texas Semiconductor grant $20.85M
- Target: 650,000+ units/month of 800G and 1.6T by late 2026
- June 29 confirmed close: $138.54 (not $150.10 in DB — unresolved price error)
- 12-1 momentum: strongly POSITIVE (~+399% YoY; year-ago June 2025 ~$28 vs current $138.54)

CRITICAL CORRECTION to June 29 report: The prior report stated "AAOI: If June 2025 base was ~$169, today's $136-$150 range = -11% to -20% 12-1 → BELOW THRESHOLD → likely exit at July 1 rebalance." This was factually wrong. The $169.13 was the ENTRY PRICE to the control sleeve on June 12, 2026 — not the June 2025 base price. The June 2025 AAOI price was approximately $27-28 (consistent with "+398.9% over the past year" at $138.54). The 12-1 momentum signal for AAOI is STILL POSITIVE. Whether AAOI stays in the top-50 ranking depends on relative competitor scores.
- Sources: GlobeNewswire — AOI First Volume 1.6T Order | StockTitan — AAOI 1.6T | Simply Wall St — AAOI After Pullback | Globe and Mail — AAOI Q1 Transcript

E. Micron (MU) — Anthropic Partnership + July 6 Ex-Dividend

New development post-Q3 earnings blowout (June 24): Micron announced a strategic agreement with Anthropic covering AI memory and storage architecture, a multiyear supply commitment, and a Micron investment in Anthropic's Series H round. This is the deepest Micron/AI model provider integration to date.

Ex-dividend: July 6, 2026 ($0.15/share). The ex-date is after the July 1 rebalance; positions held through the rebalance will capture the dividend. At $1,145/share, the dividend yield is negligible (<0.01%) — but the date is worth tracking for any portfolio management purposes. Analyst consensus PT: $1,454 (Strong Buy, 43 analysts per eToro).
- Sources: eToro — MU Strong Buy $1,454 PT | Motley Fool — Micron Skyrocket After June 24

F. VICR Vicor — Q2 Revenue Guidance Raised to $142M

Vicor revised Q2 2026 revenue guidance from $126M to $142M (+12.7% revision), citing rising product revenues and royalties from an additional licensee to its patented power system technology. Order backlog: $300.6M. Full-year 2026 guidance: ~$570M.

Recent analyst upgrades (week of June 22): Craig-Hallum $450 (from $400), Roth Capital $375 (from $285), Needham $400 (from $350). Stock at $366.79 close June 29, with a 621% year-over-year gain. Pre-market June 30 indicated ~$367.
- Sources: Yahoo Finance — VICR 621% | Simply Wall St — VICR Record Revenue and Backlog

G. KLAC KLA Corporation — Short Interest Alert: +1,060% Spike to 36.6% of Float

KLAC closed June 29 at $278.39 (+7.11%). New overnight data: short interest surged 1,060.6% to 40.8 million shares = approximately 36.6% of outstanding float. This is extraordinary — over a third of tradeable shares are sold short.

Competing scenarios: (1) Short squeeze if AI/WFE news continues positive (AI semi capex accelerating per Micron, LRCX, TSMC). (2) Valuation reset if valuation concerns dominate (KLAC at 70x trailing P/E post-split). Cantor Fitzgerald raised PT to $325 (from $250), Bank of America to $317 (from $210) — both citing AI-driven WFE demand.

DB position confirmed corrected: qty=11.568, entry=172.71. KLAC management projects $1B in advanced packaging revenue by 2026.
- Source: TradingKey — KLAC June 29 +7.11%

H. GLW Corning — Post-Reconstitution Reversal + CEO Insider Selling

GLW +14.03% on June 29 was purely mechanical (Russell 1000 Growth reconstitution passive buying). June 30 data shows -1.72% reversal already in progress as reconstitution buying is complete.

Two new negatives:
1. CEO Insider Selling: CEO Wendell Weeks + multiple SVPs sold 160,000+ shares worth $30.7M with zero offsetting insider buying. Significant negative signal for medium-term holders.
2. Solar Plant Problems: Production bottlenecks and startup inefficiencies at the new solar wafer plant: extended maintenance shutdown + hardware upgrades → $30M additional Q2 2026 operating expenses.

Analyst consensus PT: $204.30 (11 analysts, Buy) — stock was ~$255 post-spike, implying ~20% overvaluation vs. consensus. The AI fiber narrative (dominant supplier to hyperscalers building AI data center interconnects) drives the 12-1 momentum signal but the stock is now trading well above analyst targets.
- Sources: TradingKey — GLW June 29 | Public.com — GLW Forecast 2026

I. WDC/STX — Fox Advisors Double Downgrade (New Caution Flag)

Both WDC ($651.88, +11.2% June 29) and STX ($968.53, +7.6% June 29) received simultaneous Fox Advisors downgrades to Equal-Weight from Outperform. Concern: "expectations for HDD pricing may be getting ahead" of likely increases. This is a valuation-stretch thesis after 250%+ YTD gains.

Counter: Cantor Fitzgerald raised WDC to $900 (Overweight) and STX to $1,300 (Overweight); Melius Research initiated WDC at Buy with $1,050 target. Seagate redeeming $150.7M in debt (balance sheet strengthening). The AI training-data-lake demand for high-capacity HDD is structural, but pricing expectations may be running ahead.

Both names held across all four systematic sleeves. The downgrade is a watch flag, not a thesis break.
- Sources: StockAnalysis — WDC Overview | Globe and Mail — Seagate WDC Soaring

J. Bloom Energy (BE) — July 30 Earnings Upcoming; High Volatility

BE closed June 29 at $275.01 (+9.1%), recovering from -18.49% crash on June 26 (Russell rebalancing). June 30 pre-market range indicated: $261–$284. Next catalyst: July 30, 2026 earnings date. BE is up 1,300% over the past year. GF Value puts intrinsic value at $26.80 (stock at 926% premium). Barclays PT $276 (Equal Weight) is essentially at the current price.

The AI data-center power generation narrative (Bloom's solid oxide fuel cells for on-site power) drives 12-1 momentum. The July 30 earnings are a binary event that could reset the thesis in either direction.
- Source: GuruFocus — BE +9.1% Jun 29 GF Score 59

K. Fabrinet (FN) — Momentum Decay Despite Strong Fundamentals

FN (held in mom_roa and mom_v1 at $725.36 entry) shows $624.50 in June 30 search data (-4.53% early session indicated). Down 18.4% over past 30 days. Strong fundamentals: Q3 FY2026 revenue +39% YoY to $1.21B, net income +54%. Analyst consensus: 5 Buy / 2 Hold, $749 average PT ($635-$850 range).

FN was recently added to a leveraged single-stock ETF lineup (June 20) — increases short-term speculation and volatility. The momentum decay despite excellent fundamentals is a classic divergence between business quality and price momentum. The July 1 rebalance will likely exit FN from the top-50 lists (mom_roa, mom_v2) based on its 30-day decline.
- Source: Simply Wall St — FN Leveraged ETF | Perplexity — FN Stock

L. GOOG/GOOGL — Dow Jones Debut, Rough June

GOOG/GOOGL completed their first Dow Jones session June 29 (+4% on inclusion day, replacing Verizon). June 30 indicated price: ~$350.80. Alphabet's June has been brutal: worst month since February 2022, down in 6 of past 7 weeks. Big Tech AI capex projected at $725B for 2026 collectively — Alphabet is a key contributor to this spend, positioning Google Cloud and DeepMind as beneficiaries of AI infrastructure.

Portfolio impact: GOOG and GOOGL are held in residual_roa_6535 (both classes, ~$1,998 each, 2026-05-01 entry). Entry prices: GOOG $383.41, GOOGL $385.88. At ~$350.80, both are modestly underwater from entry.
- Sources: GuruFocus — GOOGL Joins Dow | CNBC — Alphabet Joins Dow | Motley Fool — Alphabet Joins Dow June 30

M. Iran Ceasefire / Oil / XLE Sector Dynamics

US-Iran MoU in force; Strait of Hormuz reopened; naval blockade lifted. WTI crude: $73.61 (Brent $77.68) — approximately 20% below 2026 peak ($120+). XLE declined even on June 29's strong day (+0% or negative) while XLK rose 2.4%.

Sector overlay XLE veto (June 12, score 3) fully vindicated. The original rationale — "Hormuz supply-shock trade reversing on ceasefire news" — has played out exactly as predicted. The control sector_top4 sleeve holds XLE at 25% weight (entered $58.88, May 1) and is losing on this position versus the treatment's cash slot.

Whether XLE remains in the top-4 sectors for July 1 scoring is unknown without the signal data, but with oil at $73.61 vs. 2026 highs of $120+, XLE's 12-1 momentum has severely weakened. The July 1 sector rebalance may naturally eject XLE from the top 4, resolving the veto position.
- Sources: CNBC — Oil Drops 20% | Al Jazeera — US-Iran MoU Oil Falls | Intellectia — US-Iran Ceasefire Impact

N. AFJK Aimei Health — July 6 SPAC Going-Concern Deadline

AFJK (held in mom_roa, mom_v1, mom_v2 at $40.89 entry) has a signed Business Combination Agreement with United Hydrogen dependent on CSRC (Chinese regulatory) approval. Q1 2026 10-Q contains explicit going-concern language: "substantial doubt about going concern if no deal is completed by the July 6, 2026 combination deadline." Outside-trust cash: $18,516; working capital deficit: ~$3.5M as of March 31, 2026.

Most recent available price: $43.00 (June 9). If the deal fails by July 6, the SPAC liquidates at trust NAV (~$10/share) — a ~75% loss from the June 9 price.

July 1 rebalance should exit AFJK from all three sleeves. The momentum signal that originally placed it in portfolios has turned deeply negative; the July 1 score almost certainly drops it below any threshold.
- Sources: StockTitan — AFJK Q1 10-Q | Yahoo Finance — AFJK

O. Semiconductor Equipment Sector — Lam Research (LRCX) and UCTT

LRCX closed June 29 +6.62%. Bank of America raised PT to $480 (from prior level), Wells Fargo to $450. Record $2B quarterly revenue in Customer Support Business Group. LRCX tools are used in advanced memory (HBM), foundry logic, packaging, and high-bandwidth memory — the entire AI infrastructure capex stack.

UCTT (Ultra Clean Holdings) closed June 29 +10%, reaching an all-time high of $125.10. Now +452% over the past year, +230% YTD. 59% of revenue from Lam Research and AMAT — direct beneficiary of WFE spending acceleration. Pre-market: stock continuing at $125.35.
- Sources: TradingKey — LRCX June 29 +6.62% | Investing.com — UCTT All-Time High $125 | StockTitan — UCTT Overview

Last decision: June 12, 2026. VETO on AAOI (score 4.0, invalidation $150.00). AAOI has since fallen from $169.13 to $138.54 (-18.1%). VETO correct.

1.6T shipment catalyst (July 1): If AAOI 8-K confirms shipment start tomorrow, this is bullish for AAOI price. Even if AAOI rallies, the treatment leads by $18.2K true — the veto was the right call. The experiment enters a new phase at July 1 when the control exits AAOI (or retains it if rank holds) and potentially takes a new #1 position.

CRITICAL: New evaluation required. The July 1 rebalance will change the control sleeve's holding to whatever name is ranked #1 in mom_roa signal on June 30 close. The treatment must evaluate this new name before the July 1 buy order executes. This eval is OVERDUE.

CORRECTION to prior report's AAOI momentum analysis: The prior report stated AAOI would exit July 1 on "negative 12-1 momentum." This was based on confusing the June 12 sleeve entry price ($169.13) with the June 2025 base price (~$27-28). AAOI's 12-1 return is ~+399% YoY — the momentum signal is strongly positive. AAOI exits the top-50 (if it does) on RANK, not on negative signal.

6. Strategic Insights

A. H1 2026 Final Scorecard — 60 Trading Days of Forward Evidence

At today's 5:15pm MTM, the H1 record is sealed. Based on June 29 data (essentially the H1 final):
- All four systematic sleeves beat SPY (+2.82%) by 4–9pp inception
- ROA-filtered sleeves (mom_roa +10.36%, residual_roa +11.76%) outperform pure momentum (mom_v1 +6.86%, mom_v2 +8.07%)
- Both sector sleeves underperformed SPY: sector_top4 +2.42%, llm_overlay_sector +0.25%
- LLM stock overlay treatment holds cash and leads control by $18.2K (true) — a successful VETO, but not positive alpha from LLM discretion

60 trading days is directionally positive for the factor approach but below the 12–18 month threshold for statistical edge declaration. The ROA filter's outperformance over pure momentum across this specific period is the most interesting signal: the quality-profitability requirement adds defensive properties (lower drawdown in June 23-26 selloff) while maintaining upside capture. Continue tracking.

B. Window Dressing = Final Inflated Signal for July 1 Scoring

Today's institutional buying of Q2 winners artificially inflates the momentum signal scores at the close. This means the July 1 rebalance buys names at slightly inflated prices (window dressing is done by 4pm). However, this bias is symmetric — the exit prices for names leaving the portfolio also benefit from the window dressing bid. Net impact: likely small, but the first few days of July may see a modest reversal of June 30's window-dressing spike in individual names.

C. KLAC Short Interest Spike Is the Portfolio's Most Unusual Market Microstructure Signal

36.6% of float sold short in KLAC is remarkable. The most likely explanation: the 10-for-1 split dramatically increased the share count and share borrowing availability, creating new short-selling capacity that didn't exist at $1,727/share. The short sellers are betting on valuation mean reversion (70x P/E). The counter is that KLAC's WFE tools are in structural demand for every advanced chip fab worldwide (AI, HBM, advanced packaging). A sustained squeeze scenario is plausible given the extreme short positioning.

Portfolio impact is modest (KLAC is one position in residual_roa at ~$3,222 value), but the dynamic is worth watching for the broader semiconductor equipment thesis.

D. AAOI: The 12-1 Momentum Signal Is Positive — July 1 Outcome Is a Rank Question

Correcting the prior report's error: AAOI has ~+399% 12-1 return. It will only exit the portfolios if its score ranks below the top-N threshold. With the 1.6T shipments starting July 1 and Q2 guidance of $180-198M revenue (another record), the fundamental backdrop remains intact. The -22% price decline over the past month reflects valuation mean-reversion from a stretched level, not fundamental deterioration.

For the LLM experiment: If AAOI exits the control sleeve on rank (not negative signal), the experiment gets a new test case tomorrow. If AAOI remains in the top-50 and the control retains it, the treatment must decide whether to buy (after its prior VETO). Either way, a decision is required before July 1 execution.

E. The Biotech Cluster in mom_v1 — Natural Culling Expected at Rebalance

Mom_v1's accumulation of clinical-stage biotechs (CAPR, PTN, CELC, PRAX, STRO, RVMD, CNTA, COGT, SION, FBRX, PVLA, FTH, TYRA, NKTR, INBX, JBIO, DRUG, AMLX, SRZN) is the momentum factor doing its job: biotech stocks with clinical momentum get elevated prices, triggering the signal. But many of these have 30-60 day momentum turning negative (PTN -23.4%, CAPR -13.9%, CELC -18.4%), and the natural rebalance will cull them without requiring any manual action. The CAPR PDUFA (Aug 22) is the highest-priority binary biotech risk still in the portfolio.

F. Sector_top4 Underperformance Analysis

Both sector sleeves underperformed SPY in H1 (+2.42% control, +0.25% treatment vs SPY +2.82%). The underperformance drivers:
1. XLE allocation (25% weight) in the control dragged returns as oil fell 20% from peak
2. The sector rotation strategy has a structural lower-beta profile vs. the AI-driven individual stock sleeves
3. The llm_overlay_sector treatment gave up the XLK/XLI/XLB gains by holding 25% cash

The sector strategy's long-term raison d'être is diversification and drawdown control, not raw return maximization. At 60 days of data, the appropriate response is to continue tracking, not to alter the strategy.

Critical outstanding items: (1) July 1 rebalance execution tomorrow — score lock completed at today's close; (2) AAOI 1.6T shipments begin July 1 — binary catalyst for re-entering overlay experiment; (3) Iran-Qatar talks July 1 — fragile ceasefire continues; (4) NFP Thursday July 2 — key Fed rate path input; (5) LLM overlay eval for new mom_roa #1 name still OVERDUE; (6) Verify ECHO/SATS ticker fix in DB before rebalance execution.

1. Market Summary — June 30, 2026 (Q2 Close)

A. Index Closes

Index Close Day Change Q2 Return (Apr 1-Jun 30) Notable
S&P 500 7,499.36 +0.79% +13.5% Best Q since Q1 2020
Nasdaq Composite 26,213.72 +1.52% +20.0% Best Q since Q4 2020
Dow Jones 52,319.20 +0.26% ~+8% New all-time high close
SPY ETF (portfolio benchmark) $746.77 +0.78% — DB price_cache confirmed

Source: TheStreet — Stock Market Today June 30 | Yahoo Finance — Stock Market News Jun 30 | Schwab — Stocks Open Flat to End Best Quarter | BNN Bloomberg — US Stocks Rise and Trim June Losses

B. Primary Market Drivers

1. Quarter-End Window Dressing
Today was the final session of Q2 2026 — the best quarter for the S&P 500 and Nasdaq since 2020. Institutional funds executing quarter-end window dressing (buying winners, selling losers before sending client statements) provided a systematic tailwind for high-momentum names. AI infrastructure, semiconductors, and our portfolio's concentrated momentum positions all benefited from this mechanical bid. The semiconductor ETF SMH climbed more than 3% on the day.

2. Broad AI Semiconductor Continuation
The Micron Q3 blowout (June 25, $41.46B revenue, +346% YoY) continued to reverberate through the supply chain. AMD +7.7%, Nvidia +2.6%, Intel +6%, Lam Research +5.46%. The AI infrastructure upcycle narrative is deepening: Micron alone contributed 4.5 points of S&P 500 EPS growth per Seeking Alpha analysis, anchoring the AI capex ROI argument that Oracle's Q4 (June 9) had challenged. Source: Intellectia AI — Chip Stocks Rebound Strategy June 2026

3. Iran-Qatar Ceasefire Stability
Both sides maintained the ceasefire and proceeded to Doha talks. Oil remained range-bound (WTI ~$73; Brent ~$77). No escalation means energy names continued to underperform vs. tech on this session — XLE -0.88% vs XLK +2.76%. The Iran-Hormuz risk premium continues to fade from equities while the peace dividend accrues to AI infrastructure and broad risk-on assets. Source: Al Jazeera — Oil Prices Rise as US-Iranian Strikes Threaten Strait Reopening | CNBC — Oil Drops 20% from 2026 Peak on Ceasefire Optimism

4. Nike Earnings Beat (After Bell)
Nike (NKE) reported Q4 FY2026 after the close: EPS 20c vs 13c expected (+54% beat), revenue $10.97B vs $10.86B expected. Full-year revenue $46.4B. Q4 gross margin expanded +8.9%, aided by a ~$986M tariff refund following Supreme Court action on global tariff duties. Weakness: Greater China -12%, Converse -30%+. Nike is not held in any portfolio sleeve, but the beat is a positive macro signal. Source: NIKE SEC 8-K Q4 FY2026 | CNBC — Nike NKE Q4 2026 Earnings | MarketBeat — NKE Q4 2026 Report

5. JOLTS + Consumer Confidence (June 30 AM)
Both the June JOLTS Job Openings and Conference Board Consumer Confidence were released pre-market. Based on available reporting, both releases appeared to beat or meet expectations (JOLTS forecast 7.29M, CB Consumer Confidence forecast 94.4). The market's positive reaction is consistent with beat readings. Exact actuals were not confirmed in available search sources — NFP Thursday July 2 (consensus 170K) is the more market-moving labor release. Source: XTB — Economic Calendar JOLTS Consumer Confidence Nike | YouTube — JOLTs and Consumer Confidence Beat Expectations

A. Top Gainers

MXL (MaxLinear) — +18.03% — Portfolio's Largest Single-Name Day Gain

Jun 29 Close Jun 30 Close Inception P&L (entry $91.44)
Price $108.47 $128.03 +40.0%

Two Wall Street analysts more than doubled their price targets on June 30:
- Northland Capital: PT raised to $110 (Outperform), citing rising AI optical demand and multi-year AI infrastructure upcycle
- Stifel: PT raised to $105 (from $49), highlighting rerating of AI-focused data center chip names

Additional catalyst: MaxLinear announced a collaboration with Los Alamos National Laboratory demonstrating up to ~39x write and ~7x read speedups for OpenZFS storage — reinforcing the high-performance data center infrastructure thesis. MXL makes high-speed analog/mixed-signal semiconductors for broadband and data-center interconnect. Present in: mom_roa, mom_v1, mom_v2. Sources: StocksToTrade — MXL AI Data Boom June 30 | Timothy Sykes — MXL Stock Rips Higher | TIKR — MaxLinear 76% Post-Q1 Rally

BE (Bloom Energy) — +10.07% — Recovers ABOVE Entry Price

Jun 25 Jun 26 Close Jun 29 Close Jun 30 Close vs. Entry ($290.67)
Price $351.28 (ATH) ~$252 $275.01 $302.70 +4.1% (was -13.3%)

This is one of the most significant position developments this week. After BE's brutal -12.3% drop on June 26 (AI capex sentiment selloff, not fundamentals), the stock recovered to above the May 1 entry price in just two sessions.

Catalysts today:
- UBS reiterated Buy, lifting price target to $322 (from $280), citing new FERC rules and "bring your own power" trends that support demand for BE's solid oxide fuel cells
- CEO Elliott (BE) confirmed the company does not plan to raise equity despite surging AI data center demand and the Oracle 2.8GW deal
- Fundamental thesis unchanged: $20-24B backlog, Oracle 2.8GW, Brookfield $5B partnership

Present in: mom_roa, mom_v1, mom_v2, residual_roa (all four). Sources: StocksToTrade — Bloom Energy Rallies AI Power Crunch June 30 | StockAnalysis — Bloom Energy Overview

4. LLM Overlay Treatment vs. Control

A. Experiment 1 (mom_roa_top1) — CLOSED at June 30 Re-Inception

Both the control (mom_roa_top1_paper) and treatment (llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper) were reset to $100,000 today (June 30) in preparation for the July 1 rebalance.

Final standings at experiment close-out:

Control (AAOI concentrated) Treatment (cash since Jun 12 VETO)
Last DB NAV (Jun 29) $88,757.18 (overstated — AAOI price error) $100,000.00
True estimated NAV (Jun 29) ~$81,829 (-18.17%) $100,000 (0.00%)
AAOI June 30 close (DB) $148.16 —
True estimated final NAV ~$87,400 (~-12.6%) $100,000
Treatment lead at close-out — ~+12.6pp to +18.2pp

Note: AAOI June 29 DB price of $150.10 was flagged as potentially overstated in the pre-market report (web sources cited ~$138.54). Final treatment advantage range depends on which June 29 AAOI base is used. Both scenarios show the treatment materially outperforming.

Experiment 1 Summary: The treatment (cash since the June 12 VETO of AAOI at $169.13) outperformed the control (hold AAOI) by approximately 12-18pp over the 18-day live experiment. The LLM's VETO rationale ("extreme valuation ~110-215x fwd with net losses after ~10x 12m run; momentum rolling over") was directionally correct — AAOI declined from $169.13 to $148.16 over the period, a -12.4% move vs cash flat. The gap was as wide as +19.75pp (June 26) before AAOI's +10.6% recovery on June 29 narrowed it.

B. llm_overlay_sector_top4 — CLOSED at Re-Inception

Final standing: Treatment $100,250 (+0.25%) vs Control sector_top4_full $103,452 (+3.50%) = Control leads by +3.25pp. The XLE veto was directionally correct (XLE -0.88% today, continuing its post-Iran-peak decline), but the XLK, XLI, and XLB allocations in the control outperformed the cash slot in the treatment. Being right on ONE sector veto is not enough to overcome losing the returns on 3 fully-deployed positions.

C. New Experimental Sleeves (initialized June 30)

Two new sleeves appeared in the DB today:
- llm_cascade_top1_paper — $100,000 at inception (June 30). Likely a "cascade" LLM decision architecture (multi-stage evaluation) vs. the prior single-prompt overlay.
- llm_cascade_sector4_paper — $100,000 at inception (June 30). Sector equivalent.

Both sleeves have no positions and no logged decisions yet. Their design intent is inferred from their names; no documentation was found in available DB tables. Additionally:
- sector_top4_paper was re-incepted at $100,000 — appears to be a fresh control sleeve.
- All original overlay sleeves also reset: mom_roa_top1_paper, llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper, llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper.

AAOI July 1 1.6T Catalyst (imminent for new experiment decisions):
The $200M+ 1.6T transceiver shipment to a long-term hyperscaler customer begins tomorrow (July 1). AAOI's 12-1 momentum (current $148 vs ~$169 Jun 2025 baseline) is likely now negative, meaning AAOI probably exits the control's signal and a new name enters. The new control position TBD — requires running overlay_prep.bat and identifying the new #1 mom_roa name. Source: GlobeNewswire — AOI Receives First Volume 1.6T Order

5. Q2 2026 Period Summary (2-Month Live Period: May 1 to June 30)

Today marks the close of the second calendar quarter of 2026 and the completion of the portfolio's first two months of live paper trading.

Q2 2026 macro context: The S&P 500 returned +13.5% for the full quarter (April through June 30), its best quarterly performance since Q1 2020, despite the ongoing Iran war. The Nasdaq returned +20%, its best quarterly gain since Q4 2020. Technology led (+28% for the quarter) while healthcare, industrials, and financials rotated higher in late June. Source: TheStreet — Tech Recovery Nasdaq S&P Jumping June 30 | Alexandria Capital — 2026 Mid-Year Review

Our portfolio's May 1 to June 30 (61 trading days) performance vs. SPY:

The two-month period included six major regime events:
1. June 5 Broadcom guidance miss — $1.3T semiconductor wipeout; worst tech day in 2026
2. June 10 Iran escalation + CPI 4.2% — dual risk-off selloff; Dow -900 pts
3. June 12-13 history-gap backfill re-inception — six contaminated sleeves reset
4. June 25 Micron Q3 blowout — +346% YoY, HBM supercycle confirmed; memory/storage rips
5. June 26 AI capex doubt selloff — BE -12.3%, WDC -10.6%, OpenAI IPO delay uncertainty
6. June 29-30 Iran ceasefire + quarter-end window dressing — tech/AI names recapture losses

Despite six significant regime events, all four systematic factor sleeves materially outperformed SPY over the full period. This is directionally positive evidence for factor alpha, though the 61-trading-day sample is far below the minimum 12-18 months needed for statistical confidence.

Key statistical notes:
- The history-gap re-inception on June 13 was a portfolio reset; prior data (May 1-June 12) for the reconstituted sleeves should be weighted cautiously
- sector_top4_full (-0.12pp vs SPY) is essentially tracking SPY — the sector rotation factor has not yet demonstrated edge in this market regime
- residual_roa's outperformance is partially attributable to the KLAC split correction which understated its NAV until the June 29 fix

6. Strategic Insights

A. BE and VRT Recovery: Sentiment Selloffs Are Reversible

The June 26 selloffs in BE (-12.3%) and VRT (-4.3%) were pure AI-capex sentiment correlation, not fundamental deterioration. Both stocks recovered to above entry within two trading sessions:
- BE: $252 to $302.70 (+20.1% in 2 days) — recovered full entry cushion, now +4.1%
- VRT: $303.95 to $334.82 (+10.2% in 2 days) — recovered full entry cushion, now +1.9%

The lesson: sentiment-driven drawdowns on momentum names tend to be fast and deep but are also quickly reversed when the sentiment trigger fades. The July 1 rebalance occurs on fundamentals (12-1 momentum) — a single week's price action does not override a 12-month signal.

B. MXL's +18% Shows the Analyst PT Cascade Effect in Mid-Caps

MaxLinear's +18% day was triggered by two analysts more than doubling their price targets (Northland to $110, Stifel to $105). MXL was at $91.44 at our May 1 entry and is now $128.03 (+40%). The "analyst PT cascade" is a well-documented momentum amplifier: a PT raise in a stock with a strong 12-month signal attracts additional institutional attention. MXL is a small enough cap that two analyst upgrades can meaningfully move the stock. This dynamic is precisely what the momentum signal is designed to capture.

C. VSAT Has Transformed From Pure Momentum to Multi-Layer Thesis

VSAT entered the portfolio on purely mechanical momentum grounds. Over the past two days, it has accumulated:
- Government contract anchor: $437.7M U.S. Space Force PTS-G award (multi-year)
- Analyst consensus shift: Three firms doubled PTs (Oppenheimer $140, Deutsche Bank $97, Needham $90)
- M&A speculation: Rocket Lab's $8B Iridium acquisition makes VSAT a speculative buyout target

A stock that entered as a "12-1 momentum score" is now a fundamentally reinforced position. Up 44.9% from June 26 over two days and likely to retain a very strong momentum score at July 1.

D. sector_top4_full is Lagging and the Issue is Structural

sector_top4_full returned only +1.00% today vs SPY +0.78% — essentially neutral alpha despite a strong tech day. Since inception: +3.50% vs SPY +3.62% = -0.12pp underperformance. The sector rotation factor has not shown edge vs SPY in the May-June period. The XLE drag (-0.88%) is a persistent headwind in a ceasefire environment. Worth flagging for the post-July-1 research queue: the sector momentum signal may need to update more frequently than monthly to capture regime rotation.

E. Quarter-End Window Dressing Amplified Real Momentum

Today's +3.66% (mom_roa) and +3.04% (mom_v2) outperformance vs SPY +0.78% occurred on the highest window-dressing day of the year. Institutional funds systematically buy the quarter's winners at quarter-end, structurally amplifying the momentum factor. The MXL PT cascade, BE UBS upgrade, and INTC Cantor PT raise are fundamental developments that would have occurred on any trading day; the quarter-end timing amplified market reaction. In H2, momentum names will not have this structural boost — the sustainability test will come from fundamental strength rather than institutional flow timing.

The one verifiable pre-market signal: S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 futures were modestly lower Wednesday morning ("S&P 500 futures slip after strongest quarter since 2020," Nasdaq 100 futures ~30,398 vs. an earlier print of 30,444.75), attributed to caution around the Doha Iran talks after Q2's best quarterly rally since 2020. NFP (Thursday July 2, consensus 170K) remains the week's key print.

3. Catalyst and Macro Review

A. Iran-Qatar talks, ongoing (July 1)
US and Iranian officials are holding indirect, low-level technical talks through Qatari and Pakistani mediators in Doha today; Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi met Qatar's PM to discuss formalizing the tentative ceasefire agreement. No direct US-Iran meeting is planned. Oil traders reportedly are treating the truce "as a permanent deal," which is consistent with Brent's continued slide. This is a structural headwind for residual_roa_6535's energy cluster and a modest tailwind (via lower input costs / de-risking) for broad equities. Source: CNN — Live Updates: US and Iranian Officials Holding Indirect Talks in Qatar

B. ISM Manufacturing PMI (10:00 ET today) — not yet released as of this report
The June ISM Manufacturing print is scheduled for 10:00 a.m. ET July 1. Prior comparable ISM Manufacturing readings this cycle have been in the low-50s (May actual 54.0 vs. 53.3 forecast, 52.7 prior per one source) — search results were inconsistent enough (one source cited a "102.5" prior that appears to reference a different sub-index) that no specific July 1 print number should be assumed. This is a watch item, not a known result. Source: FXMacroData — ISM Manufacturing PMI July 2026 | ISM — PMI Reports

C. Fed — Sintra ECB Forum
Fed Chair Kevin Warsh appeared at the ECB's central bank forum in Sintra, Portugal on June 30/July 1. No new rate-path guidance was reported beyond the standing June FOMC dot plot (fed funds 3.50-3.75%; 9 of 19 members projecting at least one more hike in 2026, 8 projecting no change) — a meaningfully more hawkish tilt than the "one-to-two cuts" priced earlier in the year. No FOMC meeting occurs this week; the next scheduled Fed commentary of substance is likely light given the July 4 holiday week. Source: CNBC — Kevin Warsh ECB Forum Live Updates | Federal Reserve — June 17, 2026 FOMC Projections

D. Semiconductor/AI infrastructure sector — no reversal signal overnight
No new negative catalyst was found for the semiconductor complex overnight. World Semiconductor Trade Statistics estimates 2026 industry sales growth of ~90% to $1.5T globally. The benchmark Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ) is +99% YTD. This context supports continuation of the AI-capex thesis underlying MU, WDC, TER, VICR, STX, LRCX, KLAC, ICHR, MXL, ONTO, and other portfolio holdings, though the sector remains vulnerable to sentiment-driven air-pockets as seen June 5 (Broadcom guidance miss) and June 26 (AI-capex doubt selloff). Source: Intellectia AI — Chip Stocks Rebound Strategy June 2026

E. Micron (MU) — new legal risk flag
Micron, the portfolio's single largest position by exposure ($14,880 across all 4 factor sleeves, present in every systematic sleeve), now faces a new price-fixing lawsuit alongside heightened regulatory scrutiny of memory-market pricing strategy, per aggregated coverage. No specifics (plaintiff, jurisdiction, damages sought) were available in the search results — this needs a dedicated follow-up search once case details surface. MU also has a $0.15/share dividend with an ex-date of July 6, 2026. Source: Public.com — Micron Forecast/News

F. Global macro tail-risk backdrop (non-portfolio-specific, context only)
Several outlets flagged systemic-risk themes for early July: business development companies (BDCs) turning unprofitable industry-wide amid rising borrowing costs (Reuters); some Chinese hedge fund managers publicly calling the AI trade "unsustainable" and "ready to burst" (Yahoo Finance); the Bank of England modeling AI-driven "flash crash" scenarios and discussing trading kill-switches (Telegraph); the Japanese yen at 40-year lows with JGB-selloff contagion risk flagged (FXStreet); South Korea's won near 2009 lows on foreign equity selling (Bloomberg). None of these are tied to specific portfolio holdings, but they are relevant as background volatility risk for a book concentrated in AI-infrastructure momentum names. Source: Climate and Economy — July 1, 2026 Economic Roundup

This applies to mom_roa_top1_paper (control), llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper (original single-prompt treatment), llm_cascade_top1_paper (new cascade-design treatment), sector_top4_paper (re-incepted control), llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper (original macro-veto treatment), and llm_cascade_sector4_paper (new cascade-design treatment).

B. The July 1 decisions are already logged in the DB (llm_overlay_log, sector_overlay_log, both timestamped 2026-06-30 17:36-17:40 UTC) — meaning the "OVERDUE" LLM-eval items flagged in the 6/30 report are in fact complete; they simply have not been executed into trades yet.

Inferred (not confirmed): if the cascade design back-fills vetoed slots by continuing down the ranked list, the new cascade sector sleeve would hold XLK/XLI/XLB/XLV — i.e., swap Energy out for Healthcare relative to the existing sector_top4_full control (XLB/XLE/XLI/XLK). Whether the original (non-cascade) llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper treatment uses the same 6-ETF evaluation or applies its older single-veto-to-cash logic to just the original 4 sectors is not resolved by the log alone — both designs' actual position construction should be checked once today's rebalance executes.

C. AAOI's 1.6T shipment milestone starts today (July 1) — the binary catalyst flagged repeatedly in prior reports. AAOI's business fundamentals event (start of $200M+ 1.6T transceiver shipments to a hyperscaler) is now live, even though AAOI has been displaced from the #1 mom_roa cascade slot. AAOI remains held in mom_roa_6535, mom_v1, and mom_v2 (top-50/top-100 sleeves), so the shipment-start catalyst is still portfolio-relevant beyond the single-name overlay experiments. 6. Strategic Insights

A. The cascade LLM design is now empirically distinct from the original single-prompt overlay, and today is the first real test of that distinction. The original overlay made one up/down call per candidate (buy AAOI, veto BE, etc.). The new cascade design chains through multiple candidates in ranked order until it finds a BUY (stock side: BE→SLGL→WDC) or fills all available slots (sector side: 6 ETFs evaluated to fill 4 slots). This is a structurally different bet: the cascade design should, by construction, almost never end up in cash for long on the sector side (it keeps searching until slots fill), whereas the original macro-veto design could sit partially in cash indefinitely. If the goal of running two overlay generations in parallel is to isolate "does search depth help," today's picks (WDC over BE/SLGL; XLV swapped in for XLE) are the first live data point. No conclusion is possible yet — both new cascade sleeves have zero trading days.

B. The BE veto shows the same discipline pattern as its May 29 veto did. BE was vetoed on May 29, 2026 at $290 with a nearly identical rationale (rich valuation, single-customer concentration, above analyst PT) and BE ran from ~$290 to briefly $351 before crashing to $252 and recovering to $302.70 by June 30 — a round-trip that validated caution more than it validated timing (an investor who "waited for a pullback toward 220" as the May 29 note suggested never got that entry). The July 1 veto uses a similar but updated rationale (valuation even richer at ~128x fwd EPS, momentum now cooling -20.5% off 52-week high). Whether repeated over-cautious vetoes on a name with genuinely strong fundamentals become a structural drag on the treatment sleeve's returns is worth tracking explicitly as the experiment accumulates decisions — one data point so far.

C. sector_top4_full's persistent SPY-tracking (-0.12pp since inception) predates any LLM overlay and is a separate finding from the overlay experiments. The underlying systematic sector-momentum signal (XLB/XLE/XLI/XLK, unchanged since May 1) has not demonstrated edge over SPY in two months. Today's cascade evaluation independently arrives at swapping Energy for Healthcare — consistent with the human-observable fact that Energy has been the sleeve's structural drag (XLE -0.88% just on 6/30, oil down another leg since). If the swap executes and Healthcare's inclusion improves relative performance, that would be modest evidence that manual/LLM sector selection can add value where the pure-momentum ranking has stalled — but this is a single-name-swap test on a two-month-old sleeve with no statistical power yet.

D. No new evidence today changes the standing assessment that the four systematic factor sleeves (mom_roa_6535, residual_roa_6535, mom_v2, mom_v1) have a real, if short-sample, edge over SPY (+6.3pp to +10.8pp over 61 trading days across six distinct regime shocks), while the sector-rotation and LLM-overlay concepts remain unproven. Today's data (futures caution, soft oil, no negative semiconductor catalyst, one new legal risk flag on MU, and a completed-but-unexecuted LLM cascade decision) does not materially change that picture. The most substantive new "sleeve concept" evidence today is structural rather than return-based: the cascade methodology itself (evaluate N candidates until one clears the bar) is now live and testable against the simpler single-veto design, which is a genuinely new axis of experimentation rather than a re-run of the existing momentum-factor question.

Day moves: S&P 500 -0.22% to 7,483.23 | Nasdaq -0.66% to 26,040.03 | Dow -0.03% to 52,305.24 (intraday ATH 52,742.66 before a Caterpillar-led reversal). Broad market roughly flat-to-down on the first session of Q3, but memory/chip-equipment names sold off hard in isolation: Micron -10.57% to -10.6%, Lam Research -9.71%, KLA -11.77%, Teradyne -11.68%, Aehr -12.11%, MaxLinear -12.22%, Corning -13.62%(biggest single loser), Intel -9.03% — while mega-cap tech held up (MSFT +3.02%, AAPL +1.73%, NVDA -1.25%). XLK -2.57% (dragged by chip cluster), XLC +2.44% and XLF +2.18% led sector gainers.

Key movers with reason: MU/LRCX/KLAC/TER/AEHR/MXL/INTC crushed by (1) a new DRAM antitrust class-action filed June 25 vs. Micron/Samsung/SK Hynix alleging coordinated DDR3/DDR4 supply cuts, (2) SK Hynix's $29.4B Nasdaq ADR listing (ticker SKHY, targeted July 10) stoking competitive/dilution anxiety in memory, (3) fresh US export restrictions on chipmaking tools to China (Hua Hong Semiconductor) hitting equipment names, and (4) broad profit-taking after semis ran +80% in H1 2026. GLW -13.62% (idiosyncratic — AI-driven parabolic run + FTSE Russell index-inclusion rally pushed P/E past 105x; heavy June insider selling by CEO Wendell Weeks + SVPs, 160k+ shares). WDC -6.32% (swept into the same profit-taking despite BofA raising PT to $732 and five other banks raising targets to $685 on tight HDD supply/AI storage demand) — notable because WDC is today's LLM-cascade stock pick. AFJK -12.84% (small-float SPAC volatility into its July 6 merger deadline, no fresh negative catalyst found). Gainers: BETR +9.78%, PARR +4.85% (refiner benefiting from softer crude input costs), DINO +4.18%, PACS +3.94%.

Macro event: June ISM Manufacturing PMI 53.3% (6th straight month of expansion, down 0.7pt from May's 54%; Prices Index eased to 73% from 82.1%). Brent -0.96% to ~$72.25, WTI -1.3% to $68.58 on constructive US-Iran talks in Doha (Kushner/Witkoff) — negative for residual_roa_6535's ~15% energy weight (APA, DINO, PBR, INSW, FTI, NRT, TEN, UAN), though several of those names were flat-to-up today on the refining/product side. NFP releases tomorrow, July 2 (consensus 170K) — key Fed rate-path input.

LLM overlay standings: The entire 6-sleeve LLM experiment (2 controls + 2 cash-overlay treatments + 2 new "always-invested cascade" treatments) was aligned to a fresh $100k/July-1 inception on 2026-06-30 (Appendix AK/AK.5-AK.6 in the record) — today is Day 1. Decisions ARE logged for 07-01: stock control candidate = BE (raw mom_roa #1); cash overlay verdict = VETO (BE priced-for-perfection, ~128x fwd EPS, momentum cooling -20.5% off 52wk high) → stays in cash; cascade walked deeper (SLGL #2 VETO — pre-revenue biotech, binary Phase-3 readout) to WDC #3 = BUY (score 7, inval $560, AI-HDD supercycle thesis). Sector control = XLK/XLE/XLI/XLB; cash overlay = XLK/XLI/XLB HOLD, XLE VETO (fading momentum + soft oil) → 3-of-4 filled, one slot cash; cascade walked to XLV (defensive rotation, inval $150) as its 4th sector, replacing XLE. None of this had executed into paper_positions as of this report's ~17:30 CDT data pull — the monthly rebalance is scheduled for 6:03pm local (rescheduled 2026-07-01 per Appendix AP to run after the real close lands via the 5:15pm MTM pipeline) and had not yet fired. All 6 LLM sleeves + the 4 systematic 07-01 duplicates + spy_benchmark_0701 show $100,000 cash / 0 positions in the DB as of this report.

Critical outstanding items: (1) Confirm the 6:03pm 07-01 rebalance actually executed the above 11-sleeve cohort (BE/cash/WDC on stock side; XLK+XLE+XLI+XLB / XLK+XLI+XLB+cash / XLK+XLI+XLB+XLV on sector side) — first live test point for both the realigned LLM experiment and the new cascade design; (2) MU DRAM antitrust suit and SK Hynix's July 10 IPO are now live, ongoing risks for the ~3.5-3.7% MU position sitting in all four systematic sleeves; (3) NFP Thursday July 2 (170K consensus); (4) AFJK's SPAC merger deadline is July 6 — five days out, no resolution yet; (5) ARWR SHASTA-3/4 Phase 3 readout still pending, no new data today, "mid-2026" timeline unchanged.

1. Market Summary — July 1, 2026

A. Index Closes

Index Close Day Change Notable
S&P 500 7,483.23 -0.22% First down day of Q3 after best quarter since 2020
Nasdaq Composite 26,040.03 -0.66% Chip-led profit-taking
Dow Jones 52,305.24 -0.03% Intraday ATH 52,742.66 before a Caterpillar-led reversal (CAT -~7% intraday; CAT is held in residual_roa_6535, closed -6.90%)
SPY ETF (portfolio benchmark) $745.76 -0.14% DB price_cache confirmed

Source: Zacks — Stock Market News for July 1, 2026 | CNBC — Stock market news for July 1, 2026 | TheStreet — Stock Market Today July 1 2026

B. Primary Market Drivers

1. Semiconductor/memory-specific profit-taking, not a broad selloff. The Nasdaq's decline was concentrated: the Motley Fool and CNBC coverage both single out Micron (-10%+) as the session's headline mover, and our own portfolio data shows the same pattern — a cluster of memory, chip-test, and chip-equipment names down 6-14% (MU, LRCX, KLAC, TER, AEHR, MXL, INTC, AMKR, ONTO, MKSI, UCTT, COHR, CIEN, WDC, STX) while mega-cap tech (MSFT +3.02%, AAPL +1.73%) and non-tech sectors (XLC +2.44%, XLF +2.18%) were flat to higher. Reporting frames this as profit-taking after semis rallied "more than 80%" in H1 2026, compounded by two fresh, name-specific catalysts below. Source: The Motley Fool — Why Micron Stock Is Plummeting Today | CNBC — Stock market news July 1, 2026

2. New DRAM antitrust class action (filed June 25, still developing). 17 plaintiffs sued Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron in the N.D. Cal. federal court, alleging the three (who together hold ~90% of the global DRAM market) coordinated to shift ~25% of DRAM wafer capacity from commodity DDR3/DDR4 to higher-margin HBM since 2022, artificially restricting legacy-memory supply and driving prices up roughly 700% over four years. Micron has denied the allegations ("we compete vigorously, fairly and in compliance with all applicable laws"). Samsung and SK Hynix both previously pleaded guilty to criminal DRAM price-fixing in 2005. This is now a live legal overhang on MU, which is the single largest position (~3.5-3.7% weight) in all four systematic sleeves. Source: AppleInsider — Samsung, SK hynix & Micron face lawsuit over DRAM prices | Tom's Hardware — DRAM price-fixing lawsuit | PC Gamer — Memory crisis heads to court

3. SK Hynix's record $29.4B Nasdaq ADR listing (targeted July 10, ticker SKHY). The world's second-largest memory chipmaker (holds ~60% of HBM market share) filed to list 17.79M new ADR shares on Nasdaq, which would be the largest ADR listing in market history, surpassing Alibaba's 2014 debut. This introduces a large new supply of US-listed memory-sector equity just as the DRAM antitrust story breaks, and appears to be contributing to competitive/dilution anxiety across the memory complex (MU, and by extension memory-test names like AEHR and TER). Source: CNBC — SK Hynix plans to raise $29 billion via Nasdaq listing | CNBC — SK Hynix US listing price target

4. Fresh US export restrictions on chipmaking tools to China. Equipment makers were informed of new limitations on shipments to Hua Hong Semiconductor (China's #2 contract chipmaker), pressuring Lam Research, Applied Materials, and KLA on top of the memory-driven selloff — Lam -9.71%, KLA (KLAC) -11.77% in our portfolio data. Source: ElectronicsForYou — China Exposure Hits Lam Research, Applied Materials, KLA Stocks | TradingView/StockStory — MACOM, Marvell, Applied Materials, KLA, Lam Research shares falling

5. Caterpillar reversal dented the Dow intraday. The Dow hit a fresh intraday ATH of 52,742.66 before CAT (an AI-capex/infrastructure beneficiary, held in residual_roa_6535) reversed roughly 7% intraday, pulling the index back to a near-flat -0.03% close. CAT closed our books at -6.90% today. Source: Zacks — Stock Market News for July 1, 2026

6. Oil eased on constructive Iran talks. Brent fell to ~$72.25 (-0.96%) and WTI to $68.58 (-1.3%) after President Trump said US-Iran talks in Doha (led by Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff) were going well, extending the de-escalation from the June 17 memorandum of understanding. This is a continued headwind for residual_roa_6535's ~15% energy weight, though several individual energy names (DINO +4.18%, PARR +4.85%) actually rose today on refining-margin/input-cost dynamics even as crude fell. Source: CNBC — Oil prices fall after Trump says US-Iran talks in Qatar are going well

7. June ISM Manufacturing PMI: 53.3%, sixth straight month of expansion, down 0.7pt from May's 54%. New Orders Index 56% (vs. 56.8% in May); Prices Index eased notably to 73% from 82.1% — a mild disinflationary signal for the Fed ahead of Thursday's NFP. Source: PR Newswire — Manufacturing PMI at 53.3%; June 2026 ISM Manufacturing PMI Report

B. Positions near a stop or invalidation level

The four systematic momentum sleeves run no intra-month stop-loss mechanism — this was tested extensively (13 attempts, all rejected — see sleeves_verdict.md) and rejected because reactive stops degrade CAGR in-sample without a compensating held-out benefit. Positions are held until the next monthly rebalance regardless of drawdown, so today's -5% to -14% single-name moves do not trigger any automated action.

The closest thing to explicit levels in the system are the LLM overlay/cascade invalidation levels set today for the pending 07-01 rebalance:

Ticker Inval. Level Jul 1 Close Cushion
WDC (cascade stock pick) $560 $598.37 +6.8%
XLK (cascade/control sector #1) $174 $185.62 +6.7%
XLI (cascade/control sector #2) $172 $183.36 +6.6%
XLB (cascade/control sector #3) $48 $51.02 +6.3%
XLV (cascade sector #4) $150 $159.54 +6.4%

None are close to breach despite today's chip-driven selloff — XLK absorbed a -2.57% day and is still 6.7% above its invalidation level. WDC itself fell -6.32% today and remains 6.8% above its $560 invalidation level; a similar-magnitude down day tomorrow would put it within single-digit percentage points of that level, worth flagging as the position won't even be live until the 6:03pm rebalance executes.

C. New developments

  • DRAM antitrust class action (filed June 25, N.D. Cal.) against Micron/Samsung/SK Hynix is the dominant new fundamental development this week — it directly threatens the ~3.5-3.7% MU position common to all four systematic sleeves and is likely to remain an overhang through discovery/response cycles.
  • SK Hynix's $29.4B Nasdaq ADR listing targeted for July 10 is a new, dated catalyst to watch — a listing of this size in the memory space could reprice the whole DRAM/HBM complex around that date.
  • New US export restrictions on chipmaking tools to China (Hua Hong Semiconductor) is a fresh geopolitical/regulatory headwind for the equipment names (LRCX, KLAC, AMAT — AMAT not held) that wasn't in play as of yesterday's pre-market report.
  • Corning (GLW) insider selling: CEO Wendell Weeks and several SVPs sold over 160,000 shares in June per Form 4 filings — worth tracking given GLW's continued rich valuation (P/E >105x) even after today's -13.62% drop.
  • No new ARWR SHASTA-3/4 data — Phase-3 plozasiran readout timeline remains "mid-2026," unchanged from the pre-market report; ARWR itself was a modest -1.31% today, tracking broadly with the market rather than reacting to any binary catalyst.
  • AFJK's July 6 merger deadline is now five days out with no new extension or completion news found; the stock's -12.84% today appears to be ordinary high-volatility SPAC-into-catalyst trading rather than a specific negative data point.

4. LLM Overlay: Treatment vs. Control Update

Structural context: As of 2026-06-30 (Appendix AK/AK.5-AK.6 of the project record), the entire LLM-experiment cohort was reset to a synchronized $100,000/July-1 inception, and a third "always-invested cascade" design was added alongside the original single-veto ("cash") design — running as a genuinely separate pair, not a replacement, so the existing veto-vs-cash comparison isn't disturbed. This means today, 2026-07-01, is trading day 1 for all six LLM-experiment sleeves. No performance comparison is possible yet.

Today's decisions (logged in llm_overlay_log / sector_overlay_log, pending 6:03pm execution):

Stock side:
| Candidate (ranked) | Verdict | Score | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| BE (raw mom_roa #1 → control's pick) | VETO | 4 | Accelerating fundamentals (FY26 revenue guide 3.4-3.8B, ~80% YoY; Nebius/Oracle/AEP AI-datacenter deals) but ~128x fwd EPS, at/above mean PT, momentum cooling (-5.2% 1mo, -20.5% off 52wk high) into a hawkish Fed |
| SLGL (#2) | VETO | 3 | Pre-revenue single-asset clinical biotech, binary Phase-3 SGT-610 readout Q4 2026 |
| WDC (#3) | BUY | 7 | AI-HDD supercycle: Q3 revenue +45% YoY, capacity fully committed through 2028-29, EPS revisions +70% above consensus; rich (~59x) but fundamentally accelerating; inval. $560 |

  • Control (mom_roa_top1_paper): holds BE regardless of LLM verdict (pure momentum #1) — pending execution.
  • Cash-overlay treatment (llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper): BE vetoed → stays in cash — pending execution.
  • Cascade treatment (llm_cascade_top1_paper): walks past BE and SLGL to WDC (the first BUY) — pending execution.

Sector side:
| Candidate (ranked) | Verdict | Score | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| XLK | HOLD | 7 | Strongest momentum (3mo +42.7%), not overbought (RSI 51), AI-datacenter capex intact |
| XLE | VETO | 3 | Below 50DMA, -5.9% 1mo/-14.4% 3mo, RSI 26; WTI soft, Iran de-escalation removing war premium |
| XLI | HOLD | 7 | Broad strength near 52wk high, reshoring/AI-infra/electrification tailwind |
| XLB | HOLD | 5 | Marginal — just below 50DMA, but 3mo +3.6% and copper/electrification demand intact |
| XLY (#5) | VETO | 4 | Weakening consumer, hawkish-rate headwind on discretionary, weak 12-1 momentum |
| XLV (#6) | HOLD | 6 | Defensive rotation catching a bid in the hawkish/volatile regime; GLP-1/oncology tailwinds; inval. $150 |

  • Control (sector_top4_paper): XLK + XLE + XLI + XLB (pure top-4 momentum) — pending execution.
  • Cash-overlay treatment (llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper): XLK + XLI + XLB HOLD, XLE VETO → 3 of 4 slots filled, 1 slot cash — pending execution.
  • Cascade treatment (llm_cascade_sector4_paper): walks past XLE and XLY to XLV, filling all 4 slots with XLK/XLI/XLB/XLV — pending execution.

Kill switch reminder: unchanged — 12 months or ≥30 picks, drop the overlay if scores don't predict returns or treatment doesn't beat control. Honest prior remains that this is expected to fail, per the original experiment design. Zero data points exist yet under the new synchronized-inception design; the first real signal (does cascade "out-pick" raw momentum, does cash-veto avoid the busts) will start to accumulate from tomorrow's fills onward.

NAV volatility has been elevated all week — daily swings of ±3-6% have been routine since June 22 (mom_roa_6535 alone: 114,081 → 108,016 → 106,754 → 109,514 → 105,797 → 110,357 → 114,399 → 108,004), consistent with an ongoing high-volatility regime in the AI/semiconductor complex that has been building since the June 5 Broadcom-guidance selloff (~$1.3T single-day market-cap wipeout across chips) referenced in earlier reports.

6. Strategic Insights

A. Today is a live demonstration of the concentration risk inherent in pure momentum factor construction. All four systematic sleeves are 27-58% weighted to Technology (mom_roa_6535 58.4%, mom_v2 51.8%, mom_v1 45.7%, residual_roa_6535 27.1%), and within that weight, heavily clustered in semiconductor/memory/chip-equipment names specifically — MU, LRCX, KLAC/TER/AEHR/MXL/INTC/AMKR/ONTO/MKSI/UCTT/COHR/CIEN/WDC/STX all appear across multiple sleeves simultaneously. When a sub-sector-specific shock hits (today: DRAM antitrust suit + SK Hynix IPO + China export curbs), the momentum factor has no diversification against it — it bought into the winners of the last 12 months, and the winners of the last 12 months were disproportionately semiconductor names. residual_roa_6535's dual-signal construction (residual momentum × ROA, which naturally pulls in financials/healthcare/energy/consumer names alongside tech) cut today's damage roughly in half (-3.77% vs. -5.59% to -5.64% for the pure-momentum sleeves) — a real-time, single-day confirmation of the lower-drawdown finding from its original backtest validation.

B. The MU DRAM lawsuit is now a genuine, ongoing tail risk sitting in every systematic sleeve simultaneously, not a one-day event. Because MU is the single largest position (~3.5-3.7% weight) in all four sleeves and the lawsuit was filed just six days ago, this is very early in what could be a multi-month legal and competitive overhang (echoing memory's earlier-flagged "MU price-fixing lawsuit" outstanding item from June 26). The portfolio has no mechanism to reduce single-name concentration risk mid-month — a structural gap the LLM overlay experiments are implicitly testing a fix for (by substituting away from a vetoed pick), but that experiment restarted from scratch yesterday and has zero live data yet.

C. The realigned/cascade LLM overlay experiment gets its first real trading day tomorrow, on an unusually eventful entry point. The cascade design's very first live pick (WDC) is a name that fell -6.32% on the same day it was chosen — meaning the cascade sleeve will enter with a mark-to-market loss on day one relative to its entry price if the fill happens at or near today's close mechanics, though the WDC investment thesis (AI-HDD supercycle, capacity committed through 2028-29) is unrelated to today's chip-sector-wide selloff. Similarly the sector cascade's new pick (XLV, defensive healthcare rotation) is entering into a sector that was one of today's few tech-alternative gainers (+0.55%). Both are early tests of whether "always invested, walk deeper into the ranking" out-picks a design that just holds cash on a veto — no conclusion possible with zero trading days, but worth flagging that day 1 is entering right after the sharpest single-session dispersion the portfolio has shown in some time.

D. No changed conclusion on the standing four-sleeve edge-over-SPY thesis, but today adds a real stress-test data point. mom_roa_6535 (+4.52pp vs SPY), residual_roa_6535 (+5.20pp), mom_v2 (+1.59pp), and mom_v1 (+1.06pp) all still lead SPY since inception even after today's drawdown — the edge narrowed sharply today (roughly -4 to -5pp of relative performance lost in a single session for the pure-momentum sleeves) but did not disappear. Whether that edge survives a sustained semiconductor-sector correction (as opposed to a single bad day) remains the open question this factor strategy has not yet been tested against in this dataset — worth explicit tracking if the DRAM/chip-equipment overhang persists into next week.

Report generated: 2026-07-01 (post-market close, ~17:30 CDT — before the scheduled 18:03 monthly LLM rebalance fired; the 11-sleeve 07-01 cohort's pending decisions are logged in Section 4 but NOT yet reflected in paper_positions/paper_nav) | Coverage: July 1 close NAVs and positions from var/trades.db (paper_nav, paper_positions, llm_overlay_log, sector_overlay_log, price_cache), cross-referenced against docs/Project Record — Full Chronological History.md Appendices AK, AL, AP for today's structural changes | Sources: The Motley Fool — Why Micron Stock Is Plummeting Today | FX Leaders — MU Stock Heads Under $1,000 | AppleInsider — DRAM price-fixing lawsuit | Tom's Hardware — DRAM lawsuit detail | CNBC — SK Hynix $29B Nasdaq ADR listing | CNBC — SK Hynix US listing HSBC price target | ElectronicsForYou — China export curbs hit LRCX/AMAT/KLAC | TradingKey — Corning GLW -14.06% July 1 | GuruFocus — Corning significant decline | TradingKey — Western Digital WDC -7.03% July 1 | Yahoo Finance SG — WDC fair value AI storage | StockTitan — AFJK merger deadline pushed to July 6 | Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals IR — SHASTA-3/4/MUIR-3 enrollment complete | Zacks — Stock Market News for July 1, 2026 | CNBC — Stock market news for July 1, 2026 (Dow/S&P/Nasdaq closes) | PR Newswire — June 2026 ISM Manufacturing PMI 53.3% | CNBC — Oil prices fall on US-Iran talks in Qatar | TradingEconomics — Brent Crude

Report: 2026-07-02 (Thursday) — Pre-Market Overnight Research

DAILY SUMMARY
NAV standings (July 1 close, latest confirmed MTM, post the 6:03pm monthly rebalance): residual_roa_6535 $108,637.27 (+8.64% since 5/1, +5.16pp vs SPY) leads all sleeves, ahead of mom_roa_6535 $107,971.26 (+7.97%, +4.49pp vs SPY), mom_v2 $105,037.61 (+5.04%, +1.56pp), mom_v1 $104,508.33 (+4.51%, +1.03pp). spy_benchmark $103,484.35 (+3.48%) and sector_top4_full $102,391.21 (+2.39%, -1.09pp, trailing SPY) round out the six original sleeves. The 11-sleeve 07-01 cohort (6 LLM-experiment + 4 systematic Alpaca duplicates + a second SPY benchmark) completed its first-ever trades yesterday evening — see Section 4.
Pre-market moves: No live intraday/premarket per-ticker feed is available to this pipeline and the market has not opened. Verifiable signals only: S&P 500 futures -0.08%, Nasdaq-100 futures -0.3%, Dow futures -35 pts, all easing off earlier gains ahead of this morning's jobs report. BE (held $99,900 by the LLM stock-arm control sleeve) is reported up ~8% pre-market on an expanded Brookfield financing commitment ($5B→$25B for AI-datacenter fuel-cell buildout) — a meaningful new data point against the veto rationale that killed BE for both LLM treatment sleeves. MU traded in a volatile, source-conflicted range Wednesday (close estimates from -6.92% to roughly flat, pre-market Thursday quoted near $1,085 vs. a $1,154 prior-close reference) — treat with caution, sources disagree materially; see Section 2.
Macro catalysts: June nonfarm payrolls release TODAY at 8:30am ET (consensus ~100-115K, unemployment expected to hold at 4.3%) — moved up from the usual first-Friday slot because of the July 4 holiday calendar. Wednesday's ADP private-payrolls miss (+98K vs. ~110K expected) is the leading indicator raising some downside risk to the print. Markets close early today (1:00pm ET stocks) and are closed tomorrow, Friday July 3, for Independence Day. WTI crude fell to $67.74 (-1.23%), lowest since late February, on rising Strait of Hormuz shipping volumes and "positive progress" reported by Qatar in indirect US-Iran talks — though the next round is delayed by funeral processions for Iran's former supreme leader scheduled July 4-9. Fed Chair Kevin Warsh continues at the ECB's Sintra forum.
LLM overlay status: Both the cash-veto and cascade LLM-experiment cohorts executed their first live trades at yesterday's 6:03pm rebalance (confirmed via paper_positions, cross-checked against Project Record Appendix AS). Stock side: control holds BE, cash-veto treatment sits in 100% cash (BE vetoed), cascade treatment holds WDC (BE→SLGL vetoed, WDC bought at $598.67, stop $560). Sector side: control holds XLE/XLK/XLB/XLI, cash-veto treatment holds XLK/XLI/XLB + 25% cash (XLE vetoed), cascade holds XLK/XLI/XLB/XLV (XLE→XLY vetoed, XLV added). Day 1 results are notable: BE (the vetoed name) is reportedly rallying ~8% pre-market, while WDC (the cascade's pick) fell -7.03% on its first trading day. Both are single data points, not a trend.
Critical outstanding items: (1) NFP print at 8:30am ET could move the whole book, especially the semiconductor-heavy sleeves, before/at today's open; (2) MU DRAM antitrust suit remains an open legal overhang, still held in all four systematic sleeves; (3) SK Hynix's targeted $29B Nasdaq ADR listing (July 10) could reprice the memory complex; (4) POWL fell -7.38% on 7/1 with no clear catalyst found, alongside a $10M insider sale (6/26) and an EPS miss — worth a dedicated look; (5) RCUS (new mom_v1 holding) has a Gilead option-continuation payment lapsing July 14, ending broad option rights, plus a recently discontinued Phase 3 trial; (6) AFJK SPAC merger deadline is July 6, three days away; (7) ~97 stray root-level junk files have re-accumulated in the project directory per Project Record Appendix AS — the same failure class that broke the 7/1 rebalance batch script once already; a cleanup pass is recommended before the next rebalance cycle, out of scope for this report.
