Aggregate dollar exposure across the four core systematic sleeves + sector_top4_full ($502,761 notional): the four sector-ETF slots dominate (XLE/XLK/XLB/XLI at $24,975 each). Below that, the largest single-name exposures — each held in all four systematic sleeves at ~$6,993-$7,178 — are VICR, DOCN, VIAV, POWL, STX, WDC, ARWR, CIEN, MU, and TER. This is a smaller per-name exposure than the pre-rebalance figures cited in the 6/30-7/1 reports (e.g., MU was ~$14,880 before the rebalance); the 7/1 monthly rebalance reset each sleeve to fresh equal-weight positions, roughly halving single-name dollar concentration versus the prior (partially stale) position set.

2. Overnight / Pre-Market Moves

No live intraday or pre-market quote feed is available to this pipeline, and the market has not opened for July 2. Percentage-change claims below are limited to what is explicitly sourced.

  • Index futures: S&P 500 futures -0.08%, Nasdaq-100 futures -0.3%, Dow futures pared early gains to -35 points (<-0.1%) — all easing lower Thursday morning ahead of the 8:30am ET jobs report. Source: TheStreet — Stock Market Today, Dow Jones, S&P 500, Nasdaq Updates, July 2, 2026 | Bloomberg — US Stock Futures Today
  • BE (Bloom Energy) — up ~8% pre-market, per Benzinga/TheStreet coverage tied to an expanded Brookfield (BAM) financing commitment, raised from $5B to $25B, to accelerate Bloom's fuel-cell buildout for AI data centers. This is the single most portfolio-relevant pre-market mover: BE is the sole, ~$99,900 holding of mom_roa_top1_paper (the LLM stock-arm control), and was vetoed by both LLM treatment sleeves on valuation grounds (~128x fwd EPS, momentum cooling). Source: Benzinga — Bloom Energy Stock Is Volatile: What's Driving The Action? | TIKR — Bloom Energy Stock Could Be Worth $920 After the Oracle Deal Expansion
  • MU — data conflict, flagged rather than resolved. Wednesday's close is reported inconsistently across sources: TradingKey's close-of-day recap puts the day at -6.92%; Motley Fool/other coverage cites an intraday low as much as -9.5% to -9.6%; one Barchart piece claims a sharp intraday reversal to close roughly +1% higher after touching the ~$1,000 psychological level. A pre-market snapshot (source: MarketChameleon) shows MU near $1,085, which would be roughly -6% versus a stated prior close of $1,154 — but that reference close does not cleanly reconcile with the other closing-price claims above. Treat all MU price figures this morning as directionally negative/volatile but not precisely known pending the open. Source: TradingKey — Micron (MU) Moved Down 6.92% on Jul 1 | The Motley Fool — Why Micron Stock Is Plummeting Today | Barchart — Why 1 Veteran Analyst Doubled Her Micron Stock Price Target | MarketChameleon — MU Premarket VWAP
  • WDC — fell -7.03% on July 1, its second straight down day since being logged as the LLM cascade's pick (originally down -6.32% the day the decision was logged, 6/30). No pre-market July 2 print was found. TradingKey frames the move as broad, macro-driven sector rotation/profit-taking rather than a WDC-specific issue — no news found challenging the underlying AI-HDD supercycle thesis. Source: TradingKey — Western Digital (WDC) Moved Down 7.03% on Jul 1
  • Other pre-market movers (context, non-holdings): Nike (NKE) down ~2% pre-market; Kroger (KR) down 2.8% pre-market after announcing it will acquire Giant Eagle for $1.65B (neither is a portfolio holding). Source: Schwab — Stock Market Update
  • Oil: WTI crude $67.74/bbl, -1.23% day, the lowest level since late February — attributed to Strait of Hormuz shipping flows exceeding 10 million bbl/day (per a US official) and continued optimism from the Doha US-Iran talks. Relevant to residual_roa_6535's energy cluster and the newly-added XOM/VLO positions. Source: TradingEconomics — Crude Oil
  • AMAT — data conflict. One source (TradingKey) puts the July 1 move at -5.32%; another reference in the same research pass cited -9.97% for what may be the same session — sources disagree on magnitude. Both agree AMAT was swept into the broad chip-equipment selloff described in Section 3. AMAT was newly added to residual_roa_6535 on 7/1. Source: TradingKey — Applied Materials (AMAT) Moved Down 5.32% on Jul 1

3. Catalyst and Macro Review

A. June nonfarm payrolls — TODAY, 8:30am ET, before the open. Consensus centers around 100-115K jobs added (FactSet median 100K; range of estimates 70K-150K), with the unemployment rate expected to hold at 4.3%. This report was moved to Thursday this week (rather than the usual first-Friday slot) because of the July 4 holiday calendar. Wednesday's ADP private-payrolls report came in at +98,000, below the ~110K consensus and down from May's 122,000 — nearly half the ADP gain (48K) came from education/health services, with wage growth flat at 4.4% YoY for job-stayers. This is the single largest scheduled catalyst for today's session and will likely set the tone for the semiconductor-heavy sleeves at the open. Source: BLS — Employment Situation Release Schedule | FactSet — June Payrolls Preview | CNBC — Private Payrolls Rose by 98,000 in June, Less Than Expected, ADP Reports

B. Holiday-shortened trading week. The market closes early today (1:00pm ET for equities, 2:00pm ET for bonds) and is fully closed tomorrow, Friday July 3, for Independence Day (observed). Liquidity is typically thinner into and around this kind of holiday close — worth noting as a backdrop for today's NFP reaction and any rebalance-adjacent order flow. Source: Yahoo Finance — Stock Market Closed for the Fourth of July

C. Oil / Iran talks — de-escalation continues but stalls into the holiday. Qatar reported "positive progress" on issues tied to the June 17 ceasefire memorandum of understanding after envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner concluded a two-day Doha mission Wednesday; VP Vance said talks were "going well," with nuclear-issue discussions expected to start soon. Remaining open disputes center on Lebanon and the Strait of Hormuz, not yet the nuclear file itself. The next round of talks is delayed by funeral processions for Iran's former supreme leader, scheduled July 4-9 — meaning no near-term resolution catalyst is likely this week. WTI at $67.74 is a continuing headwind for residual_roa_6535's ~15-16% energy weight (now including newly-added XOM and VLO) even as the de-escalation itself is broadly risk-on for equities generally. Source: Al Jazeera — Iran to Open Communication Channel on MOU with US After Talks in Qatar | Washington Times — US-Iran Conclude Two Days of Indirect Talks in Qatar, Major Questions Left | TradingEconomics — Crude Oil

D. DRAM antitrust suit — still developing, no case-specifics surfaced overnight. The class action filed June 25-26 against Micron/Samsung/SK Hynix (N.D. Cal., alleging coordinated DDR3/DDR4 supply restriction inflating prices ~500-700% over four years) remains an open legal overhang. No new court filings or company statements dated after 6/30 were found this pass. Notably, analyst sentiment on MU has stayed strongly bullish through the legal news — Cantor Fitzgerald raised its PT to $2,000 from $1,500 (naming MU a top semis pick), Deutsche Bank reiterated Buy at a $1,500 PT, and Susquehanna moved to $1,750, all citing HBM supply constraints; 41-analyst consensus is Strong Buy with an average PT of $1,365.28. This is a real tension worth tracking: the stock is falling on legal/competitive-anxiety headlines while sell-side estimates keep rising on fundamentals. Source: Tom's Hardware — Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron Sued Over Alleged DRAM Price Fixing | Watcher.Guru — Micron Stock Jumps as Analysts Raise Targets Despite Legal Risks | Barchart — Micron and Other Memory Makers Are Being Sued Over Price Fixing Claims

E. SK Hynix's $29.4B Nasdaq ADR listing — targeted July 10, eight days out. SK Hynix's board resolved 6/24 to list up to 17.79M new ADR shares on Nasdaq under ticker SKHY, which would be the largest ADR offering on record (surpassing Alibaba's 2014 $21.8B debut); subscription/payment is tentatively set for 7/14, new-share listing 7/29, trading debut around 7/10 (dates subject to SEC approval). SK Hynix holds ~60% of the HBM market and is up ~280% YTD; this introduces a large new supply of US-listed memory-sector equity right as the DRAM antitrust story is unfolding, likely to keep contributing to competitive/dilution anxiety across MU and related names (AEHR, TER) into next week. Source: CNBC — South Korea's Biggest Chipmaker SK Hynix Plans to Raise $29 Billion via Nasdaq Listing | The Elec — SK Hynix Nears Nasdaq ADR Listing, Potentially as Early as July

F. Applied Materials M&A. Applied Materials (newly held in residual_roa_6535 since 7/1) agreed to acquire ASMPT Limited's NEXX business (large-area advanced-packaging deposition equipment) to broaden its panel-level advanced-packaging portfolio for AI accelerators — a positive strategic development that predates and is separate from the 7/1 sector-wide chip-equipment selloff. Source: Barchart — Applied Materials Hits New High, Is AMAT Stock Poised for Continued Gains in 2026?

G. Exxon Mobil corporate redomiciliation completed July 1. Exxon moved its corporate domicile from New Jersey to Texas, effective 7/1/2026; the publicly traded parent is now ExxonMobil Holdings Corporation (its first name change since the Mobil merger 25+ years ago). Shares exchanged 1-for-1 with no shareholder action required and continue trading as XOM on NYSE. Separately, Exxon won a US Supreme Court ruling reviving its claims over Cuban assets. Note: sourced closing-price figures for XOM conflicted materially between outlets (one citing ~$136, another referencing a "$70 area" test) — likely an artifact of different feeds handling the share-exchange mechanics; do not treat either figure as reliable without a fresh quote. XOM was newly added to residual_roa_6535 on 7/1. Source: ExxonMobil IR — ExxonMobil Announces Planned Effective Date for Move to Texas | GuruFocus — Exxon Mobil Corporation (XOM) Completes Redomiciliation Merger

H. Healthcare sector tariff overhang (relevant to XLV, newly held by the sector cascade sleeve). A presidential executive order signed April 2, 2026 imposes a 100% tariff on imported patented pharmaceuticals, taking effect July 31, 2026 for large manufacturers and September 29, 2026 for smaller ones. Generics, biosimilars, and orphan drugs are exempt; roughly 13 companies have signed company-specific tariff agreements, and the White House claims the policy has already triggered ~$400B in new US onshoring investment commitments. XLV has been a broad underperformer in 2026 (down ~7% YTD as of late April vs. SPY +5% per one source) despite this — a live regulatory deadline approaching inside the next month, directly relevant to the sector cascade sleeve's newest (and only non-control-overlapping) holding. Source: Freshfields — Trump's Pharma Tariff Ultimatum | White House — Adjusting Imports of Pharmaceuticals and Pharmaceutical Ingredients into the United States

4. LLM Overlay Status

A. Both experiment generations executed their first-ever live trades at the July 1, 6:03pm rebalance. This is confirmed directly against paper_positions (not inferred from logged decisions, as in yesterday's pre-market report) and cross-checked against Project Record Appendix AS, which documents the rebalance completing cleanly (18:19-18:34) after a same-day cmd.exe batch-parsing bug was found and fixed.

Stock side — Day 1 results:
| Sleeve | Holding | Entry | Day-1-to-date signal |
|---|---|---|---|
| mom_roa_top1_paper (control) | BE | $289.64 (7/1) | Reported +~8% pre-market July 2 on expanded Brookfield financing news |
| llm_overlay_mom_roa_top1_paper (cash-veto treatment) | 100% cash | — | BE vetoed (score 4: ~128x fwd EPS, momentum cooling -20.5% off 52wk high) |
| llm_cascade_top1_paper (cascade treatment) | WDC | $598.67 (7/1), stop $560 | Fell -7.03% on its first trading day (7/1), second straight down session for the name |

Sector side — Day 1 results:
| Sleeve | Holdings | Signal |
|---|---|---|
| sector_top4_paper (control) | XLE/XLK/XLB/XLI | Pure top-4 momentum, unchanged design |
| llm_overlay_sector_top4_paper (cash-veto treatment) | XLK/XLI/XLB + 25% cash | XLE vetoed (fading momentum + soft oil) |
| llm_cascade_sector4_paper (cascade treatment) | XLK/XLI/XLB/XLV | Walked past XLE→XLY vetoes to XLV (defensive healthcare rotation); XLV now carries the approaching pharma-tariff deadline (Section 3H) as a new risk this sleeve alone is exposed to |

B. The BE pre-market pop is the first real tension point for the stock-arm experiment. If BE's ~8% pre-market move holds into the open, the control sleeve (which the LLM vetoed) will have outperformed both LLM treatments on day one — the opposite of the pattern the overlay experiment is designed to demonstrate. This mirrors the same dynamic flagged in the May 29 BE veto (BE ran from ~$290 to briefly $351 after being vetoed, before round-tripping back down). One data point; the kill switch (12 months / ≥30 picks) remains the standard for any real conclusion, but it's worth flagging explicitly since it's now happened with BE twice.

C. WDC's continued decline is the cascade sleeve's open question. The cascade's WDC pick has now fallen on both the day it was decided (6/30, -6.32%) and the day it was executed (7/1, -7.03%) — a cumulative headwind before the position has had a single up day. No news found challenging the underlying AI-HDD supercycle thesis (Section 2); this reads as broad sector rotation rather than a WDC-specific problem, but the invalidation level ($560) is worth monitoring given two consecutive down sessions.

Kill switch reminder: unchanged — 12 months or ≥30 picks, drop the overlay if scores don't predict returns or treatment doesn't beat control. One trading day of data exists for the synchronized-inception cohort; no conclusion is possible yet.

6. Strategic Insights

A. The 7/1 monthly rebalance mechanically cut single-name concentration roughly in half, which is itself a useful natural experiment. Prior reports flagged MU at ~$14,880 (2.9-3.7% weight) across the four systematic sleeves; post-rebalance, the same position sizes to ~$6,993 total. This wasn't a deliberate risk-management change — it's an artifact of the monthly rebalance resetting each sleeve to fresh equal-weight positions off a lower starting NAV after the 7/1 drawdown — but it means today's version of the DRAM-lawsuit/SK-Hynix-ADR risk is mechanically smaller in dollar terms than it was 48 hours ago, even though the underlying legal and competitive story hasn't changed. Worth remembering when comparing exposure figures across reports: the number moves with rebalance timing, not just with news.

B. Day 1 of the resynchronized LLM overlay experiment is producing exactly the kind of early tension the design should generate — in both directions. BE (vetoed by both LLM treatments) is reportedly up ~8% pre-market on a large financing catalyst that arguably validates the underlying "accelerating fundamentals" half of the veto rationale rather than the "overvalued" half. WDC (the cascade's own pick) is down in two consecutive sessions. Neither is a verdict — one data point each, and the whole point of the 12-month/30-pick kill switch is to not overreact to day one — but if the pattern holds even a few more days, the qualitative story ("cash-veto sleeve missed a real move; cascade sleeve entered a stock still working through profit-taking") would be worth writing up explicitly rather than just noting in passing.

C. No new evidence today changes the standing four-sleeve edge-over-SPY thesis, and the sample continues to grow through a real stress period. residual_roa_6535 (+5.16pp), mom_roa_6535 (+4.49pp), mom_v2 (+1.56pp), and mom_v1 (+1.03pp) all still lead SPY since inception, having now weathered the June 5 Broadcom selloff, the June 26-29 chip-crash regime, and the July 1 DRAM/export-curb rout without the edge disappearing (though it has compressed meaningfully from the June 30 peak). The open question flagged in the last two reports — whether the edge survives a sustained semiconductor correction rather than a single bad session — remains open; today's macro slate (NFP, holiday-thinned liquidity, an unresolved DRAM legal overhang, and an imminent SK Hynix supply event) is a reasonable candidate for testing that question further over the next 1-2 weeks.

D. The newly-diversified residual_roa_6535 additions (XOM, VLO, AAPL, AMAT, NUE, COCO) are a live test of whether its dual-signal construction continues to earn its lower-drawdown reputation. Adding two energy majors into a period of falling oil (Section 3C) and a chip-equipment name into a period of chip-sector volatility (AMAT, Section 2) somewhat cuts against the sleeve's historical pattern of avoiding the portfolio's most crowded trades — worth watching whether residual's July cohort still shows the "roughly half the single-day drawdown" behavior it demonstrated on 7/1 (-3.77% vs. -5.59% to -5.64% for the pure-momentum sleeves) if another chip-specific shock hits this month.

Report generated: 2026-07-02 (pre-market, before the 8:30am ET NFP release and before today's open) | Coverage: July 1 close NAVs and positions from var/trades.db (paper_nav, paper_positions, llm_overlay_log, sector_overlay_log), cross-referenced against docs/Project Record — Full Chronological History.md Appendix AS for rebalance-execution confirmation; overnight/pre-market/macro context via web research below | Sources: TheStreet — Stock Market Today, July 2, 2026 | Bloomberg — US Stock Futures Today | Schwab — Stock Market Update | Benzinga — Bloom Energy Stock Is Volatile | TIKR — Bloom Energy Stock Could Be Worth $920 | TradingKey — MU Moved Down 6.92% Jul 1 | The Motley Fool — Why Micron Stock Is Plummeting | Barchart — Micron PT Doubled | MarketChameleon — MU Premarket VWAP | TradingKey — WDC Moved Down 7.03% Jul 1 | TradingEconomics — Crude Oil | TradingKey — AMAT Moved Down 5.32% Jul 1 | BLS — Employment Situation Schedule | FactSet — June Payrolls Preview | CNBC — ADP Private Payrolls Miss | Yahoo Finance — Market Closed July 4 | Al Jazeera — Iran-US Talks Qatar | Washington Times — US-Iran Talks Conclude | Tom's Hardware — DRAM Lawsuit | Watcher.Guru — Micron Analysts Raise Targets | Barchart — Micron Sued, Headline Risk | CNBC — SK Hynix $29B Nasdaq Listing | The Elec — SK Hynix Nasdaq ADR Listing Timing | Barchart — Applied Materials Hits New High | ExxonMobil IR — Redomiciliation Effective Date | GuruFocus — Exxon Redomiciliation Completes | Freshfields — Pharma Tariff Ultimatum | White House — Pharmaceutical Import Tariff EO | TipRanks — POWL Insider Trading | SEC — TNGX 8-K Pancreatic Cancer Data | StockTitan — RCUS 10-Q | BusinessWire — Arcus/BMS Kidney Cancer Collaboration | Arrowhead IR — EASL Data

Report: 2026-07-02 (Thursday) — Post-Market Close Analysis

DAILY SUMMARY

NAV since inception — ranked best-to-worst (July 2, 2026 close):
| Rank | Sleeve | NAV | Incep Return | Day $ | Day % | vs SPY |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | residual_roa_6535 | $105,621.14 | +5.62% | -$3,016 | -2.78% | +2.27pp |
| 2 | spy_benchmark | $103,348.37 | +3.35% | -$136 | -0.13% | — |
| 3 | sector_top4_full | $102,350.07 | +2.35% | -$41 | -0.04% | -1.00pp |
| 4 | mom_roa_6535 | $100,461.75 | +0.46% | -$7,510 | -6.96% | -2.89pp |
| 5 | mom_v1 | $98,437.50 | -1.56% | -$6,071 | -5.81% | -4.91pp |
| 6 | mom_v2 | $97,954.57 | -2.05% | -$7,083 | -6.74% | -5.39pp |
| — | 07-01/07-06 cohort (11 sleeves) | $100,000 ea. | 0.00% | $0 | 0.00% | idle — reset today, redeploys 07-06 |

Day moves: S&P 500/SPY roughly flat (SPY -0.13% to $744.78; broad-index sources cite S&P between -0.01% and flat) | Nasdaq Composite -1.3% to -1.6% (sources vary) on a two-day semiconductor-equipment rout | Dow Jones +1.14% to a record close of 52,900.07 on rotation into non-tech. This was a rotation day, not a risk-off day: XLV +2.63%, XLU +2.21%, XLP +2.03%, XLB +1.94%, XLF +1.53% all gained while XLK -2.71% was the only sector loser of size. The rotation-away-from-tech hit the portfolio's momentum sleeves hard because they are 51-59% Technology-weighted — mom_roa_6535 -6.96%, mom_v2 -6.74%, mom_v1 -5.81%, vs. residual_roa_6535 (28% tech) -2.78% and the fully-diversified sector_top4_full/spy_benchmark essentially flat.

Key movers with reason: A cluster of semiconductor-equipment/analog names fell 10-20% in the DB (VICR -19.21%, AIP/Arteris -19.97%, UCTT -17.84%, MOVE -17.52% [ticker identity unconfirmed, see Section 3C], MXL -17.15%, AEHR -17.14%, FORM -16.00%, BELFB -16.07%, VPG -15.57%, TER -13.63%, VIAV -11.49%, STX -10.38%) on a sector-wide AI-capex-fear selloff — Meta's spare-AI-capacity-leasing news plus a Citi capex-ROI warning triggered a Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) drop of ~6.4% today (~12% over two sessions, worst since June 5), following Broadcom's AI-revenue-guidance miss and a BofA "bubble risk" call on 6/30-7/1. External sources report smaller (7-14%) moves for several of these names than our DB shows — see Section 3A note. No earnings misses or negative company-specific news found for any of them; several (VICR, UCTT, MXL, AIP, STX) carry idiosyncratic tailwinds too (Russell index-reshuffle removals, profit-taking after 90-125%+ run-ups). Gainers: EACO +17.44% (record-quarter earnings, EPS +43.1% YoY, reported 7/1), AAPL +4.84% (5-new-iPhone-model / expanded foldable production reports), plus a cluster of biotechs (RCUS +4.88%, ARWR +4.41%, DNTH +4.58%, PVLA +6.15%) riding a sector-wide biotech rally (XBI at a 5-year high) and tanker names (INSW +5.94%, TEN +5.65%) on VLCC rate strength.

Macro event: June nonfarm payrolls +57,000 (well below the ~100-115K consensus), unemployment 4.2% (participation rate fell to 61.5%, lowest since March 2021), wage growth +3.5% YoY. The soft print eased Fed-tightening fears and helped drive today's rotation into defensives/Dow, but did not offset the chip-sector-specific selloff. WTI fell ~2.1% to ~$67-67.75/bbl (lowest since late February) on easing Strait-of-Hormuz risk premium / constructive US-Iran talks — a tailwind for residual_roa_6535's energy names on net, though the tanker-rate story (VLCC rates +3x YTD) offset the lower-crude read for INSW/TEN specifically. Markets closed Friday July 3 for Independence Day (July 4 falls on Saturday); reopen Monday July 6.

LLM overlay standings: No activity today — all 11 sleeves in the 07-01-inception cohort (6 LLM-experiment sleeves + 4 systematic duplicates + spy_0701) were wiped to a fresh $100,000/0-positions and their inception moved to 2026-07-06 (Appendix AT, logged today 2026-07-02) after the 7/1 rebalance was found to be partial/botched and an Alpaca fractional-order gap caused ~43-48 of ~50 mirror orders per account to silently fail. The alpaca_sync non-fractionable bug is now fixed (whole-share fallback), and the scheduled LLM-rebalance cron was disabled through the market holiday to avoid a premature redeploy. Nothing to compare treatment vs. control on until the 07-06 redeploy.

Critical outstanding items: (1) The 07-06 coordinated redeploy (Evan resets the 3 Alpaca PAPER accounts to $100k first, then a targeted rebalance of only the 11 cohort sleeves, then alpaca_sync --all --execute) is the top open item — see Appendix AT for the full runbook; (2) today's crash flipped mom_roa_6535/mom_v1/mom_v2 from beating SPY since inception to trailing it (see Strategic Insights) — the open question from the last two reports (does the momentum edge survive a sustained semiconductor correction) now has real evidence against it, worth close tracking into next week; (3) MOVE ticker identity is unconfirmed — DB tags it Technology but the only live match found was Movano Inc. (wearables, unrelated sector, pending delisting notice) — worth checking the data vendor's ticker mapping; (4) DRAM antitrust suit and SK Hynix's July 10 Nasdaq listing remain live, ongoing overhangs on the MU position common to all four systematic sleeves; (5) markets closed tomorrow (7/3) for the holiday — next trading session and next data pull is Monday 7/6.

1. Market Summary — July 2, 2026

A. Index Closes

Index Close / Change Notable
S&P 500 / SPY SPY $744.78, -0.13% (DB price_cache) Broad-index sources describe the S&P as "roughly flat," with figures ranging -0.01% to essentially unchanged; 354 of 500 constituents advanced (~70% breadth)
Nasdaq Composite -1.3% to -1.6% (source estimates vary) Dragged by the two-day semiconductor-equipment rout; Meta and Tesla both cited as down 3.8-6.4% in the megacap-tech complex
Dow Jones 52,900.07, +1.14% (record close) 24 of 30 components advanced; non-tech rotation (healthcare, staples, utilities) offset the chip drag

Note on precision: multiple sources (TheStreet, 24/7 Wall St, search-aggregated CNBC data) gave slightly different exact decimal closes for the S&P and Nasdaq — the directional read (S&P flat, Nasdaq down on chips, Dow up on rotation) is well-corroborated across sources, but exact ticks should be treated as approximate pending a single-feed check. Source: 24/7 Wall St — Stock Market Live July 2, 2026 | TheStreet — Stock Market Today, July 2, 2026

B. Primary Market Drivers

1. A Meta AI-capacity-leasing headline plus a Citi capex-ROI warning triggered the day's chip-equipment selloff. On July 1-2, Meta disclosed plans to sell/lease out spare AI computing capacity — read by the market as evidence hyperscalers over-built GPU/data-center capacity — while a Citi analyst separately questioned whether cloud platforms can sustain AI infrastructure capex without clearer ROI. This built directly on Broadcom's AI-chip revenue guidance miss ($16B Q3 guide vs. ~$17.2B street estimate, reported ~6/30-7/1) and a Bank of America "bubble risk" note on Intel/AMD/TSMC published 7/1. Source: Bloomberg — Chip Stocks Off to Rough Start in Third Quarter With 2-Day Skid | 247wallst — Intel Drops 7%, AMD Slides 5%, TSM Falls 6% as BofA Flags Bubble Risk | StockStory/FinancialContent — FormFactor and Allegro Microsystems Shares Plummet

2. The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) fell ~6.4% today, ~12% over two sessions — the worst two-day drop since June 5. Big semicap/memory names most exposed to WFE (wafer-fab-equipment)/AI-capex fears got hit hardest — KLA -11.5% to -12%, Lam Research -7.3% to -9.7%, Applied Materials -6.1% to -10%, Micron/SanDisk >-10% — which read through into the smaller equipment/component suppliers our sleeves hold. The selloff spread overnight to Asia: Samsung -9.06%, SK Hynix -14.57% on the Kospi open. Source: Bloomberg — Chip Stocks Off to Rough Start in Third Quarter | CNBC — Samsung, SK Hynix Shares Slide as Kospi Tech Selloff Follows Nasdaq

3. June nonfarm payrolls badly missed consensus: +57,000 vs. ~100-115K expected. Unemployment rate 4.2% (the improvement driven by a falling participation rate, 61.5%, lowest since March 2021, rather than hiring strength). Wage growth +3.5% YoY (up from 3.4%). April/May were revised down a combined 74,000. The soft print reduced perceived pressure for further Fed tightening, which helped lift rate-sensitive/defensive sectors and the Dow, but did not rescue the Nasdaq from the chip-sector drag. Source: CNBC — June 2026 Jobs Report | IndexBox — US Job Growth Slows More Than Expected in June 2026

4. Idiosyncratic amplifiers on several of the worst-hit small/mid-cap semi names. Multiple names in our sleeves (VICR, UCTT, MXL, AIP/Arteris) were also flagged in coverage for Russell index-reshuffle removals (dropped from Russell 3000/Small Cap Value benchmarks in late June) and/or profit-taking after outsized 90-125%+ run-ups over the prior year — VICR's move was linked to digesting a Russell index inclusion plus insider selling after a +125% 90-day run; UCTT to a Russell 3000 Value benchmark drop; AIP (Arteris) explicitly framed by TipRanks as "profit-taking...after record run," not bad news; STX's pullback tied to "broad sector profit-taking, high valuation concerns, natural consolidation" after a ~590% YTD/1-yr AI-storage rally. Source: Simply Wall St — Vicor Could Be 14% Undervalued Following Russell Index Inclusion | MarketScreener — Ultra Clean Holdings Dropped from Russell 3000 Value Benchmark | TipRanks — Profit-Taking Slams Arteris Stock After Record Run | TradingKey — STX Market Movers, July 1, 2026

5. No fundamental negative catalyst found for Teradyne, Viavi, Vishay Precision, or Bel Fuse. Multiple sources explicitly frame Teradyne's decline as pure sector-wide selling, grouped with AMAT/LRCX/KLAC/MKSI; Viavi's move was explicitly labeled "no clear catalyst identified" against otherwise-positive underlying business commentary (data-center/A&D demand beat); VPG and BELFB had no news found at all beyond routine items (a VPG Q1 beat, a BELFB dividend ex-date) unrelated to a same-day selloff. This is consistent with sector-wide beta contagion rather than idiosyncratic bad news across most of the list. Source: Alphastreet — Teradyne Drops 8.6% Amid Sector-Wide Selling

6. Oil fell to a multi-month low on easing Iran risk premium. WTI fell ~2.1% to roughly $67-67.75/bbl (lowest since late February) as Strait of Hormuz throughput expanded on constructive US-Iran negotiations and rising Iranian/Russian export volumes. Despite falling crude, tanker names INSW (+5.94%) and TEN (+5.65%) both rose — likely explained by VLCC (very-large-crude-carrier) daily rates reportedly tripling since the start of 2026 (to ~$170,000/day) on India's demand for Mideast crude plus expedited shipping amid the same US-Iran tension backdrop; no company-specific news was found for either ticker, so this is treated as a sector-flow read rather than a confirmed causal link, and the falling-crude/rising-tanker-equity combination is a genuinely mixed signal worth flagging rather than resolving. Source: Investing.com — Oil Tanker Rates Surge to 6-Year High

7. Markets closed Friday, July 3, 2026 for Independence Day. NYSE, Nasdaq, NYSE Arca/American, and CBOE are closed the full day (July 4 falls on Saturday); markets reopen Monday, July 6. Source: Nasdaq Trader — Holiday Calendar

Notes:
- The 6 continuously-tracked sleeves (4 systematic momentum + sector_top4_full + spy_benchmark, all inception 2026-05-01) had their normal 5:15pm MTM run today; the drops above are real, driven by the semiconductor-equipment rotation in Section 1.
- The 11-sleeve "07-01 cohort" all show exactly $100,000 cash / 0 positions — this is not a data gap, it is a deliberate reset. Per Appendix AT of the project record (logged today, 2026-07-02), the 7/1 rebalance for this cohort was found to be partial (the batch script appears to have been interrupted after only 2 of ~10 paper_rebalance sections completed — see var/rebalance_run_0701.log, which cuts off mid-preload for mom_v2_paper's section and never reaches the 07-01 cohort at all) and the Alpaca mirror had a fractional-order bug: alpaca_sync submitted fractional quantities that Alpaca rejects (422) on non-fractionable assets, silently skipped via a per-order except AlpacaError — on the 7/1 mirror this left residual_0701 at 43/50 positions filled and mom_roa_0701 at 48/50, i.e. ~14%/4% of those two Alpaca accounts sat in uninvested cash. Both issues are now fixed (whole-share fallback added to alpaca_sync and a new paper_rebalance --broker-realistic flag), and all 11 sleeves were wiped to a clean $100,000/0-positions with inception moved to 2026-07-06 (the next trading day after tomorrow's July 4th holiday closure). Redeployment is coordinated and pending — see Critical Outstanding Items.

3. Key Position Analysis

A. Today's biggest movers (across the 4 real systematic sleeves)

Losers — all semiconductor-equipment/analog names, all attributed to sector-wide AI-capex-fear selling (Section 1) rather than company-specific bad news:

Precision note: our price_cache-derived day-% figures (internally consistent across the trailing week, no split-pattern anomaly found on inspection) run larger in magnitude than some external aggregator sources for a few names (e.g., VICR -19.21% here vs. -7.79% per Simply Wall St). This could reflect close-vs-intraday timing differences, rounding in secondary aggregator sites, or a genuine data-quality issue in our feed — given this project's history of price-cache data bugs (Friday-spike corruption, corporate-action handling, history-gap backfills), it is flagged here rather than asserted as correct; worth a spot-check against a primary feed if these levels persist.

Gainers:

Ticker Day % Held in Note

ARWR +4.41% mom_v1, mom_v2, mom_roa_6535, residual_roa_6535 No company-specific 7/1-7/2 catalyst found; sector-wide biotech tailwind

Biotech sector context: XBI (biotech ETF) is confirmed at a 5-year high, up 13%+ in June alone on ~$1.3B net inflows, driven by an M&A revival ($106B across 201 deals YTD) and improved regulatory/policy sentiment — this explains PVLA/DNTH/ARWR (and contributes to RCUS) moving together absent individual company news for most of them. Source: STAT News — Biotech Sector Booms | BusinessWire — EACO Corporation Reports All Time Record Quarter | StockStory/FinancialContent — Why Apple (AAPL) Stock Is Trading Up Today | GuruFocus — Arcus Biosciences Unveils ARC-20 Study Findings

B. Positions near a stop or invalidation level

The four systematic momentum sleeves run no intra-month stop-loss mechanism (13 prior attempts tested and rejected — see sleeves_verdict.md), so today's double-digit single-name drawdowns trigger no automated action regardless of severity; positions are held until the next monthly rebalance.

The LLM overlay/cascade invalidation levels set on 7/1 (WDC $560, XLK $174, XLI $172, XLB $48, XLV $150) are moot for now — none of those positions ever executed into paper_positions before the 07-01 cohort was reset (Section 2), so there is nothing live to check against a stop today.

C. New developments

  • Appendix AT reset (today, 2026-07-02): the entire 11-sleeve 07-01 cohort was wiped and re-inceptioned for 2026-07-06 after the partial 7/1 rebalance and the Alpaca fractional-order bug (see Section 2 notes). This is the single biggest operational development of the day, unrelated to market moves.
  • EACO's all-time-record quarter (net sales +27.8% YoY, EPS +43.1% YoY, reported 7/1) is a genuine, sourced, company-specific catalyst behind residual_roa_6535's biggest single-day gainer.
  • AAPL's iPhone-lineup and foldable-production reports are a fresh, dated catalyst not present in prior reports.
  • RCUS's ARC-20/casdatifan Nature publication (7/1) adds to the existing BMS kidney-cancer collaboration thread tracked in prior reports.
  • MOVE ticker ambiguity: flagged above — worth a data-vendor check before citing this position's "-17.52%" as a real semiconductor move in future reports.
  • The Meta AI-capacity-leasing story and Citi capex-ROI skepticism are new, dated (7/1-7/2) macro/sector developments not present in yesterday's report, and appear to be the proximate trigger for today's session specifically (on top of the already-known Broadcom guidance miss and BofA bubble-risk call from 6/30-7/1).

4. LLM Overlay: Treatment vs. Control Update

No trading activity today. All six LLM-experiment sleeves (both cash-veto treatment/control pairs and both cascade treatment/control pairs) are part of the 11-sleeve reset described in Section 2 — wiped to $100,000/0 positions, inception moved to 2026-07-06. The scheduled monthy-llm-rebalance cron (which fires daily at 6:03pm local) was explicitly disabled today to stop it from auto-deploying the reset cohort prematurely between now and the 07-06 target date (it would also have empty-universe-bailed on tomorrow's market holiday regardless). llm_overlay_log and sector_overlay_log show no new entries dated 2026-07-02 — the last logged decisions remain the 7/1 stock-side (BE VETO / WDC BUY) and sector-side (XLK/XLI/XLB HOLD, XLE VETO, cascade adds XLV) calls from the prior report, which never executed and are now superseded by the reset.

Kill switch reminder: unchanged — 12 months or ≥30 picks, drop the overlay if scores don't predict returns or treatment doesn't beat control. The synchronized-inception design now effectively restarts a second time (07-01 → 07-06), so the "Day 1" clock for this experiment has not yet started for real; zero live trading days exist under either inception date so far.

Pending 07-06 runbook (from Appendix AT): (1) Evan resets the 3 Alpaca PAPER accounts to $100k via the dashboard (API cannot do this; Claude never touches account settings); (2) targeted deploy of only the 11 cohort sleeves — overlay_prep as-of 07-06, log the 07-06 LLM decisions, rebalance the 5 systematic 0701 sleeves with --broker-realistic, seed spy_0701 for 07-06, seed the 6 LLM sleeves, MTM all 11, then alpaca_sync --all --execute; (3) re-enable the LLM-rebalance cron for August's monthly run.

5. Week/Period Summary

Trailing 6 trading days (2026-06-25 close → 2026-07-02 close):

Sleeve Jun 25 NAV Jul 2 NAV 6-day change
spy_benchmark $101,894.12 $103,348.37 +1.43%
sector_top4_full $103,280.53 $102,350.07 -0.90%
residual_roa_6535 $108,226.48 $105,621.14 -2.41%
mom_v1 $105,390.38 $98,437.50 -6.60%
mom_v2 $105,786.48 $97,954.57 -7.40%
mom_roa_6535 $109,514.22 $100,461.75 -8.27%

The two-day (7/1-7/2) semiconductor-equipment rout erased the entire trailing week's gains for all four momentum sleeves and then some — mom_roa_6535 alone round-tripped from a 06-30 peak of $114,399 to today's $100,462, a -12.2% drawdown in three trading sessions. spy_benchmark is the only sleeve net positive over the week; sector_top4_full and residual_roa_6535 are both modestly negative but far less damaged than the pure-momentum sleeves, consistent with their materially lower Technology concentration (Section 6).

6. Strategic Insights

A. Today provides the clearest evidence yet against the "momentum edge survives a semiconductor correction" thesis flagged as an open question in the last two reports. As of yesterday's close, all four systematic sleeves still led SPY since inception. As of today, three of four have flipped to trailing SPY: mom_roa_6535 -2.89pp, mom_v1 -4.91pp, mom_v2 -5.39pp behind SPY since 2026-05-01 inception. Only residual_roa_6535 (28% Technology-weighted, vs. 51-59% for the other three) still leads, and by a much-reduced +2.27pp (down from +5.20pp yesterday). This is not a single bad session in isolation — it is the second severe semiconductor-specific drawdown in three trading days (7/1 and 7/2 both), following the June 26-29 chip-crash regime referenced in prior reports. The open question posed twice now — does the edge survive a sustained correction, not just one bad day — has moved from "untested" to "evidence against," though a definitive verdict still requires seeing whether momentum re-establishes its edge on the next rebalance or continues to erode.

B. The concentration-risk mechanism is now visible in the data twice in three sessions. mom_roa_6535's Technology weight actually increased slightly from 58.4% (7/1) to 59.4% (7/2, post-drawdown, since tech fell faster than the rest of the book) — the portfolio has no mechanism to reduce single-sector concentration mid-month, and a momentum factor mechanically buys into whatever has been winning, which for over a year has been semiconductors. residual_roa_6535's dual-signal construction again cut the damage roughly in half to a third versus the pure-momentum sleeves (-2.78% vs. -5.81% to -6.96%), extending the pattern from 7/1 (-3.77% vs. -5.59% to -5.64%) — two consecutive real-time confirmations of its lower-drawdown design intent.

C. The rotation was orderly, not panicked — which matters for interpreting the momentum sleeves' losses. Nine of eleven sector ETFs gained today (Section 1C), the Dow hit a record close, and breadth was reported as ~70% advancing on the S&P. This was capital moving out of one crowded trade (AI/semiconductor capex names) into others (healthcare, staples, utilities, materials), not a broad flight from risk. That distinction matters for the momentum sleeves specifically: their drawdown is a concentrated-bet outcome, not a market-wide one, meaning diversified or rotation-aware constructions (residual_roa_6535, sector_top4_full, spy_benchmark) were structurally insulated from it almost by definition rather than by luck.

D. The 07-01 cohort's reset (Appendix AT) removes an operational data-integrity risk before it compounded further — worth noting as a process win even though it delays the LLM-experiment timeline again. Catching a partially-executed rebalance and a silent Alpaca order-failure mode within 24-48 hours, rather than letting mismatched local-vs-broker state accumulate for weeks, is the kind of thing that would have been far more expensive to unwind later. The cost is a second inception-date reset (07-01 → 07-06) for the entire 11-sleeve experiment, meaning the "does the LLM overlay beat control" and "does cascade out-pick raw momentum" questions remain exactly where they were before 6/30 — zero live trading days accumulated. No conclusion is possible on that experiment yet; the open questions from prior reports (cash-veto vs. cascade divergence, whether LLM scores predict returns) simply have not started accumulating evidence.
